Skip to main content

Files

zeroclaw_channels/orchestrator/
mod.rs

1//! Channel subsystem for messaging platform integrations.
2//!
3//! This module provides the multi-channel messaging infrastructure that connects
4//! ZeroClaw to external platforms. Each channel implements the [`Channel`] trait
5//! defined in the `traits` submodule, which provides a uniform interface for
6//! sending messages, listening for incoming messages, health checking, and typing
7//! indicators.
8//!
9//! Channels are instantiated by [`start_channels`] based on the runtime configuration.
10//! The subsystem manages per-sender conversation history, concurrent message processing
11//! with configurable parallelism, and exponential-backoff reconnection for resilience.
12//!
13//! # Extension
14//!
15//! To add a new channel, implement [`Channel`] in a new top-level module in
16//! `zeroclaw-channels/src/`, declare it in `lib.rs` behind the appropriate feature
17//! gate, and wire it into [`start_channels`] here. See `AGENTS.md` §7.2 for the
18//! full change playbook.
19
20#[cfg(feature = "channel-acp-server")]
21pub mod acp_server;
22pub mod media_pipeline;
23#[cfg(feature = "channel-mqtt")]
24pub mod mqtt;

138type CronChannelRegistry = Arc<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>>>;
139
140/// Live channel registry consulted by `deliver_announcement` so cron sends reuse the
141/// authenticated channel instance (Matrix E2EE can't tolerate per-send session restore).
142/// Replaced wholesale by each `start_channels` call.
143static CRON_CHANNEL_REGISTRY: std::sync::RwLock<Option<CronChannelRegistry>> =
144    std::sync::RwLock::new(None);
145
146/// Observer wrapper that forwards tool-call events to a channel sender
147/// for real-time threaded notifications.
148struct ChannelNotifyObserver {
149    inner: Arc<dyn Observer>,
150    tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::UnboundedSender<String>,
151    tools_used: AtomicBool,
152}
153
154impl Observer for ChannelNotifyObserver {
155    fn record_event(&self, event: &ObserverEvent) {
156        if let ObserverEvent::ToolCallStart {
157            tool, arguments, ..
158        } = event
159        {
160            self.tools_used.store(true, Ordering::Relaxed);
161            let detail = match arguments {
162                Some(args) if !args.is_empty() => {
163                    if let Ok(v) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(args) {
164                        if let Some(cmd) = v.get("command").and_then(|c| c.as_str()) {
165                            format!(": `{}`", truncate_with_ellipsis(cmd, 200))
166                        } else if let Some(q) = v.get("query").and_then(|c| c.as_str()) {
167                            format!(": {}", truncate_with_ellipsis(q, 200))
168                        } else if let Some(p) = v.get("path").and_then(|c| c.as_str()) {
169                            format!(": {p}")
170                        } else if let Some(u) = v.get("url").and_then(|c| c.as_str()) {
171                            format!(": {u}")
172                        } else {
173                            let s = args.to_string();
174                            format!(": {}", truncate_with_ellipsis(&s, 120))
175                        }
176                    } else {
177                        let s = args.to_string();
178                        format!(": {}", truncate_with_ellipsis(&s, 120))
179                    }
180                }
181                _ => String::new(),
182            };
183            let _ = self.tx.send(format!("\u{1F527} `{tool}`{detail}"));
184        }
185        self.inner.record_event(event);
186    }
187    fn record_metric(&self, metric: &ObserverMetric) {
188        self.inner.record_metric(metric);
189    }
190    fn flush(&self) {
191        self.inner.flush();
192    }
193    fn name(&self) -> &str {
194        "channel-notify"
195    }
196    fn as_any(&self) -> &dyn std::any::Any {
197        self
198    }
199}
200
201/// Per-sender conversation history for channel messages.
202/// Bounded by `MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS` — oldest-accessed senders are evicted.
203type ConversationHistoryMap = Arc<Mutex<lru::LruCache<String, Vec<ChatMessage>>>>;
204/// Senders that requested `/new` and must force a fresh prompt on their next message.
205type PendingNewSessionSet = Arc<Mutex<HashSet<String>>>;
206/// Maximum conversation senders kept in memory (LRU eviction beyond this).
207const MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS: usize = 1000;
208/// Maximum history messages to keep per sender.
209const MAX_CHANNEL_HISTORY: usize = 50;
210/// Minimum user-message length (in chars) for auto-save to memory.
211/// Messages shorter than this (e.g. "ok", "thanks") are not stored,
212/// reducing noise in memory recall.
213const AUTOSAVE_MIN_MESSAGE_CHARS: usize = 20;
214const CURRENT_DATE_HEADING: &str = "## Current Date\n\n";
215const LEGACY_CURRENT_DATE_TIME_HEADING: &str = "## Current Date & Time\n\n";
216
217// System prompt functions live in `zeroclaw_runtime::agent::system_prompt`.
218#[allow(unused_imports)]
219pub use zeroclaw_runtime::agent::system_prompt::{
220    BOOTSTRAP_MAX_CHARS, build_system_prompt, build_system_prompt_with_mode,
221    build_system_prompt_with_mode_and_autonomy,
222};
223
224const DEFAULT_CHANNEL_INITIAL_BACKOFF_SECS: u64 = 2;
225const DEFAULT_CHANNEL_MAX_BACKOFF_SECS: u64 = 60;
226const MIN_CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 30;
227/// Default timeout for processing a single channel message (LLM + tools).
228/// Used as fallback when not configured in channels_config.message_timeout_secs.
229#[cfg(test)]
230const CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS: u64 = 300;
231/// Cap timeout scaling so large max_tool_iterations values do not create unbounded waits.
232const CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SCALE_CAP: u64 = 4;
233const CHANNEL_MIN_IN_FLIGHT_MESSAGES: usize = 8;
234const CHANNEL_MAX_IN_FLIGHT_MESSAGES: usize = 64;
235const CHANNEL_TYPING_REFRESH_INTERVAL_SECS: u64 = 4;
236const CHANNEL_HEALTH_HEARTBEAT_SECS: u64 = 30;
237const MODEL_CACHE_FILE: &str = "models_cache.json";
238const MODEL_CACHE_PREVIEW_LIMIT: usize = 10;
239const MEMORY_CONTEXT_MAX_ENTRIES: usize = 4;
240const MEMORY_CONTEXT_ENTRY_MAX_CHARS: usize = 800;
241const MEMORY_CONTEXT_MAX_CHARS: usize = 4_000;
242const CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_KEEP_MESSAGES: usize = 12;
243const CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_CONTENT_CHARS: usize = 600;
244/// Proactive context-window budget in estimated characters (~4 chars/token).
245/// When the total character count of conversation history exceeds this limit,
246/// older turns are dropped before the request is sent to the model_provider,
247/// preventing context-window-exceeded errors.  Set conservatively below
248/// common context windows (128 k tokens ≈ 512 k chars) to leave room for
249/// system prompt, memory context, and model output.
250const PROACTIVE_CONTEXT_BUDGET_CHARS: usize = 400_000;
251/// Guardrail for hook-modified outbound channel content.
252const CHANNEL_HOOK_MAX_OUTBOUND_CHARS: usize = 20_000;
253
254type ProviderCacheMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn ModelProvider>>>>;
255type RouteSelectionMap = Arc<Mutex<HashMap<String, ChannelRouteSelection>>>;
256
257fn effective_channel_message_timeout_secs(configured: u64) -> u64 {
258    configured.max(MIN_CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS)
259}
260
261#[cfg(test)]
262fn channel_message_timeout_budget_secs(
263    message_timeout_secs: u64,
264    max_tool_iterations: usize,
265) -> u64 {
266    channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(
267        message_timeout_secs,
268        max_tool_iterations,
269        CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SCALE_CAP,
270    )
271}
272
273fn channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(
274    message_timeout_secs: u64,
275    max_tool_iterations: usize,
276    scale_cap: u64,
277) -> u64 {
278    let iterations = max_tool_iterations.max(1) as u64;
279    let scale = iterations.min(scale_cap);
280    message_timeout_secs.saturating_mul(scale)
281}

349#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
350#[allow(clippy::struct_excessive_bools)]
351struct InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
352    telegram: bool,
353    slack: bool,
354    discord: bool,
355    mattermost: bool,
356    matrix: bool,
357    whatsapp: bool,
358}
359
360impl InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
361    fn enabled_for_channel(self, channel: &str) -> bool {
362        match channel {
363            "telegram" => self.telegram,
364            "slack" => self.slack,
365            "discord" => self.discord,
366            "mattermost" => self.mattermost,
367            "matrix" => self.matrix,
368            "whatsapp" => self.whatsapp,
369            _ => false,
370        }
371    }
372}
373
374fn interrupt_on_new_message_config(
375    channels: &zeroclaw_config::schema::ChannelsConfig,
376) -> InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
377    InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
378        telegram: channels
379            .telegram
380            .get("default")
381            .is_some_and(|tg| tg.interrupt_on_new_message),
382        slack: channels
383            .slack
384            .get("default")
385            .is_some_and(|sl| sl.interrupt_on_new_message),
386        discord: channels
387            .discord
388            .get("default")
389            .is_some_and(|dc| dc.interrupt_on_new_message),
390        mattermost: channels
391            .mattermost
392            .get("default")
393            .is_some_and(|mm| mm.interrupt_on_new_message),
394        matrix: channels
395            .matrix
396            .get("default")
397            .is_some_and(|mx| mx.interrupt_on_new_message),
398        whatsapp: channels
399            .whatsapp
400            .get("default")
401            .is_some_and(|wa| wa.interrupt_on_new_message),
402    }
403}

412#[derive(Clone)]
413struct ChannelRuntimeContext {
414    channels_by_name: Arc<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>>>,
415    model_provider: Arc<dyn ModelProvider>,
416    model_provider_ref: Arc<String>,
417    /// Alias of the agent that owns this runtime context. Stamped onto
418    /// every per-message tracing span so descendant events inherit the
419    /// attribution without each call site re-passing it.
420    agent_alias: Arc<String>,
421    /// Resolved aliased-agent config for the agent owning this
422    /// runtime context. Per-channel agent dispatch (one agent per
423    /// channel.`<type>`.`<alias>`) is a follow-up.
424    agent_cfg: Arc<zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig>,
425    prompt_config: Arc<zeroclaw_config::schema::Config>,
426    memory: Arc<dyn Memory>,
427    memory_strategy: Arc<dyn MemoryStrategy>,
428    tools_registry: Arc<Vec<Box<dyn Tool>>>,
429    observer: Arc<dyn Observer>,
430    system_prompt: Arc<String>,
431    model: Arc<String>,
432    temperature: Option<f64>,
433    auto_save_memory: bool,
434    max_tool_iterations: usize,
435    min_relevance_score: f64,
436    conversation_histories: ConversationHistoryMap,
437    pending_new_sessions: PendingNewSessionSet,
438    provider_cache: ProviderCacheMap,
439    route_overrides: RouteSelectionMap,
440    reliability: Arc<zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig>,
441    provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions,
442    workspace_dir: Arc<PathBuf>,
443    message_timeout_secs: u64,
444    interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig,
445    multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig,
446    media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig,
447    transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig,
448    /// Resolved per-agent transcription provider alias (`<type>.<alias>`)
449    /// for the runtime-active agent that owns this channel context.
450    /// Empty when the agent has no transcription_provider set; downstream
451    /// `TranscriptionManager.transcribe` calls then fail loud.
452    agent_transcription_provider: String,
453    hooks: Option<Arc<zeroclaw_runtime::hooks::HookRunner>>,
454    non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc<Vec<String>>,
455    autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel,
456    tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc<Vec<String>>,
457    model_routes: Arc<Vec<zeroclaw_config::schema::ModelRouteConfig>>,
458    query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig,
459    ack_reactions: bool,
460    show_tool_calls: bool,
461    session_store: Option<Arc<dyn zeroclaw_infra::session_backend::SessionBackend>>,
462    /// Non-interactive approval manager for channel-driven runs.
463    /// Enforces `auto_approve` / `always_ask` / supervised policy from
464    /// `[autonomy]` config; auto-denies tools that would need interactive
465    /// approval since no operator is present on channel runs.
466    approval_manager: Arc<ApprovalManager>,
467    activated_tools:
468        Option<std::sync::Arc<std::sync::Mutex<zeroclaw_runtime::tools::ActivatedToolSet>>>,
469    cost_tracking: Option<ChannelCostTrackingState>,
470    pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig,
471    max_tool_result_chars: usize,
472    context_token_budget: usize,
473    debouncer: Arc<zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer>,
474    /// HMAC receipt generator. `Some` when `[agent.resolved.tool_receipts] enabled = true`.
475    /// Threaded into `run_tool_call_loop` so `tool_execution::execute_one_tool`
476    /// can sign each result.
477    receipt_generator: Option<zeroclaw_runtime::agent::tool_receipts::ReceiptGenerator>,
478    /// Mirror of `[agent.resolved.tool_receipts] show_in_response`. When true,
479    /// `process_channel_message` renders the per-turn collector as a trailing
480    /// `Tool receipts:` block sent after the main reply.
481    show_receipts_in_response: bool,
482    last_applied_config_stamp: Arc<Mutex<Option<ConfigFileStamp>>>,
483    runtime_defaults_override: Arc<Mutex<Option<Arc<ChannelRuntimeOverride>>>>,
484}
485
486#[derive(Clone)]
487struct InFlightSenderTaskState {
488    task_id: u64,
489    cancellation: CancellationToken,
490    completion: Arc<InFlightTaskCompletion>,
491}
492
493struct InFlightTaskCompletion {
494    done: AtomicBool,
495    notify: tokio::sync::Notify,
496}
497
498impl InFlightTaskCompletion {
499    fn new() -> Self {
500        Self {
501            done: AtomicBool::new(false),
502            notify: tokio::sync::Notify::new(),
503        }
504    }
505
506    fn mark_done(&self) {
507        self.done.store(true, Ordering::Release);
508        self.notify.notify_waiters();
509    }
510
511    async fn wait(&self) {
512        if self.done.load(Ordering::Acquire) {
513            return;
514        }
515        self.notify.notified().await;
516    }
517}
518
519fn conversation_memory_key(msg: &zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage) -> String {
520    // Include thread_ts for per-topic memory isolation in forum groups
521    let raw = match &msg.thread_ts {
522        Some(tid) => format!("{}_{}_{}_{}", msg.channel, tid, msg.sender, msg.id),
523        None => format!("{}_{}_{}", msg.channel, msg.sender, msg.id),
524    };
525    sanitize_session_key(&raw)
526}
527
528pub fn conversation_history_key(msg: &zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage) -> String {
529    // Channel prefix includes the zeroclaw alias when present so two bots
530    // on the same platform (e.g. `discord.clamps` + `discord.glados`)
531    // compute distinct session_keys and don't share conversation history.
532    let channel_scope = match &msg.channel_alias {
533        Some(alias) => format!("{}.{}", msg.channel, alias),
534        None => msg.channel.clone(),
535    };
536    if msg.channel == "wecom_ws" {
537        return sanitize_session_key(&format!("{channel_scope}_{}", msg.reply_target));
538    }
539    // reply_target gives per-channel isolation (distinct Discord/Slack
540    // channels) and thread_ts gives per-topic isolation in forum groups.
541    // Sanitize so the runtime HashMap key matches `SessionStore::list_sessions`
542    // after a restart; otherwise hydration loads sessions under the on-disk
543    // (sanitized) name while lookup keeps producing the un-sanitized form.
544    let thread_scope = match msg.thread_ts.as_deref() {
545        // Matrix root events can be self-anchored when `reply_in_thread`
546        // is enabled so outbound replies open a thread. That anchor is a
547        // delivery detail, not a conversation-history boundary; otherwise
548        // every top-level Matrix message becomes a fresh session.
549        Some(tid) if is_matrix_channel_name(&msg.channel) && tid == msg.id => None,
550        other => other,
551    };
552    let raw = match thread_scope {
553        Some(tid) => format!("{channel_scope}_{}_{tid}_{}", msg.reply_target, msg.sender),
554        None => format!("{channel_scope}_{}_{}", msg.reply_target, msg.sender),
555    };
556    sanitize_session_key(&raw)
557}
558
559fn followup_thread_id(msg: &zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage) -> Option<String> {
560    if is_matrix_channel_name(&msg.channel) {
561        msg.thread_ts.clone()
562    } else {
563        msg.thread_ts.clone().or_else(|| Some(msg.id.clone()))
564    }
565}
566
567fn interruption_scope_key(msg: &zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage) -> String {
568    if msg.channel == "wecom_ws" && msg.reply_target.starts_with("group--") {
569        let channel_scope = match &msg.channel_alias {
570            Some(alias) => format!("{}.{}", msg.channel, alias),
571            None => msg.channel.clone(),
572        };
573        return sanitize_session_key(&format!("{channel_scope}_{}", msg.reply_target));
574    }
575
576    match &msg.interruption_scope_id {
577        Some(scope) => format!(
578            "{}_{}_{}_{}",
579            msg.channel, msg.reply_target, msg.sender, scope
580        ),
581        None => format!("{}_{}_{}", msg.channel, msg.reply_target, msg.sender),
582    }
583}
584
585/// Returns `true` when `content` is a `/stop` command (with optional `@botname` suffix).
586/// Not gated on channel type — all non-CLI channels support `/stop`.
587fn is_stop_command(content: &str) -> bool {
588    let trimmed = content.trim();
589    if !trimmed.starts_with('/') {
590        return false;
591    }
592    let cmd = trimmed.split_whitespace().next().unwrap_or("");
593    let base = cmd.split('@').next().unwrap_or(cmd);
594    base.eq_ignore_ascii_case("/stop")
595}
596
597/// Strip tool-call XML tags from outgoing messages.
598///
599/// LLM responses may contain `<function_calls>`, `<function_call>`,
600/// `<tool_call>`, `<toolcall>`, `<tool-call>`, `<tool>`, or `<invoke>`
601/// blocks that are internal protocol and must not be forwarded to end
602/// users on any channel.
603pub(crate) fn strip_tool_call_tags(message: &str) -> String {
604    const TOOL_CALL_OPEN_TAGS: [&str; 7] = [
605        "<function_calls>",
606        "<function_call>",
607        "<tool_call>",
608        "<toolcall>",
609        "<tool-call>",
610        "<tool>",
611        "<invoke>",
612    ];
613
614    fn find_first_tag<'a>(haystack: &str, tags: &'a [&'a str]) -> Option<(usize, &'a str)> {
615        tags.iter()
616            .filter_map(|tag| haystack.find(tag).map(|idx| (idx, *tag)))
617            .min_by_key(|(idx, _)| *idx)
618    }
619
620    fn matching_close_tag(open_tag: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
621        match open_tag {
622            "<function_calls>" => Some("</function_calls>"),
623            "<function_call>" => Some("</function_call>"),
624            "<tool_call>" => Some("</tool_call>"),
625            "<toolcall>" => Some("</toolcall>"),
626            "<tool-call>" => Some("</tool-call>"),
627            "<tool>" => Some("</tool>"),
628            "<invoke>" => Some("</invoke>"),
629            _ => None,
630        }
631    }
632
633    fn extract_first_json_end(input: &str) -> Option<usize> {
634        let trimmed = input.trim_start();
635        let trim_offset = input.len().saturating_sub(trimmed.len());
636
637        for (byte_idx, ch) in trimmed.char_indices() {
638            if ch != '{' && ch != '[' {
639                continue;
640            }
641
642            let slice = &trimmed[byte_idx..];
643            let mut stream =
644                serde_json::Deserializer::from_str(slice).into_iter::<serde_json::Value>();
645            if let Some(Ok(_value)) = stream.next() {
646                let consumed = stream.byte_offset();
647                if consumed > 0 {
648                    return Some(trim_offset + byte_idx + consumed);
649                }
650            }
651        }
652
653        None
654    }
655
656    fn strip_leading_close_tags(mut input: &str) -> &str {
657        loop {
658            let trimmed = input.trim_start();
659            if !trimmed.starts_with("</") {
660                return trimmed;
661            }
662
663            let Some(close_end) = trimmed.find('>') else {
664                return "";
665            };
666            input = &trimmed[close_end + 1..];
667        }
668    }
669
670    // Does the tag structure run to the end of the message? A *real* truncated
671    // tool call is the model getting cut off, so the unterminated structure is
672    // the last thing in the message. If natural-language prose resumes after the
673    // tags, this is an inline *example* (the model is discussing tool calls), not
674    // a truncation — so we should keep it. Bias toward keeping: a little leaked
675    // XML beats eating the user's text.
676    fn tool_structure_runs_to_end(inner: &str) -> bool {
677        let mut rest = inner.trim_start();
678        while rest.starts_with('<') {
679            match rest.find('>') {
680                Some(gt) => rest = rest[gt + 1..].trim_start(),
681                None => return true,
682            }
683        }
684        let tail = rest.trim();
685        if tail.is_empty() {
686            return true;
687        }
688        !looks_like_prose(tail)
689    }
690
691    // Heuristic: does `text` read like resumed natural-language prose (as opposed
692    // to a cut-off parameter value)? True on an internal sentence boundary
693    // (". " / "! " / "? " + a letter) or a multi-word string that ends like a
694    // sentence. Deliberately lenient so ambiguous tails are kept, not dropped.
695    fn looks_like_prose(text: &str) -> bool {
696        let bytes = text.as_bytes();
697        for i in 0..bytes.len().saturating_sub(1) {
698            if matches!(bytes[i], b'.' | b'!' | b'?')
699                && matches!(bytes[i + 1], b' ' | b'\n' | b'\t')
700                && text[i + 1..]
701                    .trim_start()
702                    .chars()
703                    .next()
704                    .is_some_and(|c| c.is_alphabetic())
705            {
706                return true;
707            }
708        }
709        let trimmed = text.trim_end();
710        let ends_like_sentence = trimmed
711            .chars()
712            .last()
713            .is_some_and(|c| matches!(c, '.' | '!' | '?'))
714            && trimmed
715                .chars()
716                .rev()
717                .nth(1)
718                .is_some_and(|c| c.is_alphabetic());
719        ends_like_sentence && text.trim().contains(' ')
720    }
721
722    let mut kept_segments = Vec::new();
723    let mut remaining = message;
724
725    while let Some((start, open_tag)) = find_first_tag(remaining, &TOOL_CALL_OPEN_TAGS) {
726        let before = &remaining[..start];
727        if !before.is_empty() {
728            kept_segments.push(before.to_string());
729        }
730
731        let Some(close_tag) = matching_close_tag(open_tag) else {
732            break;
733        };
734        let after_open = &remaining[start + open_tag.len()..];
735
736        if let Some(close_idx) = after_open.find(close_tag) {
737            remaining = &after_open[close_idx + close_tag.len()..];
738            continue;
739        }
740
741        if let Some(consumed_end) = extract_first_json_end(after_open) {
742            remaining = strip_leading_close_tags(&after_open[consumed_end..]);
743            continue;
744        }
745
746        // Unterminated open tag with no parseable JSON body. Drop the broken
747        // tail ONLY when it looks like tool-call structure AND that structure
748        // runs to the end of the message — a real truncation where the model was
749        // cut off mid-call. If prose resumes after the structure, the model is
750        // showing an *example*, not making a call, so keep it verbatim (a little
751        // leaked XML beats eating the reply). Text merely mentioning a tag is
752        // likewise kept.
753        let inner = after_open.trim_start();
754        let inner_lower = inner.to_ascii_lowercase();
755        let looks_like_tool_structure = inner_lower.starts_with("<invoke")
756            || inner_lower.starts_with("<parameter")
757            || inner_lower.starts_with("<tool")
758            || inner_lower.starts_with("<function")
759            || inner.starts_with('{')
760            || inner.starts_with('[');
761        if looks_like_tool_structure && tool_structure_runs_to_end(inner) {
762            remaining = "";
763            break;
764        }
765
766        kept_segments.push(remaining[start..].to_string());
767        remaining = "";
768        break;
769    }
770
771    if !remaining.is_empty() {
772        kept_segments.push(remaining.to_string());
773    }
774
775    let mut result = kept_segments.concat();
776
777    // Clean up any resulting blank lines (but preserve paragraphs)
778    while result.contains("\n\n\n") {
779        result = result.replace("\n\n\n", "\n\n");
780    }
781
782    result.trim().to_string()
783}
784
785fn channel_delivery_instructions(channel_name: &str) -> Option<&'static str> {
786    match channel_name {
787        "matrix" => Some(
788            "When responding on Matrix:\n\
789             - Use Markdown formatting (bold, italic, code blocks)\n\
790             - Be concise and direct\n\
791             - For media attachments use markers: [IMAGE:<absolute-path>], [DOCUMENT:<absolute-path>], [VIDEO:<absolute-path>], [AUDIO:<absolute-path>], or [VOICE:<absolute-path>]\n\
792             - Paths inside markers MUST be absolute (starting with /). Never use relative paths.\n\
793             - Keep normal text outside markers and never wrap markers in code fences.\n\
794             - When you receive a [Voice message], the user spoke to you. Respond naturally as in conversation.\n\
795             - Your text reply will automatically be converted to audio and sent back as a voice message.\n",
796        ),
797        "discord" => Some(
798            "When responding on Discord:\n\
799             - Use Markdown formatting (bold, italic, code blocks)\n\
800             - Be concise and direct\n\
801             - For media attachments use markers: [IMAGE:<absolute-path>], [DOCUMENT:<absolute-path>], [VIDEO:<absolute-path>], [AUDIO:<absolute-path>], or [VOICE:<absolute-path>]\n\
802             - Paths inside markers MUST be absolute (starting with /) and live inside the configured workspace directory. Never use relative paths.\n\
803             - Remote media is also accepted via http:// or https:// URLs in the same marker form.\n\
804             - Keep normal text outside markers and never wrap markers in code fences.\n",
805        ),
806        "telegram" => Some(
807            "When responding on Telegram:\n\
808             - Include media markers for files or URLs that should be sent as attachments\n\
809             - Use **bold** for key terms, section titles, and important info (renders as <b>)\n\
810             - Use *italic* for emphasis (renders as <i>)\n\
811             - Use `backticks` for inline code, commands, or technical terms\n\
812             - Use triple backticks for code blocks\n\
813             - Use emoji naturally to add personality — but don't overdo it\n\
814             - Be concise and direct. Skip filler phrases like 'Great question!' or 'Certainly!'\n\
815             - Structure longer answers with bold headers, not raw markdown ## headers\n\
816             - For media attachments use markers: [IMAGE:<path-or-url>], [DOCUMENT:<path-or-url>], [VIDEO:<path-or-url>], [AUDIO:<path-or-url>], or [VOICE:<path-or-url>]\n\
817             - Keep normal text outside markers and never wrap markers in code fences.\n\
818             - Use tool results silently: answer the latest user message directly, and do not narrate delayed/internal tool execution bookkeeping.",
819        ),
820        "qq" => Some(
821            "When responding on QQ:\n\
822             - Use Markdown formatting\n\
823             - Be concise and direct\n\
824             - For media attachments use markers: [IMAGE:<path-or-url>], [DOCUMENT:<path-or-url>], \
825               [VIDEO:<path-or-url>], [VOICE:<path-or-url>]\n\
826             - Voice supports .wav, .mp3, .silk formats only. Other audio formats use [DOCUMENT:]\n\
827             - Keep normal text outside markers and never wrap markers in code fences.\n",
828        ),
829        "wechat" => Some(
830            "When responding on WeChat:\n\
831             - Be concise and direct\n\
832             - For media attachments use markers: [IMAGE:<path-or-url>], [DOCUMENT:<path-or-url>], \
833               [VIDEO:<path-or-url>], [AUDIO:<path-or-url>], or [VOICE:<path-or-url>]\n\
834             - Keep normal text outside markers and never wrap markers in code fences.\n\
835             - Use absolute local paths when sending generated files whenever possible.\n",
836        ),
837        "wecom_ws" => Some(
838            "When responding on WeCom AI Bot WebSocket:\n\
839             - Be concise and direct\n\
840             - Use Markdown text; the channel sends progressive draft updates when enabled\n\
841             - Do not use local attachment markers; outbound image payloads are not supported yet.\n",
842        ),
843        _ => None,
844    }
845}
846
847fn build_channel_system_prompt_for_message(
848    base_prompt: &str,
849    msg: &zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage,
850    target_channel: Option<&Arc<dyn Channel>>,
851) -> String {
852    let bot_mention = target_channel.and_then(|c| c.self_addressed_mention());
853    build_channel_system_prompt(
854        base_prompt,
855        &msg.channel,
856        &msg.reply_target,
857        &msg.sender,
858        &msg.id,
859        bot_mention.as_deref(),
860    )
861}
862
863fn build_channel_system_prompt(
864    base_prompt: &str,
865    channel_name: &str,
866    reply_target: &str,
867    sender: &str,
868    message_id: &str,
869    bot_mention: Option<&str>,
870) -> String {
871    let mut prompt = base_prompt.to_string();
872
873    refresh_channel_prompt_date_section(&mut prompt);
874
875    if let Some(instructions) = channel_delivery_instructions(channel_name) {
876        if prompt.is_empty() {
877            prompt = instructions.to_string();
878        } else {
879            prompt = format!("{prompt}\n\n{instructions}");
880        }
881    }
882
883    if let Some(mention) = bot_mention {
884        let block = format!(
885            "\n\nYour addressable handle on this channel: {mention}. \
886             When you see this exact string anywhere in an inbound message, \
887             it refers to YOU, not another agent or user. This same format \
888             is also what you should emit when you need to tag yourself or \
889             address peers in outbound replies on this channel."
890        );
891        prompt.push_str(&block);
892    }
893
894    if !reply_target.is_empty() {
895        // For most channels, `reply_target` is the address to send to (channel/room
896        // ID for Slack/Discord/Matrix, peer ID for Telegram/Signal). The webhook
897        // channel is the exception: its outbound JSON has both `recipient` and
898        // `thread_id`, and downstream services routing through it expect the
899        // *sender* as the recipient and the *thread/conversation* identifier in
900        // `thread_id`. Reusing `reply_target` as `to` for webhook would strip the
901        // thread context and the receiver would discard the callback.
902        let delivery_hint = if channel_name.eq_ignore_ascii_case("webhook") {
903            format!(
904                "delivery={{\"mode\":\"announce\",\"channel\":\"{channel_name}\",\
905                 \"to\":\"{sender}\",\"thread_id\":\"{reply_target}\"}}"
906            )
907        } else {
908            format!(
909                "delivery={{\"mode\":\"announce\",\"channel\":\"{channel_name}\",\
910                 \"to\":\"{reply_target}\"}}"
911            )
912        };
913        let context = format!(
914            "\n\nChannel context: You are currently responding on channel={channel_name}, \
915             reply_target={reply_target}, sender={sender}, message_id={message_id}. \
916             The sender field is the platform-specific user ID of the person who sent \
917             this message. Use it to distinguish between different users. \
918             The message_id field identifies this incoming message; pass it as the \
919             `message_id` argument when calling the `reaction` tool. \
920             When scheduling delayed messages or reminders \
921             via cron_add for this conversation, use {delivery_hint} so the message \
922             reaches the user.\n\nCalibration note: agents in this system currently err \
923             on the side of silence when a response would be appropriate, which users \
924             find frustrating. Skew toward replying. Memory is supplementary context \
925             that informs how you respond, not a gate on whether you respond."
926        );
927        prompt.push_str(&context);
928    }
929
930    prompt
931}
932
933fn current_date_section() -> String {
934    let now = chrono::Local::now();
935    format!(
936        "{CURRENT_DATE_HEADING}{} ({})",
937        now.format("%Y-%m-%d"),
938        now.format("%:z")
939    )
940}
941
942fn refresh_channel_prompt_date_section(prompt: &mut String) {
943    let runtime_start = prompt
944        .find("\n## Runtime")
945        .map(|i| i + 1)
946        .unwrap_or(prompt.len());
947
948    if let Some((start, heading_len)) = find_latest_date_heading_before(prompt, runtime_start) {
949        let content_start = start + heading_len;
950        let section_end = prompt[content_start..]
951            .find("\n## ")
952            .map(|i| content_start + i)
953            .unwrap_or(prompt.len());
954        prompt.replace_range(start..section_end, &current_date_section());
955    }
956}
957
958fn find_latest_date_heading_before(prompt: &str, before: usize) -> Option<(usize, usize)> {
959    let prefix = &prompt[..before];
960    [CURRENT_DATE_HEADING, LEGACY_CURRENT_DATE_TIME_HEADING]
961        .iter()
962        .filter_map(|heading| prefix.rfind(heading).map(|start| (start, heading.len())))
963        .max_by_key(|(start, _)| *start)
964}
965
966fn timestamp_channel_user_content(content: &str) -> String {
967    let now = chrono::Local::now();
968    format!("[{}] {}", now.format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z"), content)
969}
970
971fn channel_history_content_for_user_turn(content: &str) -> String {
972    let (cleaned, image_refs) = zeroclaw_providers::multimodal::parse_image_markers(content);
973    if image_refs.is_empty() {
974        return content.to_string();
975    }
976
977    let mut cleaned = cleaned.trim().to_string();
978    while cleaned.contains("\n\n\n") {
979        cleaned = cleaned.replace("\n\n\n", "\n\n");
980    }
981
982    if cleaned.is_empty() {
983        "[Image attachment processed by vision model]".to_string()
984    } else {
985        cleaned
986    }
987}
988
989fn restore_current_user_turn_media_payload(
990    turns: &mut [ChatMessage],
991    history_content: &str,
992    full_content: &str,
993) {
994    if history_content == full_content {
995        return;
996    }
997
998    if let Some(turn) = turns
999        .iter_mut()
1000        .rev()
1001        .find(|turn| turn.role == "user" && turn.content == history_content)
1002    {
1003        turn.content = full_content.to_string();
1004    }
1005}
1006
1007fn strip_historical_image_payloads(turns: &mut Vec<ChatMessage>) {
1008    if turns.len() <= 1 {
1009        return;
1010    }
1011
1012    let last_idx = turns.len() - 1;
1013    for turn in &mut turns[..last_idx] {
1014        if turn.content.contains("[IMAGE:") {
1015            turn.content = channel_history_content_for_user_turn(&turn.content);
1016        }
1017    }
1018
1019    let current = turns.pop();
1020    turns.retain(|turn| !turn.content.trim().is_empty());
1021    if let Some(current) = current {
1022        turns.push(current);
1023    }
1024}
1025
1026fn normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns: Vec<ChatMessage>) -> Vec<ChatMessage> {
1027    let mut normalized = Vec::with_capacity(turns.len());
1028    let mut expecting_user = true;
1029
1030    for turn in turns {
1031        match (expecting_user, turn.role.as_str()) {
1032            // Pass through tool-role messages preserved by
1033            // keep_tool_context_turns.  After a tool result the
1034            // next expected message is an assistant response, same as
1035            // after a user message.
1036            (_, "tool") | (true, "user") => {
1037                normalized.push(turn);
1038                expecting_user = false;
1039            }
1040            (false, "assistant") => {
1041                normalized.push(turn);
1042                expecting_user = true;
1043            }
1044            // Interrupted channel turns can produce consecutive user messages
1045            // (no assistant persisted yet). Merge instead of dropping.
1046            (false, "user") | (true, "assistant") => {
1047                if let Some(last_turn) = normalized.last_mut()
1048                    && !turn.content.is_empty()
1049                {
1050                    if !last_turn.content.is_empty() {
1051                        last_turn.content.push_str("\n\n");
1052                    }
1053                    last_turn.content.push_str(&turn.content);
1054                }
1055            }
1056            _ => {}
1057        }
1058    }
1059
1060    normalized
1061}
1062
1063/// Remove `<tool_result …>…</tool_result>` blocks (and a leading `[Tool results]`
1064/// header, if present) from a conversation-history entry so that stale tool
1065/// output is never presented to the LLM without the corresponding `<tool_call>`.
1066fn strip_tool_result_content(text: &str) -> String {
1067    static TOOL_RESULT_RE: std::sync::LazyLock<regex::Regex> = std::sync::LazyLock::new(|| {
1068        regex::Regex::new(r"(?s)<tool_result[^>]*>.*?</tool_result>").unwrap()
1069    });
1070
1071    let cleaned = TOOL_RESULT_RE.replace_all(text, "");
1072    let cleaned = cleaned.trim();
1073
1074    // If the only remaining content is the header, drop it entirely.
1075    if cleaned == "[Tool results]" || cleaned.is_empty() {
1076        return String::new();
1077    }
1078
1079    cleaned.to_string()
1080}
1081
1082/// Remove a leading `[Used tools: ...]` line from a cached assistant turn.
1083///
1084/// The tool-context summary is prepended to history entries so the LLM retains
1085/// awareness of prior tool usage. However, when these entries are loaded back
1086/// into the LLM context, the bracket-format leaks into generated output and
1087/// gets forwarded to end users as-is (bug #4400). Stripping the prefix on
1088/// reload prevents the model from learning and reproducing this internal format.
1089fn strip_tool_summary_prefix(text: &str) -> String {
1090    if let Some(rest) = text.strip_prefix("[Used tools:") {
1091        // Find the closing bracket, then skip it and any leading newline(s).
1092        if let Some(bracket_end) = rest.find(']') {
1093            let after_bracket = &rest[bracket_end + 1..];
1094            let trimmed = after_bracket.trim_start_matches('\n');
1095            if trimmed.is_empty() {
1096                return String::new();
1097            }
1098            return trimmed.to_string();
1099        }
1100    }
1101    text.to_string()
1102}
1103
1104fn supports_runtime_model_switch(channel_name: &str) -> bool {
1105    matches!(
1106        channel_name,
1107        "telegram" | "discord" | "matrix" | "slack" | "wecom_ws"
1108    )
1109}
1110
1111fn is_explicitly_addressed_channel_message(channel_name: &str, content: &str) -> bool {
1112    channel_name == "wecom_ws"
1113        && content.contains("[WeCom group message addressed to this bot via @")
1114}
1115
1116fn is_matrix_channel_name(channel_name: &str) -> bool {
1117    channel_name == "matrix" || channel_name.starts_with("matrix:")
1118}
1119
1120fn parse_runtime_command(channel_name: &str, content: &str) -> Option<ChannelRuntimeCommand> {
1121    let trimmed = content.trim();
1122    if !trimmed.starts_with('/') {
1123        return None;
1124    }
1125
1126    let mut parts = trimmed.split_whitespace();
1127    let command_token = parts.next()?;
1128    let base_command = command_token
1129        .split('@')
1130        .next()
1131        .unwrap_or(command_token)
1132        .to_ascii_lowercase();
1133
1134    match base_command.as_str() {
1135        // `/new` is available on every channel — no model-switch gate.
1136        "/new" => Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::NewSession),
1137        // Model/model_provider switching is channel-gated.
1138        "/models" if supports_runtime_model_switch(channel_name) => {
1139            if let Some(model_provider) = parts.next() {
1140                Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::SetProvider(
1141                    model_provider.trim().to_string(),
1142                ))
1143            } else {
1144                Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::ShowProviders)
1145            }
1146        }
1147        "/model" if supports_runtime_model_switch(channel_name) => {
1148            let model = parts.collect::<Vec<_>>().join(" ").trim().to_string();
1149            if model.is_empty() {
1150                Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::ShowModel)
1151            } else {
1152                Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::SetModel(model))
1153            }
1154        }
1155        "/config" if supports_runtime_model_switch(channel_name) => {
1156            Some(ChannelRuntimeCommand::ShowConfig)
1157        }
1158        _ => None,
1159    }
1160}
1161
1162/// Verify `name` matches a canonical model provider family known to the
1163/// runtime registry. Returns the canonical (case-corrected) name, or `None`
1164/// when the input doesn't name a known family. Used by the channel
1165/// `/models` slash command, which accepts only the bare family name; dotted
1166/// aliases (`<family>.<alias>`) are resolved elsewhere through
1167/// `create_resilient_model_provider_from_ref`.
1168fn canonical_model_provider_name(name: &str) -> Option<String> {
1169    let candidate = name.trim();
1170    if candidate.is_empty() {
1171        return None;
1172    }
1173
1174    zeroclaw_providers::list_model_providers()
1175        .into_iter()
1176        .find(|model_provider| model_provider.name.eq_ignore_ascii_case(candidate))
1177        .map(|model_provider| model_provider.name.to_string())
1178}
1179
1180/// Outcome of resolving a `/models <arg>` request to a configured,
1181/// alias-backed provider ref. The bare family path must never construct a
1182/// provider that ignores the configured `[providers.models.<family>.<alias>]`
1183/// key/URI — every accepted route resolves to a real alias entry.
1184#[cfg_attr(test, derive(Debug))]
1185enum ModelsCommandResolution {
1186    /// A dotted `<family>.<alias>` ref backed by a configured entry.
1187    Resolved(String),
1188    /// The family is valid but has more than one configured alias; the user
1189    /// must qualify which one. Carries the canonical family and its aliases.
1190    Ambiguous {
1191        family: String,
1192        aliases: Vec<String>,
1193    },
1194    /// The family is valid but has no configured alias entry, so there is no
1195    /// credentialed provider to switch to.
1196    NoAlias(String),
1197    /// The argument names no known provider family.
1198    Unknown,
1199}
1200
1201/// Resolve a `/models <arg>` argument to a configured, alias-backed provider
1202/// ref. Accepts either a dotted `<family>.<alias>` that resolves to a real
1203/// `[providers.models.<family>.<alias>]` entry, or a bare family name that has
1204/// exactly one configured alias. A bare family with several aliases is
1205/// ambiguous; one with none has no credentialed provider. This keeps `/models`
1206/// inside the v0.8 alias model rather than constructing a bare provider that
1207/// silently ignores the configured key/URI.
1208fn resolve_models_command(
1209    config: &zeroclaw_config::schema::Config,
1210    raw: &str,
1211) -> ModelsCommandResolution {
1212    let candidate = raw.trim();
1213    if let Some((family, alias)) = candidate.split_once('.') {
1214        return match config.providers.models.find(family, alias) {
1215            Some(_) => ModelsCommandResolution::Resolved(format!("{family}.{alias}")),
1216            None => ModelsCommandResolution::NoAlias(candidate.to_string()),
1217        };
1218    }
1219
1220    let Some(family) = canonical_model_provider_name(candidate) else {
1221        return ModelsCommandResolution::Unknown;
1222    };
1223
1224    let mut aliases: Vec<String> = config
1225        .providers
1226        .models
1227        .aliases_of(&family)
1228        .map(ToString::to_string)
1229        .collect();
1230    aliases.sort();
1231    match aliases.len() {
1232        0 => ModelsCommandResolution::NoAlias(family),
1233        1 => ModelsCommandResolution::Resolved(format!("{family}.{}", aliases[0])),
1234        _ => ModelsCommandResolution::Ambiguous { family, aliases },
1235    }
1236}
1237
1238fn resolve_provider_ref_for_runtime_switch(config: &Config, raw: &str) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
1239    match resolve_models_command(config, raw) {
1240        ModelsCommandResolution::Resolved(provider_ref) => Ok(provider_ref),
1241        ModelsCommandResolution::Ambiguous { family, aliases } => {
1242            let list = aliases
1243                .iter()
1244                .map(|alias| format!("{family}.{alias}"))
1245                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
1246                .join(", ");
1247            anyhow::bail!(
1248                "model_provider `{family}` has multiple configured aliases; use one of: {list}"
1249            )
1250        }
1251        ModelsCommandResolution::NoAlias(ref_or_family) => {
1252            anyhow::bail!(
1253                "model_provider `{ref_or_family}` does not resolve to a configured provider"
1254            )
1255        }
1256        ModelsCommandResolution::Unknown => {
1257            anyhow::bail!("unknown model_provider `{raw}`")
1258        }
1259    }
1260}
1261
1262fn resolved_runtime_model_provider_ref(
1263    config: &Config,
1264    agent_alias: &str,
1265) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
1266    let agent = config
1267        .agents
1268        .get(agent_alias)
1269        .with_context(|| format!("agents.{agent_alias} is not configured"))?;
1270    let configured = agent.model_provider.trim();
1271    if configured.is_empty() {
1272        anyhow::bail!(
1273            "agents.{agent_alias}.model_provider is empty; runtime reload requires a dotted `<type>.<alias>` provider reference"
1274        );
1275    }
1276    let (model_provider, _) = model_provider_entry_for_ref(config, configured)?;
1277    Ok(model_provider)
1278}
1279
1280fn model_provider_entry_for_ref<'a>(
1281    config: &'a Config,
1282    model_provider: &str,
1283) -> anyhow::Result<(String, &'a zeroclaw_config::schema::ModelProviderConfig)> {
1284    let trimmed = model_provider.trim();
1285    if trimmed.is_empty() {
1286        anyhow::bail!("model_provider reference must not be empty");
1287    }
1288
1289    let Some((provider_type, provider_alias)) = trimmed.split_once('.') else {
1290        anyhow::bail!("model_provider `{trimmed}` must use `<type>.<alias>` form");
1291    };
1292    let Some(entry) = config.providers.models.find(provider_type, provider_alias) else {
1293        anyhow::bail!("model_provider `{trimmed}` does not resolve to a configured provider");
1294    };
1295    Ok((trimmed.to_string(), entry))
1296}
1297
1298/// Resolve runtime defaults from `config` against a specific dotted
1299/// `model_provider` reference (`"<type>.<alias>"`) — the per-agent
1300/// resolution path.
1301fn runtime_defaults_from_config(
1302    config: &Config,
1303    model_provider: &str,
1304) -> anyhow::Result<ChannelRuntimeDefaults> {
1305    let (default_model_provider, entry) = model_provider_entry_for_ref(config, model_provider)?;
1306    let model = entry
1307        .model
1308        .as_deref()
1309        .map(str::trim)
1310        .filter(|model| !model.is_empty())
1311        .map(ToString::to_string)
1312        .ok_or_else(|| {
1313            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
1314                ERROR,
1315                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Reject)
1316                    .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Failure)
1317                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({
1318                        "model_provider": model_provider,
1319                        "reason": "no_model_configured",
1320                    })),
1321                "orchestrator: model_provider has no resolvable model"
1322            );
1323            anyhow::Error::msg(format!(
1324                "no model configured: model_provider '{model_provider}' does not resolve to a \
1325                 ModelProviderConfig with a `model` field, and providers.models has no \
1326                 fallback entry."
1327            ))
1328        })?;
1329    Ok(ChannelRuntimeDefaults {
1330        default_model_provider,
1331        model,
1332        temperature: entry.temperature,
1333        api_key: entry.api_key.clone(),
1334        api_url: entry.uri.clone(),
1335        reliability: config.reliability.clone(),
1336    })
1337}
1338
1339fn runtime_config_path(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext) -> Option<PathBuf> {
1340    ctx.provider_runtime_options
1341        .zeroclaw_dir
1342        .as_ref()
1343        .map(|dir| dir.join("config.toml"))
1344}
1345
1346fn runtime_defaults_snapshot(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext) -> ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot {
1347    if let Some(runtime_override) = ctx
1348        .runtime_defaults_override
1349        .lock()
1350        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
1351        .clone()
1352    {
1353        return ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot {
1354            config: Arc::clone(&runtime_override.config),
1355            defaults: runtime_override.defaults.clone(),
1356            hot: true,
1357            generation: runtime_override.generation,
1358        };
1359    }
1360
1361    ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot {
1362        config: Arc::clone(&ctx.prompt_config),
1363        defaults: ChannelRuntimeDefaults {
1364            default_model_provider: ctx.model_provider_ref.as_str().to_string(),
1365            model: ctx.model.as_str().to_string(),
1366            temperature: ctx.temperature,
1367            api_key: None,
1368            api_url: None,
1369            reliability: (*ctx.reliability).clone(),
1370        },
1371        hot: false,
1372        generation: 0,
1373    }
1374}
1375
1376async fn config_file_stamp(path: &Path) -> Option<ConfigFileStamp> {
1377    let metadata = tokio::fs::metadata(path).await.ok()?;
1378    let modified = metadata.modified().ok()?;
1379    Some(ConfigFileStamp {
1380        modified,
1381        len: metadata.len(),
1382    })
1383}
1384
1385async fn load_runtime_config_and_defaults(
1386    path: &Path,
1387    agent_alias: &str,
1388) -> Result<(Config, ChannelRuntimeDefaults)> {
1389    let contents = tokio::fs::read_to_string(path)
1390        .await
1391        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to read {}", path.display()))?;
1392    let mut parsed: Config = zeroclaw_config::migration::migrate_to_current(&contents)
1393        .with_context(|| format!("Failed to migrate {}", path.display()))?;
1394    parsed.config_path = path.to_path_buf();
1395
1396    if let Some(zeroclaw_dir) = path.parent() {
1397        let store =
1398            zeroclaw_runtime::security::SecretStore::new(zeroclaw_dir, parsed.secrets.encrypt);
1399        parsed.decrypt_secrets(&store)?;
1400    }
1401    let applied = zeroclaw_config::env_overrides::apply_env_overrides(&mut parsed)?;
1402    parsed.env_overridden_paths = applied.paths;
1403    parsed.pre_override_snapshots = applied.snapshots;
1404
1405    let model_provider = resolved_runtime_model_provider_ref(&parsed, agent_alias)?;
1406    let defaults = runtime_defaults_from_config(&parsed, &model_provider)?;
1407    Ok((parsed, defaults))
1408}
1409
1410async fn maybe_apply_runtime_config_update(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext) -> Result<()> {
1411    let Some(config_path) = runtime_config_path(ctx) else {
1412        return Ok(());
1413    };
1414
1415    let Some(stamp) = config_file_stamp(&config_path).await else {
1416        return Ok(());
1417    };
1418
1419    {
1420        let last = ctx
1421            .last_applied_config_stamp
1422            .lock()
1423            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1424        if *last == Some(stamp) {
1425            return Ok(());
1426        }
1427    }
1428
1429    let (next_config, next_defaults) =
1430        load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, ctx.agent_alias.as_str()).await?;
1431    let next_config = Arc::new(next_config);
1432    let next_options = zeroclaw_providers::options_for_provider_ref(
1433        next_config.as_ref(),
1434        &next_defaults.default_model_provider,
1435        &ctx.provider_runtime_options,
1436    );
1437    let model_provider_instance = zeroclaw_providers::create_resilient_model_provider_from_ref(
1438        next_config.as_ref(),
1439        &next_defaults.default_model_provider,
1440        next_defaults.api_key.as_deref(),
1441        next_defaults.api_url.as_deref(),
1442        &next_defaults.reliability,
1443        &next_options,
1444    )?;
1445    let model_provider_instance: Arc<dyn ModelProvider> = Arc::from(model_provider_instance);
1446
1447    if let Err(err) = model_provider_instance.warmup().await {
1448        if zeroclaw_providers::reliable::is_non_retryable(&err) {
1449            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(WARN, ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note).with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown).with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"model_provider": next_defaults.default_model_provider, "model": next_defaults.model, "err": err.to_string()})), "Rejecting config reload: model not available (non-retryable)");
1450            return Ok(());
1451        }
1452        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
1453            WARN,
1454            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
1455                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
1456                .with_attrs(
1457                    ::serde_json::json!({"model_provider": next_defaults.default_model_provider, "err": err.to_string()})
1458                ),
1459            "ModelProvider warmup failed after config reload (retryable, applying anyway)"
1460        );
1461    }
1462
1463    {
1464        let mut override_guard = ctx
1465            .runtime_defaults_override
1466            .lock()
1467            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1468        let next_generation = override_guard.as_ref().map_or(1, |runtime_override| {
1469            runtime_override.generation.saturating_add(1)
1470        });
1471        let next_override = Arc::new(ChannelRuntimeOverride {
1472            config: Arc::clone(&next_config),
1473            defaults: next_defaults.clone(),
1474            generation: next_generation,
1475        });
1476        let cache_key =
1477            provider_cache_key(&next_defaults.default_model_provider, None, next_generation);
1478
1479        let mut cache = ctx.provider_cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1480        cache.clear();
1481        cache.insert(cache_key, Arc::clone(&model_provider_instance));
1482        *override_guard = Some(next_override);
1483    }
1484
1485    *ctx.last_applied_config_stamp
1486        .lock()
1487        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Some(stamp);
1488
1489    ::zeroclaw_log::record!(INFO, ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note).with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"path": config_path.display().to_string(), "model_provider": next_defaults.default_model_provider, "model": next_defaults.model, "temperature": next_defaults.temperature, "agent_model_provider": next_defaults.default_model_provider})), "Applied updated channel runtime config from disk");
1490
1491    Ok(())
1492}
1493
1494fn default_route_selection_from_snapshot(
1495    defaults_snapshot: &ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot,
1496) -> ChannelRouteSelection {
1497    let defaults = defaults_snapshot.defaults.clone();
1498    ChannelRouteSelection {
1499        model_provider: defaults.default_model_provider,
1500        model: defaults.model,
1501        api_key: None,
1502    }
1503}
1504
1505fn get_route_selection(
1506    ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext,
1507    sender_key: &str,
1508    defaults_snapshot: &ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot,
1509) -> ChannelRouteSelection {
1510    ctx.route_overrides
1511        .lock()
1512        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
1513        .get(sender_key)
1514        .cloned()
1515        .unwrap_or_else(|| default_route_selection_from_snapshot(defaults_snapshot))
1516}
1517
1518fn set_route_selection(
1519    ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext,
1520    sender_key: &str,
1521    next: ChannelRouteSelection,
1522    defaults_snapshot: &ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot,
1523) {
1524    let default_route = default_route_selection_from_snapshot(defaults_snapshot);
1525    let mut routes = ctx
1526        .route_overrides
1527        .lock()
1528        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1529    if next == default_route {
1530        routes.remove(sender_key);
1531    } else {
1532        routes.insert(sender_key.to_string(), next);
1533    }
1534}
1535
1536fn clear_sender_history(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str) {
1537    ctx.conversation_histories
1538        .lock()
1539        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
1540        .pop(sender_key);
1541}
1542
1543fn mark_sender_for_new_session(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str) {
1544    ctx.pending_new_sessions
1545        .lock()
1546        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
1547        .insert(sender_key.to_string());
1548}
1549
1550fn take_pending_new_session(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str) -> bool {
1551    ctx.pending_new_sessions
1552        .lock()
1553        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
1554        .remove(sender_key)
1555}
1556
1557fn replace_available_skills_section(base_prompt: &str, refreshed_skills: &str) -> String {
1558    const SKILLS_HEADER: &str = "## Available Skills\n\n";
1559    const SKILLS_END: &str = "</available_skills>";
1560    const WORKSPACE_HEADER: &str = "## Workspace\n\n";
1561
1562    if let Some(start) = base_prompt.find(SKILLS_HEADER)
1563        && let Some(rel_end) = base_prompt[start..].find(SKILLS_END)
1564    {
1565        let end = start + rel_end + SKILLS_END.len();
1566        let tail = base_prompt[end..]
1567            .strip_prefix("\n\n")
1568            .unwrap_or(&base_prompt[end..]);
1569
1570        let mut refreshed = String::with_capacity(
1571            base_prompt.len().saturating_sub(end.saturating_sub(start))
1572                + refreshed_skills.len()
1573                + 2,
1574        );
1575        refreshed.push_str(&base_prompt[..start]);
1576        if !refreshed_skills.is_empty() {
1577            refreshed.push_str(refreshed_skills);
1578            refreshed.push_str("\n\n");
1579        }
1580        refreshed.push_str(tail);
1581        return refreshed;
1582    }
1583
1584    if refreshed_skills.is_empty() {
1585        return base_prompt.to_string();
1586    }
1587
1588    if let Some(workspace_start) = base_prompt.find(WORKSPACE_HEADER) {
1589        let mut refreshed = String::with_capacity(base_prompt.len() + refreshed_skills.len() + 2);
1590        refreshed.push_str(&base_prompt[..workspace_start]);
1591        refreshed.push_str(refreshed_skills);
1592        refreshed.push_str("\n\n");
1593        refreshed.push_str(&base_prompt[workspace_start..]);
1594        return refreshed;
1595    }
1596
1597    format!("{base_prompt}\n\n{refreshed_skills}")
1598}
1599
1600fn refreshed_new_session_system_prompt(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext) -> String {
1601    let refreshed_skills = zeroclaw_runtime::skills::skills_to_prompt_with_mode(
1602        &zeroclaw_runtime::skills::load_skills_with_config(
1603            ctx.workspace_dir.as_ref(),
1604            ctx.prompt_config.as_ref(),
1605        ),
1606        ctx.workspace_dir.as_ref(),
1607        ctx.prompt_config.skills.prompt_injection_mode,
1608    );
1609    replace_available_skills_section(ctx.system_prompt.as_str(), &refreshed_skills)
1610}
1611
1612fn compact_sender_history(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str) -> bool {
1613    let mut histories = ctx
1614        .conversation_histories
1615        .lock()
1616        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1617
1618    let Some(turns) = histories.get_mut(sender_key) else {
1619        return false;
1620    };
1621
1622    if turns.is_empty() {
1623        return false;
1624    }
1625
1626    let keep_from = turns
1627        .len()
1628        .saturating_sub(CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_KEEP_MESSAGES);
1629    let mut compacted = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns[keep_from..].to_vec());
1630
1631    for turn in &mut compacted {
1632        if turn.content.chars().count() > CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_CONTENT_CHARS {
1633            turn.content =
1634                truncate_with_ellipsis(&turn.content, CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_CONTENT_CHARS);
1635        }
1636    }
1637
1638    if compacted.is_empty() {
1639        turns.clear();
1640        return false;
1641    }
1642
1643    *turns = compacted;
1644    true
1645}
1646
1647/// Proactively trim conversation turns so that the total estimated character
1648/// count stays within [`PROACTIVE_CONTEXT_BUDGET_CHARS`].  Drops the oldest
1649/// turns first, but always preserves the most recent turn (the current user
1650/// message).  Returns the number of turns dropped.
1651fn proactive_trim_turns(turns: &mut Vec<ChatMessage>, budget: usize) -> usize {
1652    let total_chars: usize = turns.iter().map(|t| t.content.chars().count()).sum();
1653    if total_chars <= budget || turns.len() <= 1 {
1654        return 0;
1655    }
1656
1657    let mut excess = total_chars.saturating_sub(budget);
1658    let mut drop_count = 0;
1659
1660    // Walk from the oldest turn forward, but never drop the very last turn.
1661    while excess > 0 && drop_count < turns.len().saturating_sub(1) {
1662        excess = excess.saturating_sub(turns[drop_count].content.chars().count());
1663        drop_count += 1;
1664    }
1665
1666    if drop_count > 0 {
1667        turns.drain(..drop_count);
1668    }
1669    drop_count
1670}
1671
1672fn append_sender_turn(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str, turn: ChatMessage) {
1673    // Persist to JSONL before adding to in-memory history.
1674    if let Some(ref store) = ctx.session_store
1675        && let Err(e) = store.append(sender_key, &turn)
1676    {
1677        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
1678            WARN,
1679            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
1680                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
1681                .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"error": format!("{}", e)})),
1682            "Failed to persist session turn"
1683        );
1684    }
1685
1686    // Use the user-configured max_history_messages (fall back to
1687    // MAX_CHANNEL_HISTORY when the config value is 0 or absent).
1688    let max_history = {
1689        let configured = ctx.agent_cfg.resolved.max_history_messages;
1690        if configured > 0 {
1691            configured
1692        } else {
1693            MAX_CHANNEL_HISTORY
1694        }
1695    };
1696
1697    let mut histories = ctx
1698        .conversation_histories
1699        .lock()
1700        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1701    let turns = histories.get_or_insert_mut(sender_key.to_string(), Vec::new);
1702    turns.push(turn);
1703    while turns.len() > max_history {
1704        turns.remove(0);
1705    }
1706}
1707
1708/// Extract tool-call (assistant with tool_call content) and tool-result
1709/// messages from the current turn in the LLM history, excluding the final
1710/// assistant text response.  "Current turn" = everything after the last
1711/// user-role message.
1712fn extract_current_turn_tool_messages(history: &[ChatMessage]) -> Vec<ChatMessage> {
1713    // Find the index of the last user message — tool messages for the
1714    // current turn come after it.
1715    let last_user_idx = history.iter().rposition(|m| m.role == "user").unwrap_or(0);
1716
1717    let tail = &history[last_user_idx + 1..];
1718    if tail.is_empty() {
1719        return Vec::new();
1720    }
1721
1722    // Everything except the very last assistant message (which is the
1723    // final text response that gets stored separately).
1724    let end = if tail.last().is_some_and(|m| m.role == "assistant") {
1725        tail.len() - 1
1726    } else {
1727        tail.len()
1728    };
1729
1730    tail[..end]
1731        .iter()
1732        .filter(|m| m.role == "assistant" || m.role == "tool")
1733        .cloned()
1734        .collect()
1735}
1736
1737/// Remove tool-role and intermediate assistant tool-call messages from
1738/// conversation turns older than the most recent `keep_turns` user→assistant
1739/// exchanges.  This prevents unbounded history growth while preserving
1740/// tool context for the N most recent turns.
1741fn strip_old_tool_context(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender_key: &str, keep_turns: usize) {
1742    let mut histories = ctx
1743        .conversation_histories
1744        .lock()
1745        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1746
1747    let Some(turns) = histories.get_mut(sender_key) else {
1748        return;
1749    };
1750
1751    // Walk backwards to find the oldest user message that still belongs to the
1752    // most recent `keep_turns` exchanges. Everything before that boundary is
1753    // old enough to strip. If the session has fewer than `keep_turns` user
1754    // turns, preserve every message.
1755    let mut user_count = 0;
1756    let mut strip_before = 0;
1757    for (i, turn) in turns.iter().enumerate().rev() {
1758        if turn.role == "user" {
1759            user_count += 1;
1760            if user_count == keep_turns {
1761                strip_before = i;
1762                break;
1763            }
1764        }
1765    }
1766
1767    if user_count < keep_turns {
1768        return;
1769    }
1770
1771    // Remove tool and intermediate assistant messages before the boundary.
1772    // An "intermediate assistant" is one whose content looks like a tool
1773    // call, either in legacy XML / JSON forms or in native `tool_calls` JSON.
1774    let mut i = 0;
1775    while i < strip_before && i < turns.len() {
1776        let dominated = turns[i].role == "tool"
1777            || (turns[i].role == "assistant" && is_tool_call_content(&turns[i].content));
1778        if dominated {
1779            turns.remove(i);
1780            // Adjust boundary since we removed an element.
1781            strip_before = strip_before.saturating_sub(1);
1782        } else {
1783            i += 1;
1784        }
1785    }
1786}
1787
1788/// Heuristic: does this assistant message content represent a tool call
1789/// rather than a final text response?
1790fn is_tool_call_content(content: &str) -> bool {
1791    let trimmed = content.trim();
1792    trimmed.contains("<tool_call>")
1793        || trimmed.starts_with("{\"tool_call\"")
1794        || is_named_tool_call_json(trimmed)
1795        || is_native_tool_call_json(trimmed)
1796}
1797
1798fn is_named_tool_call_json(content: &str) -> bool {
1799    let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(content) else {
1800        return false;
1801    };
1802
1803    value
1804        .get("name")
1805        .and_then(|name| name.as_str())
1806        .is_some_and(|name| {
1807            !name.is_empty()
1808                && (value.get("args").is_some()
1809                    || value.get("arguments").is_some()
1810                    || value.get("parameters").is_some())
1811        })
1812}
1813
1814fn is_native_tool_call_json(content: &str) -> bool {
1815    let Ok(value) = serde_json::from_str::<serde_json::Value>(content) else {
1816        return false;
1817    };
1818
1819    let Some(tool_calls) = value.get("tool_calls").and_then(|calls| calls.as_array()) else {
1820        return false;
1821    };
1822
1823    !tool_calls.is_empty()
1824        && tool_calls.iter().all(|call| {
1825            call.get("function")
1826                .and_then(|function| function.get("name"))
1827                .and_then(|name| name.as_str())
1828                .or_else(|| call.get("name").and_then(|name| name.as_str()))
1829                .is_some()
1830        })
1831}
1832
1833fn rollback_orphan_user_turn(
1834    ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext,
1835    sender_key: &str,
1836    expected_content: &str,
1837) -> bool {
1838    let mut histories = ctx
1839        .conversation_histories
1840        .lock()
1841        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
1842    let Some(turns) = histories.get_mut(sender_key) else {
1843        return false;
1844    };
1845
1846    let should_pop = turns
1847        .last()
1848        .is_some_and(|turn| turn.role == "user" && turn.content == expected_content);
1849    if !should_pop {
1850        return false;
1851    }
1852
1853    turns.pop();
1854    if turns.is_empty() {
1855        histories.pop(sender_key);
1856    }
1857
1858    // Also remove the orphan turn from the persisted JSONL session store so
1859    // it doesn't resurface after a daemon restart (fixes #3674).
1860    if let Some(ref store) = ctx.session_store
1861        && let Err(e) = store.remove_last(sender_key)
1862    {
1863        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
1864            WARN,
1865            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
1866                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
1867                .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"error": format!("{}", e)})),
1868            "Failed to rollback session store entry"
1869        );
1870    }
1871
1872    true
1873}
1874
1875fn should_rollback_failed_user_turn(error: &anyhow::Error) -> bool {
1876    if error
1877        .downcast_ref::<zeroclaw_providers::ProviderCapabilityError>()
1878        .is_some_and(|capability| capability.capability.eq_ignore_ascii_case("vision"))
1879    {
1880        return true;
1881    }
1882
1883    zeroclaw_providers::reliable::is_non_retryable(error)
1884}
1885
1886fn should_skip_memory_context_entry(key: &str, content: &str) -> bool {
1887    if zeroclaw_memory::is_assistant_autosave_key(key) {
1888        return true;
1889    }
1890
1891    // Skip raw per-turn user messages: re-injecting them causes each
1892    // recalled entry to embed all prior generations, growing exponentially.
1893    // Consolidated knowledge is already promoted to Core/Daily entries.
1894    if zeroclaw_memory::is_user_autosave_key(key) {
1895        return true;
1896    }
1897
1898    if zeroclaw_memory::should_skip_autosave_content(content) {
1899        return true;
1900    }
1901
1902    if key.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().ends_with("_history") {
1903        return true;
1904    }
1905
1906    // Skip entries containing image markers to prevent duplication.
1907    // When auto_save stores a photo message to memory, a subsequent
1908    // memory recall on the same turn would surface the marker again,
1909    // causing two identical image blocks in the model_provider request.
1910    if content.contains("[IMAGE:") {
1911        return true;
1912    }
1913
1914    // Skip entries containing tool_result blocks. After a daemon restart
1915    // these can be recalled from SQLite and injected as memory context,
1916    // presenting the LLM with a `<tool_result>` without a preceding
1917    // `<tool_call>` and triggering hallucinated output.
1918    if content.contains("<tool_result") {
1919        return true;
1920    }
1921
1922    content.chars().count() > MEMORY_CONTEXT_MAX_CHARS
1923}
1924
1925fn is_context_window_overflow_error(err: &anyhow::Error) -> bool {
1926    let lower = err.to_string().to_lowercase();
1927    [
1928        "exceeds the context window",
1929        "context window of this model",
1930        "maximum context length",
1931        "context length exceeded",
1932        "too many tokens",
1933        "token limit exceeded",
1934        "prompt is too long",
1935        "input is too long",
1936    ]
1937    .iter()
1938    .any(|hint| lower.contains(hint))
1939}
1940
1941fn load_cached_model_preview(workspace_dir: &Path, provider_name: &str) -> Vec<String> {
1942    let cache_path = workspace_dir.join("state").join(MODEL_CACHE_FILE);
1943    let Ok(raw) = std::fs::read_to_string(cache_path) else {
1944        return Vec::new();
1945    };
1946    let Ok(state) = serde_json::from_str::<ModelCacheState>(&raw) else {
1947        return Vec::new();
1948    };
1949
1950    state
1951        .entries
1952        .into_iter()
1953        .find(|entry| entry.model_provider == provider_name)
1954        .map(|entry| {
1955            entry
1956                .models
1957                .into_iter()
1958                .take(MODEL_CACHE_PREVIEW_LIMIT)
1959                .collect::<Vec<_>>()
1960        })
1961        .unwrap_or_default()
1962}
1963
1964/// Build a cache key that includes the runtime-defaults generation, the
1965/// model_provider name, and, when a route-specific API key is supplied, a hash
1966/// of that key. Generation `0` is the immutable startup config, so its key shape
1967/// stays unchanged; hot-reload generations get isolated cache entries.
1968fn provider_cache_key(provider_name: &str, route_api_key: Option<&str>, generation: u64) -> String {
1969    let base = match route_api_key {
1970        Some(key) => {
1971            use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
1972            let mut hasher = std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
1973            key.hash(&mut hasher);
1974            format!("{provider_name}@{:x}", hasher.finish())
1975        }
1976        None => provider_name.to_string(),
1977    };
1978    if generation == 0 {
1979        base
1980    } else {
1981        format!("g{generation}:{base}")
1982    }
1983}
1984
1985/// Resolve a provider ref's own credentials strictly from its
1986/// `[providers.models.<type>.<alias>]` entry. No default/global fallback: a
1987/// provider only ever uses its own `api_key` / `uri`. Returns `(None, None)`
1988/// for a ref that does not parse or does not resolve, so the provider factory
1989/// surfaces the misconfiguration instead of silently borrowing another
1990/// provider's key.
1991fn provider_credentials_for_ref(
1992    config: &zeroclaw_config::schema::Config,
1993    provider_ref: &str,
1994) -> (Option<String>, Option<String>) {
1995    let Some((type_key, alias_key)) = provider_ref.trim().split_once('.') else {
1996        return (None, None);
1997    };
1998    config
1999        .providers
2000        .models
2001        .find(type_key, alias_key)
2002        .map_or((None, None), |entry| {
2003            (entry.api_key.clone(), entry.uri.clone())
2004        })
2005}
2006
2007async fn get_or_create_provider(
2008    ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext,
2009    provider_name: &str,
2010    route_api_key: Option<&str>,
2011    defaults_snapshot: &ChannelRuntimeDefaultsSnapshot,
2012) -> anyhow::Result<Arc<dyn ModelProvider>> {
2013    let cache_key = provider_cache_key(provider_name, route_api_key, defaults_snapshot.generation);
2014
2015    if let Some(existing) = ctx
2016        .provider_cache
2017        .lock()
2018        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
2019        .get(&cache_key)
2020        .cloned()
2021    {
2022        return Ok(existing);
2023    }
2024
2025    let config = Arc::clone(&defaults_snapshot.config);
2026    let defaults = defaults_snapshot.defaults.clone();
2027
2028    // Only return the pre-built startup default model_provider while the
2029    // current runtime defaults still match startup and there is no
2030    // route-specific credential override. Once config reload changes defaults,
2031    // the cache/store path above owns the live default provider.
2032    if route_api_key.is_none()
2033        && provider_name == defaults.default_model_provider.as_str()
2034        && provider_name == ctx.model_provider_ref.as_str()
2035        && !defaults_snapshot.hot
2036    {
2037        return Ok(Arc::clone(&ctx.model_provider));
2038    }
2039    // Resolve credentials and URL strictly from the requested provider's own
2040    // `[providers.models.<type>.<alias>]` entry. There is no global/default
2041    // fallback: a provider never inherits another provider's api_key or
2042    // api_url. An unresolved ref yields no credentials so the factory
2043    // surfaces the misconfiguration.
2044    let (entry_api_key, entry_api_url) =
2045        provider_credentials_for_ref(config.as_ref(), provider_name);
2046    let effective_api_key = route_api_key.map(ToString::to_string).or(entry_api_key);
2047
2048    let model_provider = create_resilient_model_provider_nonblocking(
2049        config,
2050        provider_name,
2051        effective_api_key,
2052        entry_api_url,
2053        defaults.reliability,
2054        ctx.provider_runtime_options.clone(),
2055    )
2056    .await?;
2057    let model_provider: Arc<dyn ModelProvider> = Arc::from(model_provider);
2058
2059    if let Err(err) = model_provider.warmup().await {
2060        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
2061            WARN,
2062            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
2063                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
2064                .with_attrs(
2065                    ::serde_json::json!({"model_provider": provider_name, "err": err.to_string()})
2066                ),
2067            "ModelProvider warmup failed"
2068        );
2069    }
2070
2071    let mut cache = ctx.provider_cache.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
2072    let cached = cache
2073        .entry(cache_key)
2074        .or_insert_with(|| Arc::clone(&model_provider));
2075    Ok(Arc::clone(cached))
2076}
2077
2078async fn create_resilient_model_provider_nonblocking(
2079    config: Arc<zeroclaw_config::schema::Config>,
2080    provider_name: &str,
2081    api_key: Option<String>,
2082    api_url: Option<String>,
2083    reliability: zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig,
2084    provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions,
2085) -> anyhow::Result<Box<dyn ModelProvider>> {
2086    let provider_name = provider_name.to_string();
2087    tokio::task::spawn_blocking(move || {
2088        let options = zeroclaw_providers::options_for_provider_ref(
2089            &config,
2090            &provider_name,
2091            &provider_runtime_options,
