7884    #[cfg(feature = "channel-webhook")]
7885    for (alias, wh) in &config.channels.webhook {
7886        if !active_channel_aliases.contains(&format!("webhook.{alias}")) {
7887            continue;
7888        }
7889        if !wh.enabled {
7890            continue;
7891        }
7892        channels.push(ConfiguredChannel {
7893            display_name: "Webhook",
7894            alias: Some(alias.clone()),
7895            channel: crate::paced_channel::PacedChannel::wrap(
7896                Arc::new(WebhookChannel::new(
7897                    alias.clone(),
7898                    wh.port,
7899                    wh.listen_path.clone(),
7900                    wh.send_url.clone(),
7901                    wh.send_method.clone(),
7902                    wh.auth_header.clone(),
7903                    wh.secret.clone(),
7904                    wh.max_retries,
7905                    wh.retry_base_delay_ms,
7906                    wh.retry_max_delay_ms,
7907                )),
7908                wh,
7909            ),
7910        });
7911    }
7912
7913    #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-webhook"))]
7914    if !config.channels.webhook.is_empty() {
7915        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
7916            WARN,
7917            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
7918                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown),
7919            "Webhook channel is configured but this build was compiled without \
7920             `channel-webhook`; skipping Webhook."
7921        );
7922    }
7923
7924    channels
7925}
7926
7927fn no_real_time_channels_message() -> &'static str {
7928    "No real-time channels configured. Run `zeroclaw quickstart` to set one up."
7929}
7930
7931/// Run health checks for configured channels.
7932pub async fn doctor_channels(config: Config) -> Result<()> {
7933    let config_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(config));
7934    #[allow(unused_mut)]
7935    let mut channels = collect_configured_channels(&config_arc, "health check", &[]);
7936
7937    #[cfg(feature = "channel-nostr")]
7938    {
7939        // Materialize the work list into owned values BEFORE any `.await`
7940        // so the RwLockReadGuard is dropped before the async constructor
7941        // runs (parking_lot guards are not Send).
7942        let nostr_jobs: Vec<(String, String, Vec<String>)> = {
7943            let config = config_arc.read();
7944            let active_nostr: std::collections::HashSet<String> = config
7945                .agents
7946                .values()
7947                .filter(|a| a.enabled)
7948                .flat_map(|a| a.channels.iter().map(|c| c.as_str().to_string()))
7949                .collect();
7950            config
7951                .channels
7952                .nostr
7953                .iter()
7954                .filter(|(alias, _)| active_nostr.contains(&format!("nostr.{alias}")))
7955                .map(|(alias, ns)| (alias.clone(), ns.private_key.clone(), ns.relays.clone()))
7956                .collect()
7957        };
7958        for (alias, private_key, relays) in nostr_jobs {
7959            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> = {
7960                let cfg_arc = config_arc.clone();
7961                let alias = alias.clone();
7962                Arc::new(move || cfg_arc.read().channel_external_peers("nostr", &alias))
7963            };
7964            channels.push(ConfiguredChannel {
7965                display_name: "Nostr",
7966                alias: Some(alias.clone()),
7967                channel: Arc::new(
7968                    NostrChannel::new(&private_key, relays, alias, peer_resolver).await?,
7969                ),
7970            });
7971        }
7972    }
7973
7974    #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-nostr"))]
7975    {
7976        let config = config_arc.read();
7977        if !config.channels.nostr.is_empty() {
7978            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
7979                WARN,
7980                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
7981                    .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown),
7982                "Nostr channel is configured but this build was compiled without \
7983                 `channel-nostr`; skipping Nostr health check."
7984            );
7985        }
7986    }
7987
7988    if channels.is_empty() {
7989        println!("{}", no_real_time_channels_message());
7990        return Ok(());
7991    }
7992
7993    println!("🩺 ZeroClaw Channel Doctor");
7994    println!();
7995
7996    let mut healthy = 0_u32;
7997    let mut unhealthy = 0_u32;
7998    let mut timeout = 0_u32;
7999
8000    for configured in channels {
8001        let result =
8002            tokio::time::timeout(Duration::from_secs(10), configured.channel.health_check()).await;
8003        let state = classify_health_result(&result);
8004
8005        match state {
8006            ChannelHealthState::Healthy => {
8007                healthy += 1;
8008                println!("  ✅ {:<9} healthy", configured.display_name);
8009            }
8010            ChannelHealthState::Unhealthy => {
8011                unhealthy += 1;
8012                println!(
8013                    "  ❌ {:<9} unhealthy (auth/config/network)",
8014                    configured.display_name
8015                );
8016            }
8017            ChannelHealthState::Timeout => {
8018                timeout += 1;
8019                println!("  ⏱️  {:<9} timed out (>10s)", configured.display_name);
8020            }
8021        }
8022    }
8023
8024    if !config_arc.read().channels.webhook.is_empty() {
8025        println!("  ℹ️  Webhook   check via `zeroclaw gateway` then GET /health");
8026    }
8027
8028    println!();
8029    println!("Summary: {healthy} healthy, {unhealthy} unhealthy, {timeout} timed out");
8030    Ok(())
8031}
8032
8033fn build_owner_by_channel_key(
8034    config: &Config,
8035    enabled_agents: &[String],
8036    collected_channel_keys: &[String],
8037) -> HashMap<String, String> {
8038    // Owner map: `<channel_type>.<alias>` (and bare `<channel_type>` for
8039    // backward-compat with cron callers / singleton channels) → agent_alias.
8040    // Built from each enabled agent's `agents.<alias>.channels` list — the
8041    // schema treats this as the source of truth for channel ownership.
8042    let mut owner_by_channel_key: HashMap<String, String> = HashMap::new();
8043    for alias_str in enabled_agents {
8044        let Some(agent_cfg) = config.agents.get(alias_str) else {
8045            debug_assert!(
8046                false,
8047                "enabled agent alias missing from config.agents: {}",
8048                alias_str
8049            );
8050            continue;
8051        };
8052        for ch in &agent_cfg.channels {
8053            let ch_str: &str = ch.as_ref();
8054            owner_by_channel_key.insert(ch_str.to_string(), alias_str.clone());
8055            if let Some((bare, _)) = ch_str.split_once('.') {
8056                owner_by_channel_key
8057                    .entry(bare.to_string())
8058                    .or_insert_with(|| alias_str.clone());
8059            }
8060        }
8061    }
8062
8063    // Legacy fallback mode: when no enabled agent declares channel bindings,
8064    // channel collection accepts all enabled channels. Those channels must
8065    // also be routable, so bind collected channel keys to the runtime-active
8066    // agent selection (explicit `"default"` alias when present, else
8067    // lexicographically-smallest enabled alias).
8068    // `owner_by_channel_key.is_empty()` means every enabled agent had an
8069    // empty `agents.<alias>.channels` list; this is the same "legacy mode"
8070    // signal used by `collect_configured_channels` to accept all enabled
8071    // channel blocks.
8072    if owner_by_channel_key.is_empty() && !collected_channel_keys.is_empty() {
8073        let fallback_owner = config
8074            .resolved_runtime_agent_alias()
8075            .filter(|alias| enabled_agents.iter().any(|enabled| enabled == *alias))
8076            .map(ToString::to_string)
8077            .or_else(|| enabled_agents.first().cloned());
8078
8079        if let Some(owner_alias) = fallback_owner {
8080            for channel_key in collected_channel_keys {
8081                owner_by_channel_key.insert(channel_key.clone(), owner_alias.clone());
8082                if let Some((bare, _)) = channel_key.split_once('.') {
8083                    owner_by_channel_key
8084                        .entry(bare.to_string())
8085                        .or_insert_with(|| owner_alias.clone());
8086                }
8087            }
8088        }
8089    }
8090
8091    owner_by_channel_key
8092}
8093
8094/// Start all configured channels and route messages to the agent
8095#[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)]
8096pub async fn start_channels(
8097    config: Config,
8098    canvas_store: Option<zeroclaw_runtime::tools::CanvasStore>,
8099    cancel: tokio_util::sync::CancellationToken,
8100) -> Result<()> {
8101    // Wrap into the canonical shared handle so channels and persistence
8102    // paths share one source of truth. The local `config` shadowing
8103    // keeps this function's body (which threads `config` through dozens
8104    // of sync reads and awaits) compatible with the old `Config` shape
8105    // via a one-time clone; channels themselves consult `config_arc`.
8106    let config_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(config));
8107    let config: Config = config_arc.read().clone();
8108    // No agent's model provider resolves yet — the user has channels
8109    // configured but hasn't finished onboarding their model_provider.
8110    // Returning Ok() here lets the daemon supervisor mark the channels
8111    // component "done" instead of restart-looping. The user completes
8112    // onboarding at /onboard and reloads via /admin/reload to bring channels
8113    // up. Resolution is strict: an enabled agent counts only if its mandatory
8114    // `<type>.<alias>` ref resolves to a configured entry with a `model`.
8115    let any_agent_provider_resolves = config
8116        .agents
8117        .iter()
8118        .filter(|(_, a)| a.enabled)
8119        .any(|(_, a)| runtime_defaults_from_config(&config, a.model_provider.as_str()).is_ok());
8120    if !any_agent_provider_resolves {
8121        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8122            WARN,
8123            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8124                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown),
8125            "Channels supervisor: no model configured. Waiting for reload \
8126             (complete onboarding at /onboard or set \
8127             [providers.models.<type>.<alias>] model = \"...\" and reload)."
8128        );
8129        cancel.cancelled().await;
8130        return Ok(());
8131    }
8132
8133    // Every `[channels.<type>.<alias>]` block is owned by exactly one agent
8134    // (declared via `agents.<alias>.channels = [...]`). One
8135    // `ChannelRuntimeContext` per enabled agent; `AgentRouter::multi` resolves
8136    // each inbound message to the owning agent. Discord/Telegram/Slack/etc.
8137    // sockets stay shared at the channel layer.
8138    let enabled_agents: Vec<String> = {
8139        let mut v: Vec<String> = config
8140            .agents
8141            .iter()
8142            .filter(|(_, a)| a.enabled)
8143            .map(|(alias, _)| alias.clone())
8144            .collect();
8145        if v.is_empty() {
8146            anyhow::bail!("start_channels requires at least one enabled [agents.<alias>] entry");
8147        }
8148        v.sort();
8149        v
8150    };
8151
8152    let observer: Arc<dyn Observer> =
8153        Arc::from(observability::create_observer(&config.observability));
8154    let runtime: Arc<dyn platform::RuntimeAdapter> =
8155        Arc::from(platform::create_runtime(&config.runtime)?);
8156
8157    // i18n is process-global; initialize once before the per-agent loop
8158    // touches tool descriptions.
8159    let i18n_locale = config
8160        .locale
8161        .as_deref()
8162        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
8163        .map(ToString::to_string)
8164        .unwrap_or_else(zeroclaw_runtime::i18n::detect_locale);
8165    zeroclaw_runtime::i18n::init(&i18n_locale);
8166
8167    // Single session backend shared across agents — they're scoped by
8168    // `session_key` (which already encodes `<channel_type>.<alias>`), so
8169    // multiple agent ctxs reading the same backend never overlap.
8170    let shared_session_store: Option<Arc<dyn zeroclaw_infra::session_backend::SessionBackend>> =
8171        if config.channels.session_persistence {
8172            match zeroclaw_infra::make_session_backend(
8173                &config.data_dir,
8174                &config.channels.session_backend,
8175            ) {
8176                Ok(backend) => {
8177                    ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8178                        INFO,
8179                        ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note),
8180                        &format!(
8181                            "📂 Session persistence enabled (backend: {})",
8182                            config.channels.session_backend
8183                        )
8184                    );
8185                    Some(backend)
8186                }
8187                Err(e) => {
8188                    ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8189                        WARN,
8190                        ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8191                            .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
8192                            .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"error": format!("{}", e)})),
8193                        "Session persistence disabled"
8194                    );
8195                    None
8196                }
8197            }
8198        } else {
8199            None
8200        };
8201
8202    // Channel infrastructure (listeners, `channels_by_name`, the mpsc bus)
8203    // is built once inside the loop on the first iteration — the primary
8204    // agent's `tool_specs` are used to wire Telegram slash commands.
8205    // Subsequent iterations reuse `channels_by_name_shared` to populate
8206    // their tool handles and to seed their `ChannelRuntimeContext`.
8207    let mut channels_by_name_shared: Option<Arc<HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>>>> = None;
8208    let mut collected_channel_keys: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
8209    let mut max_in_flight_messages: Option<usize> = None;
8210    let mut listener_handles: Vec<tokio::task::JoinHandle<()>> = Vec::new();
8211    let mut rx_holder: Option<tokio::sync::mpsc::Receiver<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>> =
8212        None;
8213
8214    let mut agent_ctxs: HashMap<String, Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext>> = HashMap::new();
8215
8216    for agent_alias in &enabled_agents {
8217        let agent = config
8218            .resolved_agent_config(agent_alias)
8219            .with_context(|| format!("agents.{agent_alias} is not configured"))?;
8220        let risk_profile = config
8221            .risk_profile_for_agent(agent_alias)
8222            .with_context(|| {
8223                format!(
8224                    "agents.{agent_alias}.risk_profile does not name a configured risk_profiles entry"
8225                )
8226            })?
8227            .clone();
8228
8229        // Resolve the agent's model provider strictly from its mandatory
8230        // `<type>.<alias>` reference. No fallback to a first/default provider:
8231        // an agent whose ref does not resolve to a configured entry with a
8232        // `model` is rejected here.
8233        let runtime_defaults = runtime_defaults_from_config(&config, agent.model_provider.as_str())
8234            .with_context(|| format!("agents.{agent_alias}.model_provider"))?;
8235        let provider_name = runtime_defaults.default_model_provider.clone();
8236        let model = runtime_defaults.model.clone();
8237        let temperature = runtime_defaults.temperature;
8238        let provider_api_key = runtime_defaults.api_key.clone();
8239        let provider_api_url = runtime_defaults.api_url.clone();
8240        let provider_reliability = runtime_defaults.reliability.clone();
8241        let provider_runtime_options =
8242            zeroclaw_providers::provider_runtime_options_for_agent(&config, agent_alias);
8243        let model_provider: Arc<dyn ModelProvider> = Arc::from(
8244            create_resilient_model_provider_nonblocking(
8245                Arc::new(config.clone()),
8246                &provider_name,
8247                provider_api_key.clone(),
8248                provider_api_url.clone(),
8249                provider_reliability.clone(),
8250                provider_runtime_options.clone(),
8251            )
8252            .await?,
8253        );
8254
8255        if let Err(e) = model_provider.warmup().await {
8256            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8257                WARN,
8258                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8259                    .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown)
8260                    .with_attrs(
8261                        ::serde_json::json!({"error": format!("{}", e), "agent": agent_alias})
8262                    ),
8263                "ModelProvider warmup failed (non-fatal)"
8264            );
8265        }
8266
8267        let security = Arc::new(SecurityPolicy::for_agent(&config, agent_alias)?);
8268        let mem: Arc<dyn Memory> = zeroclaw_memory::create_memory_for_agent(
8269            &config,
8270            agent_alias,
8271            provider_api_key.as_deref(),
8272        )
8273        .await?;
8274        let (composio_key, composio_entity_id) = if config.composio.enabled {
8275            (
8276                config.composio.api_key.as_deref(),
8277                Some(config.composio.entity_id.as_str()),
8278            )
8279        } else {
8280            (None, None)
8281        };
8282
8283        // Per-agent workspace: `<install>/agents/<alias>/workspace/`. Holds
8284        // this agent's IDENTITY.md / SOUL.md / USER.md / TOOLS.md /
8285        // AGENTS.md / MEMORY.md — the personality files the gateway UI
8286        // edits via /config/agents/<alias>?tab=personality. The system
8287        // prompt builder below reads these to render the agent's voice;
8288        // file_read / file_write tools scope path access to this root.
8289        let workspace = config.agent_workspace_dir(agent_alias);
8290        // Per-agent skills: install-wide workspace + open_skills set,
8291        // unioned with this agent's declared `skill_bundles`.
8292        let skills =
8293            zeroclaw_runtime::skills::load_skills_for_agent(&workspace, &config, agent_alias);
8294
8295        let all_tools_result_ch = tools::all_tools_with_runtime(
8296            Arc::new(config.clone()),
8297            &security,
8298            &risk_profile,
8299            agent_alias,
8300            Arc::clone(&runtime),
8301            Arc::clone(&mem),
8302            composio_key,
8303            composio_entity_id,
8304            &config.browser,
8305            &config.http_request,
8306            &config.web_fetch,
8307            &workspace,
8308            &config.agents,
8309            provider_api_key.as_deref(),
8310            &config,
8311            canvas_store.clone(),
8312            false,
8313            None,
8314        );
8315        let mut built_tools = all_tools_result_ch.tools;
8316        let delegate_handle_ch = all_tools_result_ch.delegate_handle;
8317
8318        // Wire peripheral tools (gpio_read/gpio_write etc.) so channel-driven
8319        // sessions (Telegram, Discord, etc.) can actuate hardware when
8320        // [peripherals] is configured. Mirrors the agent loop wiring.
8321        // The helper is safe to call unconditionally (returns empty when
8322        // no peripherals are wired).
8323        let peripheral_tools =
8324            zeroclaw_runtime::agent::loop_::load_peripheral_tools(config.peripherals.clone()).await;
8325        if !peripheral_tools.is_empty() {
8326            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8327                INFO,
8328                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8329                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"count": peripheral_tools.len()})),
8330                "Peripheral tools added (channels orchestrator)"
8331            );
8332            built_tools.extend(peripheral_tools);
8333        }
8334        let reaction_handle_ch = all_tools_result_ch.reaction_handle;
8335        let ask_user_handle_ch = all_tools_result_ch.ask_user_handle;
8336        let poll_handle_ch = all_tools_result_ch.poll_handle;
8337        let escalate_handle_ch = all_tools_result_ch.escalate_handle;
8338
8339        // ── Built-in SecurityPolicy tool gate (parity with agent::run /
8340        // process_message / from_config) ────────────────────────────────────
8341        // Apply the agent's allowlist (`allowed_tools`) AND denylist
8342        // (`excluded_tools`) to the eager built-in registry, BEFORE MCP and
8343        // skill tools are added. `start_channels` previously enforced only the
8344        // risk-profile denylist on the prompt catalog here — never the
8345        // per-agent allowlist on the registry sent to the model — so an agent
8346        // allowlisted to e.g. `file_read` still received raw `shell` /
8347        // `file_write` in its native tool specs. Filtering before skill
8348        // registration is also what lets a scoped elevation wrapper survive:
8349        // the raw target is removed while the distinct prefixed
8350        // `{skill}__{tool}` wrapper is appended later. MCP tools are injected
8351        // after this gate and are intentionally exempt (a restrictive allowlist
8352        // must not silently drop a server's tools); the risk-profile denylist
8353        // still applies to them.
8354        let before_policy_filter_ch = built_tools.len();
8355        apply_policy_tool_filter(&mut built_tools, Some(security.as_ref()), None);
8356        if built_tools.len() != before_policy_filter_ch {
8357            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8358                INFO,
8359                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8360                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({
8361                        "agent": agent_alias,
8362                        "before": before_policy_filter_ch,
8363                        "retained": built_tools.len(),
8364                        "policy_allowed": security.allowed_tools.as_ref().map(|v| v.len()),
8365                        "policy_excluded": security.excluded_tools.as_ref().map(|v| v.len()),
8366                    })),
8367                "Applied SecurityPolicy built-in tool filter (channel path)"
8368            );
8369        }
8370
8371        // Wire MCP tools into the per-agent registry before freezing —
8372        // non-fatal. When `mcp.deferred_loading` is enabled, MCP tools are
8373        // exposed via a `tool_search` built-in rather than added eagerly.
8374        let mut deferred_section = String::new();
8375        let mut ch_activated_handle: Option<
8376            std::sync::Arc<std::sync::Mutex<zeroclaw_runtime::tools::ActivatedToolSet>>,
8377        > = None;
8378        // Resolution-only MCP wrappers for skill MCP elevation (kind = "mcp").
8379        let mut ch_mcp_elevation_arcs: Vec<std::sync::Arc<dyn zeroclaw_runtime::tools::Tool>> =
8380            Vec::new();
8381        if config.mcp.enabled && !config.mcp.servers.is_empty() {
8382            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8383                INFO,
8384                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8385                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"agent": agent_alias})),
8386                &format!(
8387                    "Initializing MCP client — {} server(s) configured",
8388                    config.mcp.servers.len()
8389                )
8390            );
8391            match zeroclaw_runtime::tools::McpRegistry::connect_all(&config.mcp.servers).await {
8392                Ok(registry) => {
8393                    let registry = std::sync::Arc::new(registry);
8394                    ch_mcp_elevation_arcs =
8395                        zeroclaw_runtime::tools::collect_mcp_elevation_arcs(&registry).await;
8396                    if config.mcp.deferred_loading {
8397                        let deferred_set =
8398                            zeroclaw_runtime::tools::DeferredMcpToolSet::from_registry(
8399                                std::sync::Arc::clone(&registry),
8400                            )
8401                            .await;
8402                        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8403                            INFO,
8404                            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(
8405                                module_path!(),
8406                                ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note
8407                            )
8408                            .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"agent": agent_alias})),
8409                            &format!(
8410                                "MCP deferred: {} tool stub(s) from {} server(s)",
8411                                deferred_set.len(),
8412                                registry.server_count()
8413                            )
8414                        );
8415                        deferred_section =
8416                            zeroclaw_runtime::tools::build_deferred_tools_section(&deferred_set);
8417                        let activated = std::sync::Arc::new(std::sync::Mutex::new(
8418                            zeroclaw_runtime::tools::ActivatedToolSet::new(),
8419                        ));
8420                        ch_activated_handle = Some(std::sync::Arc::clone(&activated));
8421                        built_tools.push(Box::new(zeroclaw_runtime::tools::ToolSearchTool::new(
8422                            deferred_set,
8423                            activated,
8424                        )));
8425                    } else {
8426                        let names = registry.tool_names();
8427                        let mut registered = 0usize;
8428                        for name in names {
8429                            if let Some(def) = registry.get_tool_def(&name).await {
8430                                let wrapper: std::sync::Arc<dyn Tool> = std::sync::Arc::new(
8431                                    zeroclaw_runtime::tools::McpToolWrapper::new(
8432                                        name,
8433                                        def,
8434                                        std::sync::Arc::clone(&registry),
8435                                    ),
8436                                );
8437                                if let Some(ref handle) = delegate_handle_ch {
8438                                    handle.write().push(std::sync::Arc::clone(&wrapper));
8439                                }
8440                                built_tools
8441                                    .push(Box::new(zeroclaw_runtime::tools::ArcToolRef(wrapper)));
8442                                registered += 1;
8443                            }
8444                        }
8445                        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8446                            INFO,
8447                            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(
8448                                module_path!(),
8449                                ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note
8450                            )
8451                            .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"agent": agent_alias})),
8452                            &format!(
8453                                "MCP: {} tool(s) registered from {} server(s)",
8454                                registered,
8455                                registry.server_count()
8456                            )
8457                        );
8458                    }
8459                }
8460                Err(e) => {
8461                    ::zeroclaw_log::record!(ERROR, ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Fail).with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Failure).with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"agent_alias": agent_alias, "error": format!("{}", e)})), "MCP registry failed to initialize");
8462                }
8463            }
8464        }
8465
8466        // Skill tools share the workspace-loaded `skills` Vec but each
8467        // agent gets its own `ToolBox` so per-agent security policies
8468        // gate execution.
8469        // Resolution registry = built-in arcs + resolution-only MCP wrappers.
8470        let skill_resolution_registry: Vec<std::sync::Arc<dyn zeroclaw_runtime::tools::Tool>> =
8471            all_tools_result_ch
8472                .unfiltered_tool_arcs
8473                .iter()
8474                .cloned()
8475                .chain(ch_mcp_elevation_arcs.iter().cloned())
8476                .collect();
8477        zeroclaw_runtime::tools::register_skill_tools_with_context(
8478            &mut built_tools,
8479            &skills,
8480            security.clone(),
8481            &skill_resolution_registry,
8482        );
8483
8484        let tool_specs: Vec<(String, String)> = built_tools
8485            .iter()
8486            .map(|t| (t.name().to_string(), t.description().to_string()))
8487            .collect();
8488
8489        let tools_registry = Arc::new(built_tools);
8490
8491        let mut tool_descs: Vec<(&str, &str)> = vec![
8492            (
8493                "shell",
8494                "Execute terminal commands. Use when: running local checks, build/test commands, diagnostics. Don't use when: a safer dedicated tool exists, or command is destructive without approval.",
8495            ),
8496            (
8497                "file_read",
8498                "Read file contents. Use when: inspecting project files, configs, logs. Don't use when: a targeted search is enough.",
8499            ),
8500            (
8501                "file_write",
8502                "Write file contents. Use when: applying focused edits, scaffolding files, updating docs/code. Don't use when: side effects are unclear or file ownership is uncertain.",
8503            ),
8504            (
8505                "memory_store",
8506                "Save to memory. Use when: preserving durable preferences, decisions, key context. Don't use when: information is transient/noisy/sensitive without need.",
8507            ),
8508            (
8509                "memory_recall",
8510                "Search memory. Use when: retrieving prior decisions, user preferences, historical context. Don't use when: answer is already in current context.",
8511            ),
8512            (
8513                "memory_forget",
8514                "Delete a memory entry. Use when: memory is incorrect/stale or explicitly requested for removal. Don't use when: impact is uncertain.",
8515            ),
8516        ];
8517
8518        if matches!(
8519            config.skills.prompt_injection_mode,
8520            zeroclaw_config::schema::SkillsPromptInjectionMode::Compact
8521        ) {
8522            tool_descs.push((
8523                "read_skill",
8524                "Load the full source for an available skill by name. Use when: compact mode only shows a summary and you need the complete skill instructions.",
8525            ));
8526        }
8527        if config.browser.enabled {
8528            tool_descs.push((
8529                "browser_open",
8530                "Open approved HTTPS URLs in system browser (allowlist-only, no scraping)",
8531            ));
8532        }
8533        if config.composio.enabled {
8534            tool_descs.push((
8535                "composio",
8536                "Execute actions on 1000+ apps via Composio (Gmail, Notion, GitHub, Slack, etc.). Use action='list' to discover actions, 'list_accounts' to retrieve connected account IDs, 'execute' to run (optionally with connected_account_id), and 'connect' for OAuth.",
8537            ));
8538        }
8539        tool_descs.push((
8540            "schedule",
8541            "Manage scheduled tasks (create/list/get/cancel/pause/resume). Supports recurring cron and one-shot delays.",
8542        ));
8543        tool_descs.push((
8544            "pushover",
8545            "Send a Pushover notification to your device. Requires PUSHOVER_TOKEN and PUSHOVER_USER_KEY in .env file.",
8546        ));
8547        if !config.agents.is_empty() {
8548            tool_descs.push((
8549                "delegate",
8550                "Delegate a subtask to a specialized agent. Use when: a task benefits from a different model (e.g. fast summarization, deep reasoning, code generation). The sub-agent runs a single prompt and returns its response.",
8551            ));
8552        }
8553
8554        // Filter out tools excluded for non-CLI channels so this agent's
8555        // system prompt does not advertise them for channel-driven runs.
8556        {
8557            let active_profile = &risk_profile;
8558            let excluded = &active_profile.excluded_tools;
8559            if !excluded.is_empty() && active_profile.level != AutonomyLevel::Full {
8560                tool_descs.retain(|(name, _)| !excluded.iter().any(|ex| ex == name));
8561            }
8562        }
8563        let effective_tool_names: HashSet<&str> =
8564            tools_registry.iter().map(|tool| tool.name()).collect();
8565        tool_descs.retain(|(name, _)| effective_tool_names.contains(name));
8566
8567        let bootstrap_max_chars = if agent.resolved.compact_context {
8568            Some(6000)
8569        } else {
8570            None
8571        };
8572        let native_tools = model_provider.supports_native_tools();
8573        let expose_text_tool_protocol = apply_text_tool_prompt_policy(
8574            native_tools,
8575            agent.resolved.strict_tool_parsing,
8576            &mut tool_descs,
8577            &mut deferred_section,
8578        );
8579        let mut system_prompt = build_system_prompt_with_mode_and_autonomy(
8580            &workspace,
8581            &model,
8582            &tool_descs,
8583            &skills,
8584            Some(&agent.identity),
8585            bootstrap_max_chars,
8586            Some(&risk_profile),
8587            native_tools,
8588            config.skills.prompt_injection_mode,
8589            agent.resolved.compact_context,
8590            agent.resolved.max_system_prompt_chars,
8591            true,
8592        );
8593        if expose_text_tool_protocol {
8594            system_prompt.push_str(&build_tool_instructions_for_names(
8595                tools_registry.as_ref(),
8596                &effective_tool_names,
8597            ));
8598        }
8599        if !deferred_section.is_empty() {
8600            system_prompt.push('\n');
8601            system_prompt.push_str(&deferred_section);
8602        }
8603        if agent.resolved.tool_receipts.enabled && agent.resolved.tool_receipts.inject_system_prompt
8604        {
8605            system_prompt.push_str(
8606                "\n## Tool Execution Receipts\n\n\
8607                 Every tool result includes a `[receipt: ...]` field. This is a cryptographic \
8608                 signature proving the tool actually executed. You must include the receipt \
8609                 verbatim when referencing tool results. Do not modify, omit, or fabricate receipts. \
8610                 A missing or invalid receipt indicates a fabricated tool call.\n\n",
8611            );
8612        }
8613
8614        // === First iteration only: set up shared channel infrastructure ===
8615        //
8616        // We collect channels here (using *this* agent's `tool_specs`, since
8617        // the loop puts the primary agent first) and stash the
8618        // `channels_by_name` registry so subsequent iterations can populate
8619        // their tool handles without re-building Discord/Telegram/etc.
8620        // sockets. The first agent's `tool_specs` wire Telegram-style slash
8621        // commands; multi-agent installs that want per-bot command sets
8622        // require a future per-channel `tool_specs` lookup (tracked
8623        // alongside the per-channel ChannelRuntimeContext follow-up).
8624        if channels_by_name_shared.is_none() {
8625            if !skills.is_empty() {
8626                println!(
8627                    "  🧩 Skills:   {}",
8628                    skills
8629                        .iter()
8630                        .map(|s| s.name.as_str())
8631                        .collect::<Vec<_>>()
8632                        .join(", ")
8633                );
8634            }
8635
8636            #[allow(unused_mut)]
8637            let mut configured_channels: Vec<ConfiguredChannel> =
8638                collect_configured_channels(&config_arc, "runtime startup", &tool_specs);
8639
8640            #[cfg(feature = "channel-nostr")]
8641            if let Some((alias, ns)) = config.channels.nostr.iter().next() {
8642                let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> = {
8643                    let cfg_arc = config_arc.clone();
8644                    let alias = alias.clone();
8645                    Arc::new(move || cfg_arc.read().channel_external_peers("nostr", &alias))
8646                };
8647                configured_channels.push(ConfiguredChannel {
8648                    display_name: "Nostr",
8649                    alias: Some(alias.clone()),
8650                    channel: Arc::new(
8651                        NostrChannel::new(
8652                            &ns.private_key,
8653                            ns.relays.clone(),
8654                            alias.clone(),
8655                            peer_resolver,
8656                        )
8657                        .await?,
8658                    ),
8659                });
8660            }
8661            #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-nostr"))]
8662            if !config.channels.nostr.is_empty() {
8663                ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8664                    WARN,
8665                    ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8666                        .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown),
8667                    "Nostr channel is configured but this build was compiled without \
8668                     `channel-nostr`; skipping Nostr."
8669                );
8670            }
8671            let channels: Vec<Arc<dyn Channel>> = configured_channels
8672                .iter()
8673                .map(|cc| Arc::clone(&cc.channel))
8674                .collect();
8675            if channels.is_empty() {
8676                ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8677                    INFO,
8678                    ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note),
8679                    "No active channels to supervise (none configured or all disabled). \
8680                     Waiting for reload signal."
8681                );
8682                cancel.cancelled().await;
8683                return Ok(());
8684            }
8685
8686            println!("🦀 ZeroClaw Channel Server");
8687            println!("  🤖 Model:    {model} (agent: {agent_alias})");
8688            let effective_backend = config.resolve_active_storage().kind();
8689            println!(
8690                "  🧠 Memory:   {} (auto-save: {})",
8691                effective_backend,
8692                if config.memory.auto_save { "on" } else { "off" }
8693            );
8694            let channel_labels: Vec<String> = configured_channels
8695                .iter()
8696                .map(|cc| composite_channel_key(cc.channel.name(), cc.alias.as_deref()))
8697                .collect();
8698            collected_channel_keys = channel_labels.clone();
8699            println!("  📡 Channels: {}", channel_labels.join(", "));
8700            println!("  🤖 Agents:   {}", enabled_agents.join(", "));
8701            println!();
8702            println!("  Listening for messages... (Ctrl+C to stop)");
8703            println!();
8704
8705            zeroclaw_runtime::health::mark_component_ok("channels");
8706
8707            let initial_backoff_secs = config
8708                .reliability
8709                .channel_initial_backoff_secs
8710                .max(DEFAULT_CHANNEL_INITIAL_BACKOFF_SECS);
8711            let max_backoff_secs = config
8712                .reliability
8713                .channel_max_backoff_secs
8714                .max(DEFAULT_CHANNEL_MAX_BACKOFF_SECS);
8715
8716            let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>(100);
8717
8718            for cc in &configured_channels {
8719                listener_handles.push(spawn_supervised_listener(
8720                    cc.channel.clone(),
8721                    cc.alias.clone(),
8722                    tx.clone(),
8723                    initial_backoff_secs,
8724                    max_backoff_secs,
8725                    cancel.clone(),
8726                ));
8727            }
8728            drop(tx);
8729
8730            // Composite-key registry (see `composite_channel_key`).
8731            let cbn = Arc::new({
8732                let mut map: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
8733                let mut name_counts: HashMap<&str, usize> = HashMap::new();
8734                for cc in &configured_channels {
8735                    *name_counts.entry(cc.channel.name()).or_insert(0) += 1;
8736                }
8737                for cc in &configured_channels {
8738                    let name = cc.channel.name();
8739                    let composite = composite_channel_key(name, cc.alias.as_deref());
8740                    map.insert(composite, Arc::clone(&cc.channel));
8741                    if name_counts.get(name).copied().unwrap_or(0) == 1 {
8742                        map.entry(name.to_string())
8743                            .or_insert_with(|| Arc::clone(&cc.channel));
8744                    }
8745                }
8746                map
8747            });
8748            *CRON_CHANNEL_REGISTRY
8749                .write()
8750                .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Some(Arc::clone(&cbn));
8751
8752            let in_flight = max_in_flight_messages_for_config(channels.len(), &config.channels);
8753            println!("  🚦 In-flight message limit: {in_flight}");
8754
8755            max_in_flight_messages = Some(in_flight);
8756            channels_by_name_shared = Some(cbn);
8757            rx_holder = Some(rx);
8758        }
8759
8760        let channels_by_name = Arc::clone(
8761            channels_by_name_shared
8762                .as_ref()
8763                .expect("channels_by_name initialized on first iteration"),
8764        );
8765
8766        // Wire this agent's reaction / ask_user / escalate tool handles
8767        // into the shared `channels_by_name` map.
8768        {
8769            let mut map = reaction_handle_ch.write();
8770            for (name, ch) in channels_by_name.as_ref() {
8771                map.insert(name.clone(), Arc::clone(ch));
8772            }
8773        }
8774        if let Some(ref handle) = ask_user_handle_ch {
8775            let mut map = handle.write();
8776            for (name, ch) in channels_by_name.as_ref() {
8777                map.insert(name.clone(), Arc::clone(ch));
8778            }
8779        }
8780        if let Some(ref handle) = poll_handle_ch {
8781            let mut map = handle.write();
8782            for (name, ch) in channels_by_name.as_ref() {
8783                map.insert(name.clone(), Arc::clone(ch));
8784            }
8785        }
8786        if let Some(ref handle) = escalate_handle_ch {
8787            let mut map = handle.write();
8788            for (name, ch) in channels_by_name.as_ref() {
8789                map.insert(name.clone(), Arc::clone(ch));
8790            }
8791        }
8792
8793        let mut provider_cache_seed: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn ModelProvider>> = HashMap::new();
8794        provider_cache_seed.insert(provider_name.clone(), Arc::clone(&model_provider));
8795        let message_timeout_secs =
8796            effective_channel_message_timeout_secs(config.channels.message_timeout_secs);
8797        let interrupt_on_new_message = interrupt_on_new_message_config(&config.channels);
8798
8799        let memory_strategy: Arc<dyn MemoryStrategy> = Arc::new(
8800            zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
8801                Arc::clone(&mem),
8802                config.memory.clone(),
8803                config.data_dir.clone(),
8804            ),
8805        );
8806
8807        let runtime_ctx = Arc::new(ChannelRuntimeContext {
8808            channels_by_name: Arc::clone(&channels_by_name),
8809            model_provider: Arc::clone(&model_provider),
8810            model_provider_ref: Arc::new(provider_name.clone()),
8811            agent_alias: Arc::new(agent_alias.clone()),
8812            agent_cfg: Arc::new(agent.clone()),
8813            prompt_config: Arc::new(config.clone()),
8814            memory: Arc::clone(&mem),
8815            memory_strategy,
8816            tools_registry: Arc::clone(&tools_registry),
8817            observer: Arc::clone(&observer),
8818            system_prompt: Arc::new(system_prompt),
8819            model: Arc::new(model.clone()),
8820            temperature,
8821            auto_save_memory: config.memory.auto_save,
8822            max_tool_iterations: config.effective_max_tool_iterations(agent_alias.as_str()),
8823            min_relevance_score: config.memory.min_relevance_score,
8824            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(lru::LruCache::new(
8825                std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap(),
8826            ))),
8827            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
8828            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(provider_cache_seed)),
8829            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
8830            reliability: Arc::new(config.reliability.clone()),
8831            provider_runtime_options,
8832            // Use this agent's workspace (not the install-wide data dir): the
8833            // channel runtime context drives per-message skill reloads, prompt
8834            // refresh, and file-access scoping, all of which must resolve to the
8835            // same agent workspace that boot-time registration loads from.
8836            // Pointing at `config.data_dir` silently breaks per-message skill
8837            // activation (candidates load from `<data_dir>/skills`, which is
8838            // empty) and mis-scopes file tools.
8839            workspace_dir: Arc::new(workspace.clone()),
8840            message_timeout_secs,
8841            interrupt_on_new_message,
8842            multimodal: config.multimodal.clone(),
8843            media_pipeline: config.media_pipeline.clone(),
8844            transcription_config: config.transcription.clone(),
8845            agent_transcription_provider: agent.transcription_provider.as_str().to_string(),
8846            hooks: if config.hooks.enabled {
8847                let mut runner = zeroclaw_runtime::hooks::HookRunner::new();
8848                if config.hooks.builtin.command_logger {
8849                    runner.register(Box::new(
8850                        zeroclaw_runtime::hooks::builtin::CommandLoggerHook::new(),
8851                    ));
8852                }
8853                if config.hooks.builtin.webhook_audit.enabled {
8854                    runner.register(Box::new(
8855                        zeroclaw_runtime::hooks::builtin::WebhookAuditHook::new(
8856                            config.hooks.builtin.webhook_audit.clone(),
8857                        ),
8858                    ));
8859                }
8860                Some(Arc::new(runner))
8861            } else {
8862                None
8863            },
8864            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(risk_profile.excluded_tools.clone()),
8865            autonomy_level: risk_profile.level,
8866            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(agent.resolved.tool_call_dedup_exempt.clone()),
8867            model_routes: Arc::new(config.model_routes.clone()),
8868            query_classification: config.query_classification.clone(),
8869            ack_reactions: config.channels.ack_reactions,
8870            show_tool_calls: config.channels.show_tool_calls,
8871            session_store: shared_session_store.clone(),
8872            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(&risk_profile)),
8873            activated_tools: ch_activated_handle,
8874            cost_tracking: zeroclaw_runtime::cost::CostTracker::get_or_init_global(
8875                config.cost.clone(),
8876                &config.data_dir,
8877            )
8878            .map(|tracker| {
8879                // The cost tracker's lookup site (`record_tool_loop_cost_usage`
8880                // in zeroclaw-runtime) receives the bare provider type — the
8881                // composite alias isn't threaded through the agent loop. Build
8882                // the pricing map keyed by `<type>` and merge each alias's
8883                // `pricing` table into the type-level slot. Rates are per
8884                // (provider type, model); they don't differ between an
8885                // operator's `anthropic.work` and `anthropic.personal` keys.
8886                let mut by_type: std::collections::HashMap<
8887                    String,
8888                    std::collections::HashMap<String, f64>,
8889                > = std::collections::HashMap::new();
8890                for (type_k, _alias_k, profile) in config.providers.models.iter_entries() {
8891                    if profile.pricing.is_empty() {
8892                        continue;
8893                    }
8894                    let slot = by_type.entry(type_k.to_string()).or_default();
8895                    for (key, value) in &profile.pricing {
8896                        slot.insert(key.clone(), *value);
8897                    }
8898                }
8899                // Merge the `[cost.rates.providers.models.<type>.<model>]`
8900                // section. Keys land as `"<model>.input"` / `"<model>.output"`
8901                // / `"<model>.cached_input"` so the existing lookup
8902                // (`resolve_rates`) finds them with no further changes. The
8903                // rate sheet wins on conflict — it's the forward-looking
8904                // surface, the legacy per-alias `pricing` table is the
8905                // fallback for installs that haven't migrated.
8906                for (provider_type, model_id, rates) in
8907                    config.cost.rates.providers.models.iter_entries()
8908                {
8909                    let slot = by_type.entry(provider_type.to_string()).or_default();
8910                    if let Some(input) = rates.input_per_mtok {
8911                        slot.insert(format!("{model_id}.input"), input);
8912                    }
8913                    if let Some(output) = rates.output_per_mtok {
8914                        slot.insert(format!("{model_id}.output"), output);
8915                    }
8916                    if let Some(cached) = rates.cached_input_per_mtok {
8917                        slot.insert(format!("{model_id}.cached_input"), cached);
8918                    }
8919                }
8920                ChannelCostTrackingState {
8921                    tracker,
8922                    model_provider_pricing: Arc::new(by_type),
8923                    agent_alias: Arc::new(agent_alias.clone()),
8924                }
8925            }),
8926            pacing: config.pacing.clone(),
8927            max_tool_result_chars: agent.resolved.max_tool_result_chars,
8928            context_token_budget: agent.resolved.max_context_tokens,
8929            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
8930                Duration::from_millis(config.channels.debounce_ms),
8931            )),
8932            receipt_generator: if agent.resolved.tool_receipts.enabled {
8933                Some(zeroclaw_runtime::agent::tool_receipts::ReceiptGenerator::new())
8934            } else {
8935                None
8936            },
8937            show_receipts_in_response: agent.resolved.tool_receipts.show_in_response,
8938            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
8939            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
8940        });
8941
8942        agent_ctxs.insert(agent_alias.clone(), runtime_ctx);
8943    }
8944
8945    let owner_by_channel_key =
8946        build_owner_by_channel_key(&config, &enabled_agents, &collected_channel_keys);
8947
8948    // Hydrate persisted session histories into the owning agent's
8949    // `conversation_histories` LRU. Sessions whose channel has no enabled
8950    // owner are skipped so their history doesn't end up loaded into the
8951    // fallback agent (which wouldn't reply on that channel anyway).
8952    if let Some(ref store) = shared_session_store {
8953        let mut metadata = store.list_sessions_with_metadata();
8954        metadata.sort_by_key(|m| std::cmp::Reverse(m.last_activity));
8955        // Budget proportional to the number of agents — each gets up to
8956        // `MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS` slots, so a multi-agent install
8957        // hydrates strictly more total sessions than a single-agent one.
8958        let cap = MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS.saturating_mul(enabled_agents.len().max(1));
8959        if metadata.len() > cap {
8960            metadata.truncate(cap);
8961        }
8962
8963        let mut hydrated = 0usize;
8964        let mut orphans_closed = 0usize;
8965        for m in metadata {
8966            let owner_agent = m
8967                .channel_id
8968                .as_deref()
8969                .and_then(|cid| owner_by_channel_key.get(cid).cloned())
8970                .or_else(|| {
8971                    m.channel_id
8972                        .as_deref()
8973                        .and_then(|cid| cid.split_once('.').map(|(b, _)| b.to_string()))
8974                        .and_then(|b| owner_by_channel_key.get(&b).cloned())
8975                });
8976            let target_ctx = match owner_agent.as_ref().and_then(|a| agent_ctxs.get(a)) {
8977                Some(ctx) => ctx,
8978                None => continue,
8979            };
8980            let mut msgs = store.load(&m.key);
8981            if msgs.is_empty() {
8982                continue;
8983            }
8984            if msgs.len() > MAX_CHANNEL_HISTORY {
8985                msgs.drain(..msgs.len() - MAX_CHANNEL_HISTORY);
8986            }
8987            if msgs.last().is_some_and(|msg| msg.role == "user") {
8988                let closure =
8989                    ChatMessage::assistant("[Session interrupted — not continuing this request]");
8990                if let Err(e) = store.append(&m.key, &closure) {
8991                    ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
8992                        DEBUG,
8993                        ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
8994                            .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"error": format!("{}", e)})),
8995                        &format!("Failed to persist orphan closure for {}", m.key)
8996                    );
8997                }
8998                msgs.push(closure);
8999                orphans_closed += 1;
9000            }
9001            let pruned =
9002                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::history_pruner::remove_orphaned_tool_messages(&mut msgs);
9003            if !pruned.is_empty() {
9004                ::zeroclaw_log::record!(WARN, ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note).with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown).with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"category": "agent", "agent_alias": owner_agent.as_deref().unwrap_or(""), "channel": m.channel_id.as_deref().unwrap_or(""), "session_key": m.key, "removed": pruned.removed, "orphan_tool_call_ids": pruned.orphan_tool_call_ids})), "removed orphaned tool messages from restored history (tool_use/tool_result pairing inconsistency auto-healed)");
9005            }
9006
9007            let mut histories = target_ctx
9008                .conversation_histories
9009                .lock()
9010                .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
9011            histories.push(m.key.clone(), msgs);
9012            drop(histories);
9013            hydrated += 1;
9014        }
9015        if hydrated > 0 {
9016            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
9017                INFO,
9018                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
9019                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"hydrated": hydrated})),
9020                "restored sessions from disk"
9021            );
9022        }
9023        if orphans_closed > 0 {
9024            ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
9025                INFO,
9026                ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
9027                    .with_attrs(::serde_json::json!({"orphans_closed": orphans_closed})),
9028                "closed orphaned session turns from previous crash"
9029            );
9030        }
9031    }
9032
9033    let router = AgentRouter::multi(agent_ctxs, owner_by_channel_key);
9034
9035    let rx = rx_holder.expect("rx initialized by first agent's channel setup");
9036    let max_in_flight =
9037        max_in_flight_messages.expect("max_in_flight initialized by first agent's channel setup");
9038    run_message_dispatch_loop(rx, router, max_in_flight).await;
9039
9040    for h in listener_handles {
9041        let _ = h.await;
9042    }
9043
9044    Ok(())
9045}
9046
9047/// Deliver a cron job announcement to a configured channel.
9048/// Scans for credential leaks before delivery.
9049///
9050/// `thread_id` is forwarded to channels whose outbound `thread_id` is distinct
9051/// from the recipient (notably the webhook channel, which serialises both into
9052/// the JSON callback). For channels that do not honour `thread_ts` it is a
9053/// harmless no-op.
9054pub async fn deliver_announcement(
9055    config: &zeroclaw_config::schema::Config,
9056    channel: &str,
9057    target: &str,
9058    thread_id: Option<String>,
9059    output: &str,
9060) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
9061    use zeroclaw_api::channel::SendMessage;
9062    let _ = config;
9063
9064    // Scan for credential leaks before delivering
9065    let leak_detector = zeroclaw_runtime::security::LeakDetector::new();
9066    let safe_output = match leak_detector.scan(output) {
9067        zeroclaw_runtime::security::LeakResult::Detected { redacted, .. } => redacted,
9068        zeroclaw_runtime::security::LeakResult::Clean => output.to_string(),
9069    };
9070
9071    let make_msg = |s: &str| SendMessage::new(s, target).in_thread(thread_id.clone());
9072
9073    // Snapshot out of the sync RwLock before awaiting. Use the live
9074    // channel instance when available — critical for Matrix E2EE which
9075    // must reuse the authenticated client rather than re-running session
9076    // restore per delivery.
9077    let registry_snapshot = CRON_CHANNEL_REGISTRY
9078        .read()
9079        .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())
9080        .clone();
9081    if let Some(registry) = registry_snapshot
9082        && let Some(ch) = registry.get(channel.to_ascii_lowercase().as_str())
9083    {
9084        return ch.send(&make_msg(&safe_output)).await;
9085    }
9086
9087    let (raw_type, alias) = channel.split_once('.').ok_or_else(|| {
9088        anyhow::Error::msg(format!(
9089            "delivery channel {channel:?} must be a dotted <type>.<alias> ref (e.g. telegram.work)"
9090        ))
9091    })?;
9092    let channel_type = raw_type.to_ascii_lowercase();
9093    #[allow(unused_variables)]
9094    let not_configured = || {
9095        ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
9096            ERROR,
9097            ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Fail)
9098                .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Failure),
9099            &format!("[channels.{channel_type}.{alias}] not configured")
9100        );
9101        anyhow::Error::msg(format!("[channels.{channel_type}.{alias}] not configured"))
9102    };
9103    match channel_type.as_str() {
9104        #[cfg(feature = "channel-telegram")]
9105        "telegram" => {
9106            let tg = config
9107                .channels
9108                .telegram
9109                .get(alias)
9110                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9111            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("telegram", alias);
9112            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9113                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9114            let ch =
9115                TelegramChannel::new(tg.bot_token.clone(), alias, peer_resolver, tg.mention_only);
9116            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9117        }
9118        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-telegram"))]
9119        "telegram" => {
9120            anyhow::bail!("Telegram channel requires the `channel-telegram` feature");
9121        }
9122        #[cfg(feature = "channel-discord")]
9123        "discord" => {
9124            let dc = config
9125                .channels
9126                .discord
9127                .get(alias)
9128                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9129            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("discord", alias);
9130            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9131                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9132            let ch = DiscordChannel::new(
9133                dc.bot_token.clone(),
9134                dc.guild_ids.clone(),
9135                alias,
9136                peer_resolver,
9137                dc.listen_to_bots,
9138                dc.mention_only,
9139            )
9140            .with_channel_ids(dc.channel_ids.clone())
9141            .with_workspace_dir(config.channel_workspace_dir(channel));
9142            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9143        }
9144        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-discord"))]
9145        "discord" => {
9146            anyhow::bail!("Discord channel requires the `channel-discord` feature");
9147        }
9148        #[cfg(feature = "channel-slack")]
9149        "slack" => {
9150            let sl = config
9151                .channels
9152                .slack
9153                .get(alias)
9154                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9155            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("slack", alias);
9156            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9157                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9158            let ch = SlackChannel::new(
9159                sl.bot_token.clone(),
9160                sl.app_token.clone(),
9161                sl.channel_ids.clone(),
9162                alias,
9163                peer_resolver,
9164            )
9165            .with_workspace_dir(config.channel_workspace_dir(channel));
9166            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9167        }
9168        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-slack"))]
9169        "slack" => {
9170            anyhow::bail!("Slack channel requires the `channel-slack` feature");
9171        }
9172        #[cfg(feature = "channel-signal")]
9173        "signal" => {
9174            let sg = config
9175                .channels
9176                .signal
9177                .get(alias)
9178                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9179            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("signal", alias);
9180            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9181                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9182            let ch = SignalChannel::new(
9183                sg.http_url.clone(),
9184                sg.account.clone(),
9185                sg.group_ids.clone(),
9186                sg.dm_only,
9187                alias,
9188                peer_resolver,
9189                sg.ignore_attachments,
9190                sg.ignore_stories,
9191            );
9192            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9193        }
9194        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-signal"))]
9195        "signal" => {
9196            anyhow::bail!("Signal channel requires the `channel-signal` feature");
9197        }
9198        #[cfg(feature = "channel-wechat")]
9199        "wechat" => {
9200            let wc = config
9201                .channels
9202                .wechat
9203                .get(alias)
9204                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9205            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("wechat", alias);
9206            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9207                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9208            let ch = WeChatChannel::new(
9209                alias,
9210                peer_resolver,
9211                wc.api_base_url.clone(),
9212                wc.cdn_base_url.clone(),
9213                wc.state_dir.as_ref().map(std::path::PathBuf::from),
9214            )?
9215            .with_workspace_dir(config.channel_workspace_dir(channel));
9216            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9217        }
9218        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-wechat"))]
9219        "wechat" => {
9220            anyhow::bail!("WeChat channel requires the `channel-wechat` feature");
9221        }
9222        #[cfg(feature = "channel-lark")]
9223        "lark" | "feishu" => {
9224            // [channels.lark.<alias>] is the single source of truth for both
9225            // names (AGENTS.md). from_config selects the endpoint via
9226            // use_feishu. Error text names the real config table, not the
9227            // cron alias the user wrote.
9228            let lk = config.channels.lark.get(alias).ok_or_else(|| {
9229                ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
9230                    ERROR,
9231                    ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Fail)
9232                        .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Failure),
9233                    &format!(
9234                        "[channels.lark.{alias}] not configured (cron channel \"{channel_type}.{alias}\")"
9235                    )
9236                );
9237                anyhow::Error::msg(format!(
9238                    "[channels.lark.{alias}] not configured (cron channel \"{channel_type}.{alias}\")"
9239                ))
9240            })?;
9241            // Asymmetric by design: "feishu"+use_feishu=false is a typo
9242            // (hard fail). "lark"+use_feishu=true is a soft compat path
9243            // (warn but still deliver via fallback construction).
9244            if channel_type == "feishu" && !lk.use_feishu {
9245                anyhow::bail!(
9246                    "[channels.lark.{alias}] has use_feishu=false but cron channel=\"feishu.{alias}\"; \
9247                     use channel=\"lark.{alias}\" or set use_feishu=true"
9248                );
9249            }
9250            if channel_type == "lark" && lk.use_feishu {
9251                ::zeroclaw_log::record!(
9252                    WARN,
9253                    ::zeroclaw_log::Event::new(module_path!(), ::zeroclaw_log::Action::Note)
9254                        .with_outcome(::zeroclaw_log::EventOutcome::Unknown),
9255                    &format!(
9256                        "cron channel=\"lark.{alias}\" with [channels.lark.{alias}] use_feishu=true \
9257                         falls back to one-shot channel construction; prefer channel=\"feishu.{alias}\" \
9258                         to reuse the live Feishu handle from start_channels"
9259                    )
9260                );
9261            }
9262            let peers = config.channel_external_peers("lark", alias);
9263            let peer_resolver: Arc<dyn Fn() -> Vec<String> + Send + Sync> =
9264                Arc::new(move || peers.clone());
9265            let ch = LarkChannel::from_config(lk, alias, peer_resolver)
9266                .with_approval_timeout_secs(lk.approval_timeout_secs)
9267                .with_per_user_session(lk.per_user_session)
9268                .with_streaming(lk.stream_mode, lk.draft_update_interval_ms);
9269            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9270        }
9271        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-lark"))]
9272        "lark" | "feishu" => {
9273            anyhow::bail!("Lark channel requires the `channel-lark` feature");
9274        }
9275        #[cfg(feature = "channel-webhook")]
9276        "webhook" => {
9277            let wh = config
9278                .channels
9279                .webhook
9280                .get(alias)
9281                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9282            let ch = WebhookChannel::new(
9283                alias.to_string(),
9284                wh.port,
9285                wh.listen_path.clone(),
9286                wh.send_url.clone(),
9287                wh.send_method.clone(),
9288                wh.auth_header.clone(),
9289                wh.secret.clone(),
9290                wh.max_retries,
9291                wh.retry_base_delay_ms,
9292                wh.retry_max_delay_ms,
9293            );
9294            zeroclaw_api::channel::Channel::send(&ch, &make_msg(&safe_output)).await?;
9295        }
9296        #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-webhook"))]
9297        "webhook" => {
9298            anyhow::bail!("Webhook channel requires the `channel-webhook` feature");
9299        }
9300        "wecom_ws" | "wecom-ws" => {
9301            let _ = config
9302                .channels
9303                .wecom_ws
9304                .get(alias)
9305                .ok_or_else(not_configured)?;
9306            anyhow::bail!("wecom_ws channel is not connected");
9307        }
9308        other => anyhow::bail!("unsupported delivery channel: {other}"),
9309    }
9310    #[allow(unreachable_code)]
9311    Ok(())
9312}
9313
9314#[cfg(feature = "channel-wechat")]
9315fn expand_tilde_in_path(path: &str) -> PathBuf {
9316    PathBuf::from(shellexpand::tilde(path).as_ref())
9317}
9318
9319#[cfg(test)]
9320mod tests {
9321    use super::*;
9322    use std::collections::{HashMap, HashSet};
9323    use std::sync::Arc;
9324    use std::sync::atomic::{AtomicBool, AtomicUsize, Ordering};
9325    use tempfile::TempDir;
9326    use zeroclaw_memory::{Memory, MemoryCategory, SqliteMemory};
9327    use zeroclaw_providers::{ChatMessage, ModelProvider};
9328    use zeroclaw_runtime::agent::loop_::build_tool_instructions;
9329
9330    #[test]
9331    fn no_real_time_channels_message_points_at_quickstart_not_onboard() {
9332        // The "no channels configured" message must point operators at the
9333        // current command (zeroclaw quickstart), not the deleted `zeroclaw onboard`.
9334        // Source of truth: the string at orchestrator/mod.rs:~7376.
9335        let msg = super::no_real_time_channels_message();
9336        assert!(
9337            !msg.contains("zeroclaw onboard"),
9338            "stale `zeroclaw onboard` reference in message: {msg}"
9339        );
9340        assert!(
9341            msg.contains("zeroclaw quickstart"),
9342            "expected `zeroclaw quickstart` reference, got: {msg}"
9343        );
9344    }
9345
9346    #[tokio::test]
9347    async fn channel_runtime_reload_applies_env_overrides_after_migration() {
9348        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
9349        let config_path = tmp.path().join("config.toml");
9350        std::fs::write(
9351            &config_path,
9352            r#"
9353default_provider = "openrouter"
9354
9355[model_providers.openrouter]
9356name = "openrouter"
9357
9358[agents.demo]
9359provider = "openrouter"
9360model = "meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct"
9361temperature = 0.3
9362"#,
9363        )
9364        .unwrap();
9365
9366        let env_name = "ZEROCLAW_providers__models__openrouter__agent_demo__api_key";
9367        // SAFETY: this test owns this specific env-var key and restores it
9368        // before returning. The value is synthetic and not a real credential.
9369        unsafe { std::env::set_var(env_name, "sk-or-v1-test-channel-reload") };
9370
9371        let result = load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, "demo").await;
9372
9373        // SAFETY: undo the test-only process env mutation above.
9374        unsafe { std::env::remove_var(env_name) };
9375
9376        let (config, defaults) = result.unwrap();
9377        assert_eq!(
9378            defaults.api_key.as_deref(),
9379            Some("sk-or-v1-test-channel-reload")
9380        );
9381        assert!(
9382            config
9383                .env_overridden_paths
9384                .contains("providers.models.openrouter.agent_demo.api_key")
9385        );
9386    }
9387
9388    use zeroclaw_runtime::observability::NoopObserver;
9389    use zeroclaw_runtime::tools::{Tool, ToolResult};
9390
9391    fn make_workspace() -> TempDir {
9392        let tmp = TempDir::new().unwrap();
9393        // Create minimal workspace files
9394        std::fs::write(tmp.path().join("SOUL.md"), "# Soul\nBe helpful.").unwrap();
9395        std::fs::write(tmp.path().join("IDENTITY.md"), "# Identity\nName: ZeroClaw").unwrap();
9396        std::fs::write(tmp.path().join("USER.md"), "# User\nName: Test User").unwrap();
9397        std::fs::write(
9398            tmp.path().join("AGENTS.md"),
9399            "# Agents\nFollow instructions.",
9400        )
9401        .unwrap();
9402        std::fs::write(tmp.path().join("TOOLS.md"), "# Tools\nUse shell carefully.").unwrap();
9403        std::fs::write(
9404            tmp.path().join("HEARTBEAT.md"),
9405            "# Heartbeat\nCheck status.",
9406        )
9407        .unwrap();
9408        std::fs::write(tmp.path().join("MEMORY.md"), "# Memory\nUser likes Rust.").unwrap();
9409        tmp
9410    }
9411
9412    /// Minimal mock Channel returning a configurable `name()` so the
9413    /// channel-registry routing tests can simulate two aliases of the
9414    /// same channel type without pulling in real platform SDKs.
9415    /// Identity is checked via `Arc::ptr_eq`, not by inspecting fields.
9416    struct NamedMockChannel {
9417        name: &'static str,
9418    }
9419
9420    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for NamedMockChannel {
9421        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
9422            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Channel(
9423                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ChannelKind::Webhook,
9424            )
9425        }
9426        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
9427            "test"
9428        }
9429    }
9430
9431    #[async_trait::async_trait]
9432    impl Channel for NamedMockChannel {
9433        fn name(&self) -> &str {
9434            self.name
9435        }
9436        async fn send(&self, _message: &zeroclaw_api::channel::SendMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
9437            Ok(())
9438        }
9439        async fn listen(
9440            &self,
9441            _tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>,
9442        ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
9443            Ok(())
9444        }
9445    }
9446
9447    fn mock_channel(name: &'static str) -> Arc<dyn Channel> {
9448        Arc::new(NamedMockChannel { name })
9449    }
9450
9451    struct MentionMockChannel {
9452        name: &'static str,
9453        mention: &'static str,
9454    }
9455
9456    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for MentionMockChannel {
9457        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
9458            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Channel(
9459                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ChannelKind::Discord,
9460            )
9461        }
9462        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
9463            "test"
9464        }
9465    }
9466
9467    #[async_trait::async_trait]
9468    impl Channel for MentionMockChannel {
9469        fn name(&self) -> &str {
9470            self.name
9471        }
9472        fn self_addressed_mention(&self) -> Option<String> {
9473            Some(self.mention.to_string())
9474        }
9475        async fn send(&self, _message: &zeroclaw_api::channel::SendMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
9476            Ok(())
9477        }
9478        async fn listen(
9479            &self,
9480            _tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>,
9481        ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
9482            Ok(())
9483        }
9484    }
9485
9486    fn mention_mock(name: &'static str, mention: &'static str) -> Arc<dyn Channel> {
9487        Arc::new(MentionMockChannel { name, mention })
9488    }
9489
9490    fn channel_message(
9491        channel: &str,
9492        alias: Option<&str>,
9493    ) -> zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
9494        zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
9495            id: "m1".into(),
9496            sender: "u1".into(),
9497            reply_target: "r1".into(),
9498            content: "hi".into(),
9499            channel: channel.into(),
9500            channel_alias: alias.map(|s| s.to_string()),
9501            timestamp: 0,
9502            thread_ts: None,
9503            interruption_scope_id: None,
9504            attachments: vec![],
9505            subject: None,
9506        }
9507    }
9508
9509    #[test]
9510    fn composite_channel_key_aliased_uses_dotted_form() {
9511        assert_eq!(
9512            composite_channel_key("discord", Some("clamps")),
9513            "discord.clamps"
9514        );
9515        assert_eq!(
9516            composite_channel_key("telegram", Some("default")),
9517            "telegram.default"
9518        );
9519    }
9520
9521    #[test]
9522    fn composite_channel_key_unaliased_uses_bare_name() {
9523        assert_eq!(composite_channel_key("notion", None), "notion");
9524        // Empty-string alias collapses to bare name so we never produce a
9525        // `discord.` key that no message would ever match.
9526        assert_eq!(composite_channel_key("discord", Some("")), "discord");
9527    }
9528
9529    #[test]
9530    fn find_channel_for_message_resolves_by_composite_key_for_multi_alias() {
9531        // Two Discord bots in the registry: only the composite key
9532        // distinguishes them. Without this, the second insertion silently
9533        // overwrites the first via `name()` collision — the bug that left
9534        // one Discord agent unresponsive on multi-bot configs.
9535        let clamps = mock_channel("discord");
9536        let glados = mock_channel("discord");
9537        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9538        channels.insert("discord.clamps".to_string(), Arc::clone(&clamps));
9539        channels.insert("discord.glados".to_string(), Arc::clone(&glados));
9540
9541        let msg_clamps = channel_message("discord", Some("clamps"));
9542        let msg_glados = channel_message("discord", Some("glados"));
9543
