9544        let resolved_clamps = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg_clamps).expect("clamps");
9545        let resolved_glados = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg_glados).expect("glados");
9546
9547        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(resolved_clamps, &clamps), "clamps lookup");
9548        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(resolved_glados, &glados), "glados lookup");
9549        // Sanity: the two pointers are actually different.
9550        assert!(!Arc::ptr_eq(&clamps, &glados));
9551    }
9552
9553    #[test]
9554    fn aliased_inbound_emits_per_alias_mention_in_prompt() {
9555        let clamps = mention_mock("discord", "<@111>");
9556        let glados = mention_mock("discord", "<@222>");
9557        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9558        channels.insert("discord.clamps".into(), Arc::clone(&clamps));
9559        channels.insert("discord.glados".into(), Arc::clone(&glados));
9560
9561        let msg_glados = channel_message("discord", Some("glados"));
9562        let target_glados = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg_glados).cloned();
9563        let prompt_glados =
9564            build_channel_system_prompt_for_message("Base.", &msg_glados, target_glados.as_ref());
9565        assert!(
9566            prompt_glados.contains("<@222>"),
9567            "glados prompt missing its own mention: {prompt_glados}"
9568        );
9569        assert!(
9570            !prompt_glados.contains("<@111>"),
9571            "glados prompt leaked the peer's mention: {prompt_glados}"
9572        );
9573
9574        let msg_clamps = channel_message("discord", Some("clamps"));
9575        let target_clamps = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg_clamps).cloned();
9576        let prompt_clamps =
9577            build_channel_system_prompt_for_message("Base.", &msg_clamps, target_clamps.as_ref());
9578        assert!(
9579            prompt_clamps.contains("<@111>"),
9580            "clamps prompt missing its own mention: {prompt_clamps}"
9581        );
9582        assert!(
9583            !prompt_clamps.contains("<@222>"),
9584            "clamps prompt leaked the peer's mention: {prompt_clamps}"
9585        );
9586    }
9587
9588    #[test]
9589    fn unaliased_inbound_with_no_self_handle_omits_mention_block() {
9590        let webhook = mock_channel("webhook");
9591        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9592        channels.insert("webhook".into(), Arc::clone(&webhook));
9593
9594        let msg = channel_message("webhook", None);
9595        let target = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg).cloned();
9596        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt_for_message("Base.", &msg, target.as_ref());
9597
9598        assert!(
9599            target.is_some(),
9600            "registry must resolve the webhook channel"
9601        );
9602        assert!(
9603            !prompt.contains("addressable handle on this channel"),
9604            "channels without self_addressed_mention must not emit the block: {prompt}"
9605        );
9606    }
9607
9608    #[test]
9609    fn unresolved_channel_omits_mention_block() {
9610        let channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9611        let msg = channel_message("discord", Some("ghost"));
9612        let target = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg).cloned();
9613        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt_for_message("Base.", &msg, target.as_ref());
9614
9615        assert!(target.is_none());
9616        assert!(!prompt.contains("addressable handle on this channel"));
9617    }
9618
9619    #[test]
9620    fn find_channel_for_message_falls_back_to_bare_name_when_no_alias_supplied() {
9621        // Legacy inbound (or singleton channel) with `channel_alias = None`
9622        // still resolves via the bare-name slot — the registry builder
9623        // populates it for single-alias platforms so cron callers and
9624        // outbound-only channels keep working.
9625        let webhook = mock_channel("webhook");
9626        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9627        channels.insert("webhook".to_string(), Arc::clone(&webhook));
9628
9629        let msg = channel_message("webhook", None);
9630        let resolved = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg).expect("webhook");
9631        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(resolved, &webhook));
9632    }
9633
9634    #[test]
9635    fn find_channel_for_message_falls_back_to_base_for_room_qualifier() {
9636        // Multi-room channels (Matrix) deliver inbound messages with
9637        // `channel = "matrix:!roomId"`. The registry key is bare `matrix`;
9638        // the helper splits on `:` and resolves the base channel.
9639        let matrix = mock_channel("matrix");
9640        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9641        channels.insert("matrix".to_string(), Arc::clone(&matrix));
9642
9643        let msg = channel_message("matrix:!room1:example.org", None);
9644        let resolved = find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg).expect("matrix");
9645        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(resolved, &matrix));
9646    }
9647
9648    /// Build a minimal `ChannelRuntimeContext` suitable only for identity
9649    /// checks (`Arc::ptr_eq`). Every dependency is a no-op default — these
9650    /// ctxs aren't usable for actually running the dispatch loop.
9651    fn router_test_ctx() -> Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext> {
9652        Arc::new(ChannelRuntimeContext {
9653            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
9654            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
9655            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
9656            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
9657            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
9658            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
9659            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
9660                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
9661                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
9662                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
9663                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
9664                ),
9665            ),
9666            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
9667            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
9668            system_prompt: Arc::new(String::new()),
9669            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
9670            temperature: Some(0.0),
9671            auto_save_memory: false,
9672            max_tool_iterations: 0,
9673            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
9674            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(lru::LruCache::new(
9675                std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap(),
9676            ))),
9677            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
9678            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
9679            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
9680            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
9681            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
9682                telegram: false,
9683                slack: false,
9684                discord: false,
9685                mattermost: false,
9686                matrix: false,
9687                whatsapp: false,
9688            },
9689            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
9690            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
9691            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
9692            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
9693            hooks: None,
9694            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
9695            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
9696            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
9697            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
9698            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
9699            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
9700            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
9701            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
9702            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
9703            ack_reactions: true,
9704            show_tool_calls: true,
9705            session_store: None,
9706            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
9707                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
9708            )),
9709            activated_tools: None,
9710            cost_tracking: None,
9711            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
9712            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
9713            context_token_budget: 0,
9714            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
9715                Duration::ZERO,
9716            )),
9717            receipt_generator: None,
9718            show_receipts_in_response: false,
9719            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
9720            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
9721        })
9722    }
9723
9724    #[tokio::test]
9725    async fn resolve_classifier_route_returns_none_for_empty_ref() {
9726        let ctx = router_test_ctx();
9727        let empty = zeroclaw_config::providers::ModelProviderRef::default();
9728        let result = resolve_classifier_route(
9729            ctx.as_ref(),
9730            &empty,
9731            &runtime_defaults_snapshot(ctx.as_ref()),
9732        )
9733        .await;
9734        assert!(result.is_none(), "empty ref must fall back to main agent");
9735    }
9736
9737    #[tokio::test]
9738    async fn resolve_classifier_route_returns_none_for_unresolvable_ref() {
9739        let ctx = router_test_ctx();
9740        let bogus = zeroclaw_config::providers::ModelProviderRef::from("custom.does-not-exist");
9741        let result = resolve_classifier_route(
9742            ctx.as_ref(),
9743            &bogus,
9744            &runtime_defaults_snapshot(ctx.as_ref()),
9745        )
9746        .await;
9747        assert!(result.is_none(), "unresolvable ref must soft-fail to None");
9748    }
9749
9750    #[tokio::test]
9751    async fn resolve_classifier_route_returns_alias_temperature() {
9752        // Build a config where `openai.my-classifier` has `temperature = 0.0`.
9753        let mut cfg = zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default();
9754        cfg.providers.models.openai.insert(
9755            "my-classifier".to_string(),
9756            zeroclaw_config::schema::OpenAIModelProviderConfig {
9757                base: zeroclaw_config::schema::ModelProviderConfig {
9758                    model: Some("gpt-4o-mini".to_string()),
9759                    temperature: Some(0.0),
9760                    ..Default::default()
9761                },
9762            },
9763        );
9764
9765        let base_ctx = (*router_test_ctx()).clone();
9766        let ctx = Arc::new(ChannelRuntimeContext {
9767            prompt_config: Arc::new(cfg),
9768            ..base_ctx
9769        });
9770
9771        let alias_ref = zeroclaw_config::providers::ModelProviderRef::from("openai.my-classifier");
9772        let result = resolve_classifier_route(
9773            ctx.as_ref(),
9774            &alias_ref,
9775            &runtime_defaults_snapshot(ctx.as_ref()),
9776        )
9777        .await;
9778
9779        let (_, _, temp) = result.expect("must resolve to alias");
9780        assert_eq!(
9781            temp,
9782            Some(0.0),
9783            "alias temperature must be returned, not runtime_defaults.temperature"
9784        );
9785    }
9786
9787    fn seed_sender_history(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender: &str, turns: Vec<ChatMessage>) {
9788        let mut histories = ctx
9789            .conversation_histories
9790            .lock()
9791            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
9792        histories.push(sender.to_string(), turns);
9793    }
9794
9795    fn cloned_sender_history(ctx: &ChannelRuntimeContext, sender: &str) -> Vec<ChatMessage> {
9796        let histories = ctx
9797            .conversation_histories
9798            .lock()
9799            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
9800        histories.peek(sender).cloned().unwrap_or_default()
9801    }
9802
9803    fn history_signature(turns: &[ChatMessage]) -> Vec<(String, String)> {
9804        turns
9805            .iter()
9806            .map(|turn| (turn.role.clone(), turn.content.clone()))
9807            .collect()
9808    }
9809
9810    #[test]
9811    fn agent_router_multi_routes_each_alias_to_its_owning_agent() {
9812        // Two enabled agents, each owning one Discord bot. A message tagged
9813        // with `channel_alias = "clamps"` must resolve to clamps' ctx; the
9814        // same channel name with `"glados"` must resolve to glados' ctx.
9815        // This is the exact behavior that was broken before per-agent ctxs:
9816        // both bots' inbound messages used to land in one shared agent's
9817        // pipeline and reply with that agent's identity/model.
9818        let clamps_ctx = router_test_ctx();
9819        let glados_ctx = router_test_ctx();
9820        let mut by_agent: HashMap<String, Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext>> = HashMap::new();
9821        by_agent.insert("clamps".to_string(), Arc::clone(&clamps_ctx));
9822        by_agent.insert("glados".to_string(), Arc::clone(&glados_ctx));
9823        let mut owners: HashMap<String, String> = HashMap::new();
9824        owners.insert("discord.clamps".to_string(), "clamps".to_string());
9825        owners.insert("discord.glados".to_string(), "glados".to_string());
9826        let router = AgentRouter::multi(by_agent, owners);
9827
9828        let msg_clamps = channel_message("discord", Some("clamps"));
9829        let msg_glados = channel_message("discord", Some("glados"));
9830
9831        let resolved_clamps = router.resolve(&msg_clamps).expect("clamps resolves");
9832        let resolved_glados = router.resolve(&msg_glados).expect("glados resolves");
9833
9834        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(&resolved_clamps, &clamps_ctx), "clamps routing");
9835        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(&resolved_glados, &glados_ctx), "glados routing");
9836        assert!(
9837            !Arc::ptr_eq(&resolved_clamps, &resolved_glados),
9838            "ctxs distinct"
9839        );
9840    }
9841
9842    #[test]
9843    fn agent_router_multi_returns_none_for_unowned_channels() {
9844        let agent_a_ctx = router_test_ctx();
9845        let mut by_agent: HashMap<String, Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext>> = HashMap::new();
9846        by_agent.insert("agent_a".to_string(), Arc::clone(&agent_a_ctx));
9847        let mut owners: HashMap<String, String> = HashMap::new();
9848        owners.insert("discord.bot_a".to_string(), "agent_a".to_string());
9849        let router = AgentRouter::multi(by_agent, owners);
9850
9851        let cli_msg = channel_message("cli", None);
9852        assert!(router.resolve(&cli_msg).is_none(), "cli has no owner");
9853    }
9854
9855    #[test]
9856    fn agent_router_multi_resolves_bare_channel_for_singleton_owners() {
9857        let notion_agent_ctx = router_test_ctx();
9858        let mut by_agent: HashMap<String, Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext>> = HashMap::new();
9859        by_agent.insert("ops".to_string(), Arc::clone(&notion_agent_ctx));
9860        let mut owners: HashMap<String, String> = HashMap::new();
9861        owners.insert("notion".to_string(), "ops".to_string());
9862        let router = AgentRouter::multi(by_agent, owners);
9863
9864        let msg = channel_message("notion", None);
9865        let resolved = router.resolve(&msg).expect("notion resolves");
9866        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(&resolved, &notion_agent_ctx));
9867    }

9869    #[test]
9870    fn agent_router_multi_resolves_fallback_loaded_channel_to_legacy_agent() {
9871        let mut config = Config::default();
9872        config.agents.clear();
9873        config.agents.insert(
9874            "legacy".to_string(),
9875            zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig {
9876                enabled: true,
9877                channels: vec![],
9878                ..Default::default()
9879            },
9880        );
9881        let enabled_agents = vec!["legacy".to_string()];
9882        let collected_channel_keys = vec!["mattermost.default".to_string()];
9883        let owners = build_owner_by_channel_key(&config, &enabled_agents, &collected_channel_keys);
9884
9885        let legacy_ctx = router_test_ctx();
9886        let mut by_agent: HashMap<String, Arc<ChannelRuntimeContext>> = HashMap::new();
9887        by_agent.insert("legacy".to_string(), Arc::clone(&legacy_ctx));
9888        let router = AgentRouter::multi(by_agent, owners);
9889
9890        let msg = channel_message("mattermost", Some("default"));
9891        let resolved = router.resolve(&msg).expect("fallback owner resolves");
9892        assert!(Arc::ptr_eq(&resolved, &legacy_ctx));
9893    }
9894
9895    #[test]
9896    fn build_owner_by_channel_key_legacy_fallback_is_deterministic_without_default() {
9897        let mut config = Config::default();
9898        config.agents.clear();
9899        config.agents.insert(
9900            "zeta".to_string(),
9901            zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig {
9902                enabled: true,
9903                channels: vec![],
9904                ..Default::default()
9905            },
9906        );
9907        config.agents.insert(
9908            "alpha".to_string(),
9909            zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig {
9910                enabled: true,
9911                channels: vec![],
9912                ..Default::default()
9913            },
9914        );
9915
9916        let enabled_agents = vec!["alpha".to_string(), "zeta".to_string()];
9917        let collected_channel_keys = vec!["mattermost.default".to_string()];
9918        let owners = build_owner_by_channel_key(&config, &enabled_agents, &collected_channel_keys);
9919
9920        assert_eq!(
9921            owners.get("mattermost.default").map(String::as_str),
9922            Some("alpha")
9923        );
9924        assert_eq!(owners.get("mattermost").map(String::as_str), Some("alpha"));
9925    }
9926
9927    #[test]
9928    fn find_channel_for_message_returns_none_when_alias_unknown() {
9929        // A message tagged with an alias that isn't registered must not
9930        // accidentally fall through to a different bot's handle — silent
9931        // misrouting is exactly what the original collision bug caused.
9932        let clamps = mock_channel("discord");
9933        let mut channels: HashMap<String, Arc<dyn Channel>> = HashMap::new();
9934        channels.insert("discord.clamps".to_string(), Arc::clone(&clamps));
9935
9936        // No bare `discord` key and no `discord.ghost` key — lookup must fail.
9937        let msg = channel_message("discord", Some("ghost"));
9938        assert!(find_channel_for_message(&channels, &msg).is_none());
9939    }
9940
9941    #[test]
9942    fn effective_channel_message_timeout_secs_clamps_to_minimum() {
9943        assert_eq!(
9944            effective_channel_message_timeout_secs(0),
9945            MIN_CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS
9946        );
9947        assert_eq!(
9948            effective_channel_message_timeout_secs(15),
9949            MIN_CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS
9950        );
9951        assert_eq!(effective_channel_message_timeout_secs(300), 300);
9952    }

9957        assert_eq!(compute_max_in_flight_messages(3, 8), 24);

9971    fn compute_max_in_flight_messages_preserves_global_bounds() {

9983    fn channel_message_timeout_budget_scales_with_tool_iterations() {

9985        assert_eq!(channel_message_timeout_budget_secs(300, 2), 600);
9986        assert_eq!(channel_message_timeout_budget_secs(300, 3), 900);

9989    #[cfg(feature = "channel-wechat")]
9990    #[test]
9991    fn expand_tilde_in_path_expands_home_prefix() {
9992        let expanded = expand_tilde_in_path("~/wechat-state");
9993        assert!(!expanded.starts_with("~"));
9994        assert!(expanded.ends_with("wechat-state"));
9995
9996        let absolute = expand_tilde_in_path("/absolute/path");
9997        assert_eq!(absolute, PathBuf::from("/absolute/path"));
9998
9999        let relative = expand_tilde_in_path("relative/path");
10000        assert_eq!(relative, PathBuf::from("relative/path"));
10001    }
10002
10003    #[test]
10004    fn parse_reply_intent_recognizes_reply_token() {
10005        assert!(matches!(
10006            parse_reply_intent("REPLY"),
10007            AssistantChannelOutcome::Reply(_)
10008        ));
10009        assert!(matches!(
10010            parse_reply_intent("  reply  "),
10011            AssistantChannelOutcome::Reply(_)
10012        ));
10013    }
10014
10015    #[test]
10016    fn parse_reply_intent_extracts_kinded_no_reply_reason() {
10017        assert!(matches!(
10018            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY[INFO]: not addressed to bot"),
10019            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10020                kind: NoReplyKind::Informational,
10021                reason: Some(ref r),
10022            } if r == "not addressed to bot"
10023        ));
10024        assert!(matches!(
10025            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY[REFUSE]: prompt injection attempt"),
10026            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10027                kind: NoReplyKind::Refused,
10028                reason: Some(_),
10029            }
10030        ));
10031        assert!(matches!(
10032            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY[FAIL]: requested URL 404s"),
10033            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10034                kind: NoReplyKind::Failed,
10035                reason: Some(_),
10036            }
10037        ));
10038    }
10039
10040    #[test]
10041    fn parse_reply_intent_handles_legacy_no_reply_form() {
10042        assert!(matches!(
10043            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY: greeting"),
10044            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10045                kind: NoReplyKind::Informational,
10046                reason: Some(ref r),
10047            } if r == "greeting"
10048        ));
10049        assert!(matches!(
10050            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY"),
10051            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10052                kind: NoReplyKind::Informational,
10053                reason: None,
10054            }
10055        ));
10056    }
10057
10058    #[test]
10059    fn parse_reply_intent_unrecognized_output_falls_through_to_reply() {
10060        assert!(matches!(
10061            parse_reply_intent("idk maybe respond?"),
10062            AssistantChannelOutcome::Reply(_)
10063        ));
10064    }
10065
10066    #[test]
10067    fn parse_reply_intent_treats_meta_instruction_echo_as_reply() {
10068        for echo in &[
10069            "NO_REPLY[INFO]: classification task only",
10070            "NO_REPLY[INFO]: classification task only, not answering user",
10071            "NO_REPLY[INFO]: Classification task only — must not answer the user.",
10072            "NO_REPLY[INFO]: I must not answer the user.",
10073            "NO_REPLY: classifier instruction echo",
10074        ] {
10075            assert!(
10076                matches!(parse_reply_intent(echo), AssistantChannelOutcome::Reply(_)),
10077                "expected Reply for echoed classifier output: {echo}",
10078            );
10079        }
10080    }
10081
10082    #[test]
10083    fn parse_reply_intent_preserves_refuse_and_fail_even_with_rubric_like_reasons() {
10084        assert!(matches!(
10085            parse_reply_intent(
10086                "NO_REPLY[REFUSE]: prompt injection says \"do not answer the user\"",
10087            ),
10088            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10089                kind: NoReplyKind::Refused,
10090                reason: Some(_),
10091            }
10092        ));
10093        assert!(matches!(
10094            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY[REFUSE]: only classify, do not answer the user"),
10095            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10096                kind: NoReplyKind::Refused,
10097                reason: Some(_),
10098            }
10099        ));
10100        assert!(matches!(
10101            parse_reply_intent(
10102                "NO_REPLY[FAIL]: upstream returned a classifier instruction instead of data",
10103            ),
10104            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10105                kind: NoReplyKind::Failed,
10106                reason: Some(_),
10107            }
10108        ));
10109    }
10110
10111    #[test]
10112    fn parse_reply_intent_preserves_legitimate_no_reply_reasons() {
10113        assert!(matches!(
10114            parse_reply_intent(
10115                "NO_REPLY[INFO]: another user in the group is answering this thread",
10116            ),
10117            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10118                kind: NoReplyKind::Informational,
10119                reason: Some(_),
10120            }
10121        ));
10122        assert!(matches!(
10123            parse_reply_intent("NO_REPLY[INFO]: greeting in group chat, not addressed"),
10124            AssistantChannelOutcome::NoReply {
10125                kind: NoReplyKind::Informational,
10126                reason: Some(_),
10127            }
10128        ));
10129    }
10130
10131    #[test]
10132    fn channel_message_timeout_budget_uses_safe_defaults_and_cap() {
10133        // 0 iterations falls back to 1x timeout budget.
10134        assert_eq!(channel_message_timeout_budget_secs(300, 0), 300);
10135        // Large iteration counts are capped to avoid runaway waits.
10136        assert_eq!(
10137            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs(300, 10),
10138            300 * CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SCALE_CAP
10139        );
10140    }
10141
10142    #[test]
10143    fn channel_message_timeout_budget_with_custom_scale_cap() {
10144        assert_eq!(
10145            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(300, 8, 8),
10146            300 * 8
10147        );
10148        assert_eq!(
10149            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(300, 20, 8),
10150            300 * 8
10151        );
10152        assert_eq!(
10153            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(300, 10, 1),
10154            300
10155        );
10156    }
10157
10158    #[test]
10159    fn pacing_config_defaults_preserve_existing_behavior() {
10160        let pacing = zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default();
10161        assert!(pacing.step_timeout_secs.is_none());
10162        assert!(pacing.loop_detection_min_elapsed_secs.is_none());
10163        assert!(pacing.loop_ignore_tools.is_empty());
10164        assert!(pacing.message_timeout_scale_max.is_none());
10165    }
10166
10167    #[test]
10168    fn pacing_message_timeout_scale_max_overrides_default_cap() {
10169        // Custom cap of 8 scales budget proportionally
10170        assert_eq!(
10171            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(300, 10, 8),
10172            300 * 8
10173        );
10174        // Default cap produces the standard behavior
10175        assert_eq!(
10176            channel_message_timeout_budget_secs_with_cap(
10177                300,
10178                10,
10179                CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SCALE_CAP
10180            ),
10181            300 * CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SCALE_CAP
10182        );
10183    }
10184
10185    #[test]
10186    fn context_window_overflow_error_detector_matches_known_messages() {
10187        let overflow_err = anyhow::Error::msg(
10188            "OpenAI Codex stream error: Your input exceeds the context window of this model.",
10189        );
10190        assert!(is_context_window_overflow_error(&overflow_err));
10191
10192        let other_err =
10193            anyhow::Error::msg("OpenAI Codex API error (502 Bad Gateway): error code: 502");
10194        assert!(!is_context_window_overflow_error(&other_err));
10195    }
10196
10197    #[test]
10198    fn memory_context_skip_rules_exclude_history_blobs() {
10199        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10200            "telegram_123_history",
10201            r#"[{"role":"user"}]"#
10202        ));
10203        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10204            "assistant_resp_legacy",
10205            "fabricated memory"
10206        ));
10207        assert!(!should_skip_memory_context_entry("telegram_123_45", "hi"));
10208
10209        // Entries containing image markers must be skipped to prevent
10210        // auto-saved photo messages from duplicating image blocks.
10211        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10212            "telegram_user_msg_99",
10213            "[IMAGE:/tmp/workspace/photo_1_2.jpg]"
10214        ));
10215        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10216            "telegram_user_msg_100",
10217            "[IMAGE:/tmp/workspace/photo_1_2.jpg]\n\nCheck this screenshot"
10218        ));
10219        // Plain text without image markers should not be skipped.
10220        assert!(!should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10221            "telegram_user_msg_101",
10222            "Please describe the image"
10223        ));
10224
10225        // Entries containing tool_result blocks must be skipped.
10226        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10227            "telegram_user_msg_200",
10228            r#"[Tool results]
10229<tool_result name="shell">Mon Feb 20</tool_result>"#
10230        ));
10231        assert!(!should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10232            "telegram_user_msg_201",
10233            "plain text without tool results"
10234        ));
10235
10236        // Per-turn user auto-save keys must be skipped to prevent exponential
10237        // context bloat from re-injected conversation history.
10238        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10239            "user_msg",
10240            "original user message text"
10241        ));
10242        assert!(should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10243            "user_msg_a1b2c3d4e5f6",
10244            "follow-up message embedding prior context"
10245        ));
10246        // Channel-scoped keys (e.g. telegram_*) must NOT be affected.
10247        assert!(!should_skip_memory_context_entry(
10248            "telegram_user_msg_101",
10249            "Please describe the image"
10250        ));
10251    }
10252
10253    fn channel_runtime_context_for_defaults_test(
10254        zeroclaw_dir: &std::path::Path,
10255        agent_alias: &str,
10256        default_model_provider: &str,
10257        model: &str,
10258    ) -> ChannelRuntimeContext {
10259        ChannelRuntimeContext {
10260            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
10261            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
10262            model_provider_ref: Arc::new(default_model_provider.to_string()),
10263            agent_alias: Arc::new(agent_alias.to_string()),
10264            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig {
10265                model_provider: default_model_provider.into(),
10266                ..Default::default()
10267            }),
10268            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10269            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
10270                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
10271                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10272                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
10273                    zeroclaw_dir.to_path_buf(),
10274                ),
10275            ),
10276            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
10277            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
10278            system_prompt: Arc::new("system".to_string()),
10279            model: Arc::new(model.to_string()),
10280            temperature: Some(0.0),
10281            auto_save_memory: false,
10282            max_tool_iterations: 5,
10283            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
10284            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(lru::LruCache::new(
10285                std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap(),
10286            ))),
10287            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
10288            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10289            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10290            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
10291            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
10292                telegram: false,
10293                slack: false,
10294                discord: false,
10295                mattermost: false,
10296                matrix: false,
10297                whatsapp: false,
10298            },
10299            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
10300            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
10301            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
10302            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
10303            hooks: None,
10304            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions {
10305                zeroclaw_dir: Some(zeroclaw_dir.to_path_buf()),
10306                ..Default::default()
10307            },
10308            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
10309            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
10310            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
10311            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10312            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
10313            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10314            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10315            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
10316            ack_reactions: true,
10317            show_tool_calls: true,
10318            session_store: None,
10319            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
10320                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
10321            )),
10322            activated_tools: None,
10323            cost_tracking: None,
10324            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
10325            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
10326            context_token_budget: 0,
10327            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
10328                Duration::ZERO,
10329            )),
10330            receipt_generator: None,
10331            show_receipts_in_response: false,
10332            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10333            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10334        }
10335    }
10336
10337    #[test]
10338    fn runtime_defaults_are_scoped_by_runtime_context() {
10339        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
10340        let agent_a = channel_runtime_context_for_defaults_test(
10341            tmp.path(),
10342            "agent_a",
10343            "openrouter.default",
10344            "startup-a",
10345        );
10346        let agent_b = channel_runtime_context_for_defaults_test(
10347            tmp.path(),
10348            "agent_b",
10349            "anthropic.default",
10350            "startup-b",
10351        );
10352        assert!(!runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_a).hot);
10353        assert!(!runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_b).hot);
10354
10355        let hot_override = ChannelRuntimeOverride {
10356            config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
10357            defaults: ChannelRuntimeDefaults {
10358                default_model_provider: "openrouter.reloaded".to_string(),
10359                model: "hot-model".to_string(),
10360                temperature: Some(0.7),
10361                api_key: Some("hot-key".to_string()),
10362                api_url: Some("https://example.test/v1".to_string()),
10363                reliability: zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default(),
10364            },
10365            generation: 1,
10366        };
10367        *agent_a
10368            .runtime_defaults_override
10369            .lock()
10370            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()) = Some(Arc::new(hot_override));
10371
10372        let route_a = default_route_selection_from_snapshot(&runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_a));
10373        assert_eq!(route_a.model_provider, "openrouter.reloaded");
10374        assert_eq!(route_a.model, "hot-model");
10375        let snapshot_a = runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_a);
10376        assert!(snapshot_a.hot);
10377        assert_eq!(snapshot_a.generation, 1);
10378
10379        let route_b = default_route_selection_from_snapshot(&runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_b));
10380        assert_eq!(route_b.model_provider, "anthropic.default");
10381        assert_eq!(route_b.model, "startup-b");
10382        assert!(!runtime_defaults_snapshot(&agent_b).hot);
10383    }
10384
10385    #[tokio::test]
10386    async fn load_runtime_config_uses_resolved_agent_provider() {
10387        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
10388        let config_path = tmp.path().join("config.toml");
10389        tokio::fs::write(
10390            &config_path,
10391            r#"
10392schema_version = 3
10393
10394[agents.agent_a]
10395model_provider = "openrouter.hot"
10396
10397[agents.agent_b]
10398model_provider = "anthropic.default"
10399
10400[providers.models.openrouter.hot]
10401model = "hot-model"
10402api_key = "hot-key"
10403uri = "https://hot.example.test/v1"
10404temperature = 0.2
10405
10406[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10407model = "cold-model"
10408api_key = "cold-key"
10409"#,
10410        )
10411        .await
10412        .unwrap();
10413
10414        let (_config, defaults) = load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, "agent_a")
10415            .await
10416            .unwrap();
10417
10418        assert_eq!(defaults.default_model_provider, "openrouter.hot");
10419        assert_eq!(defaults.model, "hot-model");
10420        assert_eq!(defaults.api_key.as_deref(), Some("hot-key"));
10421        assert_eq!(
10422            defaults.api_url.as_deref(),
10423            Some("https://hot.example.test/v1")
10424        );
10425        assert_eq!(defaults.temperature, Some(0.2));
10426    }
10427
10428    #[tokio::test]
10429    async fn load_runtime_config_rejects_unresolved_agent_provider() {
10430        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
10431        let config_path = tmp.path().join("config.toml");
10432        tokio::fs::write(
10433            &config_path,
10434            r#"
10435[agents.agent_a]
10436model_provider = "openrouter.missing"
10437
10438[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10439model = "cold-model"
10440api_key = "cold-key"
10441"#,
10442        )
10443        .await
10444        .unwrap();
10445
10446        let err = load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, "agent_a")
10447            .await
10448            .expect_err("unresolved agent provider should reject reload");
10449
10450        assert!(
10451            err.to_string()
10452                .contains("model_provider `openrouter.missing` does not resolve")
10453        );
10454    }
10455
10456    #[tokio::test]
10457    async fn load_runtime_config_rejects_missing_agent() {
10458        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
10459        let config_path = tmp.path().join("config.toml");
10460        tokio::fs::write(
10461            &config_path,
10462            r#"
10463[agents.agent_b]
10464model_provider = "anthropic.default"
10465
10466[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10467model = "cold-model"
10468api_key = "cold-key"
10469"#,
10470        )
10471        .await
10472        .unwrap();
10473
10474        let err = load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, "agent_a")
10475            .await
10476            .expect_err("runtime reload should reject a config missing the active agent");
10477
10478        assert!(err.to_string().contains("agents.agent_a is not configured"));
10479    }
10480
10481    #[tokio::test]
10482    async fn load_runtime_config_rejects_empty_agent_provider() {
10483        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
10484        let config_path = tmp.path().join("config.toml");
10485        tokio::fs::write(
10486            &config_path,
10487            r#"
10488[agents.agent_a]
10489model_provider = ""
10490
10491[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10492model = "first-model"
10493api_key = "first-key"
10494
10495[providers.models.openrouter.default]
10496model = "second-model"
10497api_key = "second-key"
10498"#,
10499        )
10500        .await
10501        .unwrap();
10502
10503        let err = load_runtime_config_and_defaults(&config_path, "agent_a")
10504            .await
10505            .expect_err("empty agent provider should reject reload");
10506
10507        assert!(err.to_string().contains("model_provider is empty"));
10508    }

10510    #[test]
10511    fn provider_credentials_use_target_alias_key_after_reload() {
10512        let config: Config = toml::from_str(
10513            r#"
10514[providers.models.openrouter.default]
10515model = "openrouter-model"
10516api_key = "openrouter-key"
10517uri = "https://openrouter.example.test/v1"
10518
10519[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10520model = "anthropic-model"
10521api_key = "anthropic-key"
10522uri = "https://anthropic.example.test/v1"
10523"#,
10524        )
10525        .unwrap();
10526        let (api_key, api_url) = provider_credentials_for_ref(&config, "anthropic.default");
10527
10528        assert_eq!(api_key.as_deref(), Some("anthropic-key"));
10529        assert_eq!(
10530            api_url.as_deref(),
10531            Some("https://anthropic.example.test/v1")
10532        );
10533    }
10534
10535    #[test]
10536    fn provider_credentials_do_not_fall_back_to_default_alias() {
10537        let config: Config = toml::from_str(
10538            r#"
10539[providers.models.openrouter.default]
10540model = "openrouter-model"
10541api_key = "openrouter-key"
10542
10543[providers.models.anthropic.default]
10544model = "anthropic-model"
10545api_key = "anthropic-key"
10546"#,
10547        )
10548        .unwrap();
10549
10550        let (api_key, api_url) = provider_credentials_for_ref(&config, "anthropic");
10551
10552        assert_eq!(api_key, None);
10553        assert_eq!(api_url, None);
10554    }
10555
10556    #[test]
10557    fn provider_cache_key_isolates_hot_generations() {
10558        let startup = provider_cache_key("openrouter.default", None, 0);
10559        let hot_1 = provider_cache_key("openrouter.default", None, 1);
10560        let hot_2 = provider_cache_key("openrouter.default", None, 2);
10561
10562        assert_eq!(startup, "openrouter.default");
10563        assert_ne!(hot_1, startup);
10564        assert_ne!(hot_1, hot_2);
10565    }
10566
10567    #[test]
10568    fn strip_tool_result_content_removes_blocks_and_header() {
10569        let input = r#"[Tool results]
10570<tool_result name="shell">Mon Feb 20</tool_result>
10571<tool_result name="http_request">{"status":200}</tool_result>"#;
10572        assert_eq!(strip_tool_result_content(input), "");
10573
10574        let mixed = "Some context\n<tool_result name=\"shell\">ok</tool_result>\nMore text";
10575        let cleaned = strip_tool_result_content(mixed);
10576        assert!(cleaned.contains("Some context"));
10577        assert!(cleaned.contains("More text"));
10578        assert!(!cleaned.contains("tool_result"));
10579
10580        assert_eq!(
10581            strip_tool_result_content("no tool results here"),
10582            "no tool results here"
10583        );
10584        assert_eq!(strip_tool_result_content(""), "");
10585    }
10586
10587    #[test]
10588    fn strip_tool_summary_prefix_removes_prefix_and_preserves_content() {
10589        let input = "[Used tools: browser_open, shell]\nI opened the page successfully.";
10590        assert_eq!(
10591            strip_tool_summary_prefix(input),
10592            "I opened the page successfully."
10593        );
10594    }
10595
10596    #[test]
10597    fn strip_tool_summary_prefix_returns_empty_when_only_prefix() {
10598        let input = "[Used tools: browser_open]";
10599        assert_eq!(strip_tool_summary_prefix(input), "");
10600    }
10601
10602    #[test]
10603    fn strip_tool_summary_prefix_preserves_text_without_prefix() {
10604        let input = "Here is the result of the search.";
10605        assert_eq!(strip_tool_summary_prefix(input), input);
10606    }
10607
10608    #[test]
10609    fn strip_tool_summary_prefix_handles_multiple_newlines() {
10610        let input = "[Used tools: shell]\n\nThe command output is 42.";
10611        assert_eq!(
10612            strip_tool_summary_prefix(input),
10613            "The command output is 42."
10614        );
10615    }
10616
10617    #[test]
10618    fn ensure_nonempty_channel_reply_substitutes_fallback_when_empty() {
10619        let result = ensure_nonempty_channel_reply(
10620            String::new(),
10621            "   ",
10622            "whatsapp",
10623            "15551234567@s.whatsapp.net",
10624        );
10625        assert_eq!(result, EMPTY_CHANNEL_REPLY_FALLBACK);
10626    }
10627
10628    #[test]
10629    fn ensure_nonempty_channel_reply_preserves_nonempty_text() {
10630        let result = ensure_nonempty_channel_reply(
10631            "Hello".to_string(),
10632            "Hello",
10633            "whatsapp",
10634            "15551234567@s.whatsapp.net",
10635        );
10636        assert_eq!(result, "Hello");
10637    }
10638
10639    #[test]
10640    fn sanitize_channel_response_strips_used_tools_with_leading_whitespace() {
10641        let tools: Vec<Box<dyn Tool>> = Vec::new();
10642        //: response with leading whitespace before [Used tools: ...]
10643        let input = "  [Used tools: web_search_tool]\nHere is the search result.";
10644
10645        let result = sanitize_channel_response(input, &tools);
10646
10647        assert!(!result.contains("[Used tools:"));
10648        assert!(result.contains("Here is the search result."));
10649    }
10650
10651    #[test]
10652    fn normalize_cached_channel_turns_merges_consecutive_user_turns() {
10653        let turns = vec![
10654            ChatMessage::user("forwarded content"),
10655            ChatMessage::user("summarize this"),
10656        ];
10657
10658        let normalized = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns);
10659        assert_eq!(normalized.len(), 1);
10660        assert_eq!(normalized[0].role, "user");
10661        assert!(normalized[0].content.contains("forwarded content"));
10662        assert!(normalized[0].content.contains("summarize this"));
10663    }
10664
10665    #[test]
10666    fn normalize_cached_channel_turns_merges_consecutive_assistant_turns() {
10667        let turns = vec![
10668            ChatMessage::user("first user"),
10669            ChatMessage::assistant("assistant part 1"),
10670            ChatMessage::assistant("assistant part 2"),
10671            ChatMessage::user("next user"),
10672        ];
10673
10674        let normalized = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns);
10675        assert_eq!(normalized.len(), 3);
10676        assert_eq!(normalized[0].role, "user");
10677        assert_eq!(normalized[1].role, "assistant");
10678        assert_eq!(normalized[2].role, "user");
10679        assert!(normalized[1].content.contains("assistant part 1"));
10680        assert!(normalized[1].content.contains("assistant part 2"));
10681    }
10682
10683    /// Verify that an orphan user turn followed by a failure-marker assistant
10684    /// turn normalizes correctly, so the LLM sees the failed request as closed
10685    /// and does not re-execute it on the next user message.
10686    #[test]
10687    fn normalize_preserves_failure_marker_after_orphan_user_turn() {
10688        let turns = vec![
10689            ChatMessage::user("download something from GitHub"),
10690            ChatMessage::assistant("[Task failed — not continuing this request]"),
10691            ChatMessage::user("what is WAL?"),
10692        ];
10693
10694        let normalized = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns);
10695        assert_eq!(normalized.len(), 3);
10696        assert_eq!(normalized[0].role, "user");
10697        assert_eq!(normalized[1].role, "assistant");
10698        assert!(normalized[1].content.contains("Task failed"));
10699        assert_eq!(normalized[2].role, "user");
10700        assert_eq!(normalized[2].content, "what is WAL?");
10701    }
10702
10703    /// Same as above but for the timeout variant.
10704    #[test]
10705    fn normalize_preserves_timeout_marker_after_orphan_user_turn() {
10706        let turns = vec![
10707            ChatMessage::user("run a long task"),
10708            ChatMessage::assistant("[Task timed out — not continuing this request]"),
10709            ChatMessage::user("next question"),
10710        ];
10711
10712        let normalized = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns);
10713        assert_eq!(normalized.len(), 3);
10714        assert_eq!(normalized[1].role, "assistant");
10715        assert!(normalized[1].content.contains("Task timed out"));
10716        assert_eq!(normalized[2].content, "next question");
10717    }
10718
10719    #[test]
10720    fn compact_sender_history_keeps_recent_truncated_messages() {
10721        let mut histories =
10722            lru::LruCache::new(std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap());
10723        let sender = "telegram_u1".to_string();
10724        histories.push(
10725            sender.clone(),
10726            (0..20)
10727                .map(|idx| {
10728                    let content = format!("msg-{idx}-{}", "x".repeat(700));
10729                    if idx % 2 == 0 {
10730                        ChatMessage::user(content)
10731                    } else {
10732                        ChatMessage::assistant(content)
10733                    }
10734                })
10735                .collect::<Vec<_>>(),
10736        );
10737
10738        let ctx = ChannelRuntimeContext {
10739            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
10740            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
10741            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
10742            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
10743            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
10744            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10745            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
10746                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
10747                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10748                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
10749                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
10750                ),
10751            ),
10752            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
10753            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
10754            system_prompt: Arc::new("system".to_string()),
10755            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
10756            temperature: Some(0.0),
10757            auto_save_memory: false,
10758            max_tool_iterations: 5,
10759            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
10760            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(histories)),
10761            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
10762            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10763            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10764            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
10765            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
10766                telegram: false,
10767                slack: false,
10768                discord: false,
10769                mattermost: false,
10770                matrix: false,
10771                whatsapp: false,
10772            },
10773            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
10774            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
10775            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
10776            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
10777            hooks: None,
10778            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
10779            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
10780            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
10781            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
10782            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10783            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
10784            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10785            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10786            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
10787            ack_reactions: true,
10788            show_tool_calls: true,
10789            session_store: None,
10790            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
10791                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
10792            )),
10793            activated_tools: None,
10794            cost_tracking: None,
10795            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
10796            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
10797            context_token_budget: 0,
10798            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
10799                Duration::ZERO,
10800            )),
10801            receipt_generator: None,
10802            show_receipts_in_response: false,
10803            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10804            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10805        };
10806
10807        assert!(compact_sender_history(&ctx, &sender));
10808
10809        let locked_histories = ctx
10810            .conversation_histories
10811            .lock()
10812            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
10813        let kept = locked_histories
10814            .peek(&sender)
10815            .expect("sender history should remain");
10816        assert_eq!(kept.len(), CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_KEEP_MESSAGES);
10817        assert!(kept.iter().all(|turn| {
10818            let len = turn.content.chars().count();
10819            len <= CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_CONTENT_CHARS
10820                || (len <= CHANNEL_HISTORY_COMPACT_CONTENT_CHARS + 3
10821                    && turn.content.ends_with("..."))
10822        }));
10823    }
10824
10825    #[test]
10826    fn proactive_trim_drops_oldest_turns_when_over_budget() {
10827        // Each message is 100 chars; 10 messages = 1000 chars total.
10828        let mut turns: Vec<ChatMessage> = (0..10)
10829            .map(|i| {
10830                let content = format!("m{i}-{}", "a".repeat(96));
10831                if i % 2 == 0 {
10832                    ChatMessage::user(content)
10833                } else {
10834                    ChatMessage::assistant(content)
10835                }
10836            })
10837            .collect();
10838
10839        // Budget of 500 should drop roughly half (oldest turns).
10840        let dropped = proactive_trim_turns(&mut turns, 500);
10841        assert!(dropped > 0, "should have dropped some turns");
10842        assert!(turns.len() < 10, "should have fewer turns after trimming");
10843        // Last turn should always be preserved.
10844        assert!(
10845            turns.last().unwrap().content.starts_with("m9-"),
10846            "most recent turn must be preserved"
10847        );
10848        // Total chars should now be within budget.
10849        let total: usize = turns.iter().map(|t| t.content.chars().count()).sum();
10850        assert!(total <= 500, "total chars {total} should be within budget");
10851    }
10852
10853    #[test]
10854    fn proactive_trim_noop_when_within_budget() {
10855        let mut turns = vec![
10856            ChatMessage::user("hello".to_string()),
10857            ChatMessage::assistant("hi there".to_string()),
10858        ];
10859        let dropped = proactive_trim_turns(&mut turns, 10_000);
10860        assert_eq!(dropped, 0);
10861        assert_eq!(turns.len(), 2);
10862    }
10863
10864    #[test]
10865    fn proactive_trim_preserves_last_turn_even_when_over_budget() {
10866        let mut turns = vec![ChatMessage::user("x".repeat(2000))];
10867        let dropped = proactive_trim_turns(&mut turns, 100);
10868        assert_eq!(dropped, 0, "single turn must never be dropped");
10869        assert_eq!(turns.len(), 1);
10870    }
10871
10872    #[test]
10873    fn append_sender_turn_stores_single_turn_per_call() {
10874        let sender = "telegram_u2".to_string();
10875        let ctx = ChannelRuntimeContext {
10876            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
10877            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
10878            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
10879            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
10880            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
10881            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10882            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
10883                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
10884                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10885                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
10886                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
10887                ),
10888            ),
10889            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
10890            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
10891            system_prompt: Arc::new("system".to_string()),
10892            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
10893            temperature: Some(0.0),
10894            auto_save_memory: false,
10895            max_tool_iterations: 5,
10896            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
10897            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(lru::LruCache::new(
10898                std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap(),
10899            ))),
10900            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
10901            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10902            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
10903            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
10904            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
10905                telegram: false,
10906                slack: false,
10907                discord: false,
10908                mattermost: false,
10909                matrix: false,
10910                whatsapp: false,
10911            },
10912            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
10913            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
10914            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
10915            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
10916            hooks: None,
10917            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
10918            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
10919            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
10920            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
10921            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10922            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
10923            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10924            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
10925            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
10926            ack_reactions: true,
10927            show_tool_calls: true,
10928            session_store: None,
10929            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
10930                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
10931            )),
10932            activated_tools: None,
10933            cost_tracking: None,
10934            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
10935            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
10936            context_token_budget: 0,
10937            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
10938                Duration::ZERO,
10939            )),
10940            receipt_generator: None,
10941            show_receipts_in_response: false,
10942            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10943            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
10944        };
10945
10946        append_sender_turn(&ctx, &sender, ChatMessage::user("hello"));
10947
10948        let histories = ctx
10949            .conversation_histories
10950            .lock()
10951            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
10952        let turns = histories
10953            .peek(&sender)
10954            .expect("sender history should exist");
10955        assert_eq!(turns.len(), 1);
10956        assert_eq!(turns[0].role, "user");
10957        assert_eq!(turns[0].content, "hello");
10958    }
10959
10960    #[test]
10961    fn timestamp_channel_user_content_adds_wall_clock_prefix() {
10962        let stamped = timestamp_channel_user_content("hello");
10963
10964        assert!(
10965            stamped.starts_with('['),
10966            "timestamped content should start with a bracketed timestamp: {stamped}"
10967        );
10968        assert!(
10969            stamped.contains("] hello"),
10970            "timestamped content should preserve the user message after the timestamp: {stamped}"
10971        );
10972    }
10973
10974    #[test]
10975    fn rollback_orphan_user_turn_removes_only_latest_matching_user_turn() {
10976        let sender = "telegram_u3".to_string();
10977        let mut histories =
10978            lru::LruCache::new(std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap());
10979        histories.push(
10980            sender.clone(),
10981            vec![
10982                ChatMessage::user("first"),
10983                ChatMessage::assistant("ok"),
10984                ChatMessage::user("pending"),
10985            ],
10986        );
10987        let ctx = ChannelRuntimeContext {
10988            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
10989            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
10990            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
10991            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
10992            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
10993            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10994            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
10995                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
10996                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
10997                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
10998                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
10999                ),
11000            ),
11001            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
11002            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
11003            system_prompt: Arc::new("system".to_string()),
11004            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
11005            temperature: Some(0.0),
11006            auto_save_memory: false,
11007            max_tool_iterations: 5,
11008            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
11009            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(histories)),
11010            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
11011            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
11012            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
11013            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
11014            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
11015                telegram: false,
11016                slack: false,
11017                discord: false,
11018                mattermost: false,
11019                matrix: false,
11020                whatsapp: false,
11021            },
11022            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
11023            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
11024            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
11025            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
11026            hooks: None,
11027            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
11028            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
11029            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
11030            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
11031            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11032            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
11033            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11034            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11035            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
11036            ack_reactions: true,
11037            show_tool_calls: true,
11038            session_store: None,
11039            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
11040                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
11041            )),
11042            activated_tools: None,
11043            cost_tracking: None,
11044            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
11045            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
11046            context_token_budget: 0,
11047            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
11048                Duration::ZERO,
11049            )),
11050            receipt_generator: None,
11051            show_receipts_in_response: false,
11052            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
11053            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
11054        };
11055
11056        assert!(rollback_orphan_user_turn(&ctx, &sender, "pending"));
11057
11058        let locked_histories = ctx
11059            .conversation_histories
11060            .lock()
11061            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
11062        let turns = locked_histories
11063            .peek(&sender)
11064            .expect("sender history should remain");
11065        assert_eq!(turns.len(), 2);
11066        assert_eq!(turns[0].content, "first");
11067        assert_eq!(turns[1].content, "ok");
11068    }
11069
11070    #[test]
11071    fn rollback_orphan_user_turn_also_removes_from_session_store() {
11072        let tmp = tempfile::TempDir::new().unwrap();
11073        let store: Arc<dyn zeroclaw_infra::session_backend::SessionBackend> =
11074            Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::session_store::SessionStore::new(tmp.path()).unwrap());
11075
11076        let sender = "telegram_u4".to_string();
11077
11078        // Pre-populate the session store with the same turns.
11079        store.append(&sender, &ChatMessage::user("first")).unwrap();
11080        store
11081            .append(&sender, &ChatMessage::assistant("ok"))
11082            .unwrap();
11083        store
11084            .append(
11085                &sender,
11086                &ChatMessage::user("[IMAGE:/tmp/photo.jpg]\n\nDescribe this"),
11087            )
11088            .unwrap();
11089
11090        let mut histories =
11091            lru::LruCache::new(std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap());
11092        histories.push(
11093            sender.clone(),
11094            vec![
11095                ChatMessage::user("first"),
11096                ChatMessage::assistant("ok"),
11097                ChatMessage::user("[IMAGE:/tmp/photo.jpg]\n\nDescribe this"),
11098            ],
11099        );
11100
11101        let ctx = ChannelRuntimeContext {
11102            channels_by_name: Arc::new(HashMap::new()),
11103            model_provider: Arc::new(DummyModelProvider),
11104            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
11105            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
11106            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
11107            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
11108            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
11109                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
11110                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
11111                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
11112                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
11113                ),
11114            ),
11115            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
11116            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
11117            system_prompt: Arc::new("system".to_string()),
11118            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
11119            temperature: Some(0.0),
11120            auto_save_memory: false,
11121            max_tool_iterations: 5,
11122            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
11123            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(histories)),
11124            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
11125            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
11126            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
11127            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
11128            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
11129                telegram: false,
11130                slack: false,
11131                discord: false,
11132                mattermost: false,
11133                matrix: false,
11134                whatsapp: false,
11135            },
11136            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
11137            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
11138            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
11139            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
11140            hooks: None,
11141            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
11142            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
11143            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
11144            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
11145            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11146            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
11147            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11148            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
11149            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
11150            ack_reactions: true,
11151            show_tool_calls: true,
11152            session_store: Some(Arc::clone(&store)),
11153            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
11154                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
11155            )),
11156            activated_tools: None,
11157            cost_tracking: None,
11158            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
11159            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
11160            context_token_budget: 0,
11161            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
11162                Duration::ZERO,
11163            )),
11164            receipt_generator: None,
11165            show_receipts_in_response: false,
11166            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
11167            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
11168        };
11169
11170        assert!(rollback_orphan_user_turn(
11171            &ctx,
11172            &sender,
11173            "[IMAGE:/tmp/photo.jpg]\n\nDescribe this"
11174        ));
11175
11176        // In-memory history should have 2 turns remaining.
11177        let locked = ctx
11178            .conversation_histories
11179            .lock()
11180            .unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner());
11181        let turns = locked.peek(&sender).expect("history should remain");
11182        assert_eq!(turns.len(), 2);
11183
11184        // Session store should also have only 2 entries.
11185        let persisted = store.load(&sender);
11186        assert_eq!(
11187            persisted.len(),
11188            2,
11189            "session store should also lose the rolled-back turn"
11190        );
11191        assert_eq!(persisted[0].content, "first");
11192        assert_eq!(persisted[1].content, "ok");
11193    }
11194
11195    struct DummyModelProvider;
11196
11197    #[async_trait::async_trait]
11198    impl ModelProvider for DummyModelProvider {
11199        async fn chat_with_system(
11200            &self,
11201            _system_prompt: Option<&str>,
11202            _message: &str,
11203            _model: &str,
11204            _temperature: Option<f64>,
11205        ) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
11206            Ok("ok".to_string())
11207        }
11208    }
11209    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for DummyModelProvider {
11210        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
11211            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Provider(
11212                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ProviderKind::Model(
11213                    ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ModelProviderKind::Custom,
11214                ),
11215            )
11216        }
11217        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
11218            "DummyModelProvider"
11219        }
11220    }
11221
11222    struct FormatErrorModelProvider;
11223
11224    #[async_trait::async_trait]
11225    impl ModelProvider for FormatErrorModelProvider {
11226        async fn chat_with_system(
11227            &self,
11228            _system_prompt: Option<&str>,
11229            _message: &str,
11230            _model: &str,
11231            _temperature: Option<f64>,
11232        ) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
11233            Ok("ok".to_string())
11234        }
11235
11236        async fn chat_with_history(
11237            &self,
11238            messages: &[ChatMessage],
11239            _model: &str,
11240            _temperature: Option<f64>,
11241        ) -> anyhow::Result<String> {
11242            if messages
11243                .iter()
11244                .any(|msg| msg.content.contains("trigger format error"))
11245            {
11246                anyhow::bail!(
11247                    "All model_providers/models failed. Attempts:\nprovider=custom:https://example.invalid/v1 model=test-model attempt 1/3: non_retryable; error=Custom API error (400 Bad Request): {{\"error\":{{\"message\":\"Format Error\",\"type\":\"invalid_request_error\",\"param\":null,\"code\":\"400\"}},\"request_id\":\"test-request-id\"}}"
11248                );
11249            }
11250
11251            Ok("ok".to_string())
11252        }
11253    }
11254    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for FormatErrorModelProvider {
11255        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
11256            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Provider(
11257                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ProviderKind::Model(
11258                    ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ModelProviderKind::Custom,
11259                ),
11260            )
11261        }
11262        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
11263            "FormatErrorModelProvider"
11264        }
11265    }
11266
11267    #[derive(Default)]
11268    struct RecordingChannel {
11269        sent_messages: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>,
11270        start_typing_calls: AtomicUsize,
11271        stop_typing_calls: AtomicUsize,
11272        reactions_added: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<(String, String, String)>>,
11273        reactions_removed: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<(String, String, String)>>,
11274    }
11275
11276    #[derive(Default)]
11277    struct TelegramRecordingChannel {
11278        sent_messages: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>,
11279    }
11280
11281    #[derive(Default)]
11282    struct SlackRecordingChannel {
11283        sent_messages: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>,
11284    }
11285
11286    #[derive(Default)]
11287    struct WhatsAppRecordingChannel {
11288        sent_messages: tokio::sync::Mutex<Vec<String>>,
11289    }
11290
11291    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for TelegramRecordingChannel {
11292        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
11293            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Channel(
11294                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ChannelKind::Webhook,
11295            )
11296        }
11297        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
11298            "test"
11299        }
11300    }
11301
11302    #[async_trait::async_trait]
11303    impl Channel for TelegramRecordingChannel {
11304        fn name(&self) -> &str {
11305            "telegram"
11306        }
11307
11308        async fn send(&self, message: &SendMessage) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
11309            self.sent_messages
11310                .lock()
11311                .await
11312                .push(format!("{}:{}", message.recipient, message.content));
11313            Ok(())
11314        }
11315
11316        async fn listen(
11317            &self,
11318            _tx: tokio::sync::mpsc::Sender<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>,
11319        ) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
11320            Ok(())
11321        }
11322
11323        async fn start_typing(&self, _recipient: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
11324            Ok(())
11325        }
11326
11327        async fn stop_typing(&self, _recipient: &str) -> anyhow::Result<()> {
11328            Ok(())
11329        }
11330    }
11331
11332    impl ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Attributable for SlackRecordingChannel {
11333        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
11334            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Channel(
11335                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ChannelKind::Webhook,
11336            )
11337        }
11338        fn alias(&self) -> &str {
11339            "test"
11340        }
11341    }
11342

11350            self.sent_messages

11362        }
11363

11415        fn role(&self) -> ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role {
11416            ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::Role::Channel(
11417                ::zeroclaw_api::attribution::ChannelKind::Webhook,
11418            )
