19168    #[test]
19169    fn interrupt_on_new_message_disabled_for_discord_by_default() {
19170        let cfg = InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
19171            telegram: false,
19172            slack: false,
19173            discord: false,
19174            mattermost: false,
19175            matrix: false,
19176            whatsapp: false,
19177        };
19178        assert!(!cfg.enabled_for_channel("discord"));
19179    }
19180
19181    // ── interruption_scope_key tests ──────────────────────────────────────
19182
19183    #[test]
19184    fn interruption_scope_key_without_scope_id_is_three_component() {
19185        let msg = zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
19186            id: "1".into(),
19187            sender: "alice".into(),
19188            reply_target: "room".into(),
19189            content: "hi".into(),
19190            channel: "matrix".into(),
19191            channel_alias: None,
19192            timestamp: 0,
19193            thread_ts: None,
19194            interruption_scope_id: None,
19195            attachments: vec![],
19196            subject: None,
19197        };
19198        assert_eq!(interruption_scope_key(&msg), "matrix_room_alice");
19199    }
19200
19201    #[test]
19202    fn interruption_scope_key_with_scope_id_is_four_component() {
19203        let msg = zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
19204            id: "1".into(),
19205            sender: "alice".into(),
19206            reply_target: "room".into(),
19207            content: "hi".into(),
19208            channel: "matrix".into(),
19209            channel_alias: None,
19210            timestamp: 0,
19211            thread_ts: Some("$thread1".into()),
19212            interruption_scope_id: Some("$thread1".into()),
19213            attachments: vec![],
19214            subject: None,
19215        };
19216        assert_eq!(interruption_scope_key(&msg), "matrix_room_alice_$thread1");
19217    }
19218
19219    #[test]
19220    fn interruption_scope_key_thread_ts_alone_does_not_affect_key() {
19221        // thread_ts used for reply anchoring should not bleed into scope key
19222        let msg = zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
19223            id: "1".into(),
19224            sender: "alice".into(),
19225            reply_target: "C123".into(),
19226            content: "hi".into(),
19227            channel: "slack".into(),
19228            channel_alias: None,
19229            timestamp: 0,
19230            thread_ts: Some("1234567890.000100".into()), // Slack top-level fallback
19231            interruption_scope_id: None,                 // but NOT a thread reply
19232            attachments: vec![],
19233            subject: None,
19234        };
19235        assert_eq!(interruption_scope_key(&msg), "slack_C123_alice");
19236    }
19237
19238    #[tokio::test]
19239    async fn message_dispatch_different_threads_do_not_cancel_each_other() {
19240        let channel_impl = Arc::new(SlackRecordingChannel::default());
19241        let channel: Arc<dyn Channel> = channel_impl.clone();
19242
19243        let mut channels_by_name = HashMap::new();
19244        channels_by_name.insert(channel.name().to_string(), channel);
19245
19246        let runtime_ctx = Arc::new(ChannelRuntimeContext {
19247            channels_by_name: Arc::new(channels_by_name),
19248            model_provider: Arc::new(SlowModelProvider {
19249                delay: Duration::from_millis(150),
19250            }),
19251            model_provider_ref: Arc::new("test-provider".to_string()),
19252            agent_alias: Arc::new("test-agent".to_string()),
19253            agent_cfg: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default()),
19254            memory: Arc::new(NoopMemory),
19255            memory_strategy: Arc::new(
19256                zeroclaw_runtime::agent::memory_strategy::DefaultMemoryStrategy::with_config(
19257                    Arc::new(NoopMemory),
19258                    zeroclaw_config::schema::MemoryConfig::default(),
19259                    std::path::PathBuf::new(),
19260                ),
19261            ),
19262            tools_registry: Arc::new(vec![]),
19263            observer: Arc::new(NoopObserver),
19264            system_prompt: Arc::new("test-system-prompt".to_string()),
19265            model: Arc::new("test-model".to_string()),
19266            temperature: Some(0.0),
19267            auto_save_memory: false,
19268            max_tool_iterations: 10,
19269            min_relevance_score: 0.0,
19270            conversation_histories: Arc::new(Mutex::new(lru::LruCache::new(
19271                std::num::NonZeroUsize::new(MAX_CONVERSATION_SENDERS).unwrap(),
19272            ))),
19273            pending_new_sessions: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashSet::new())),
19274            provider_cache: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
19275            route_overrides: Arc::new(Mutex::new(HashMap::new())),
19276            reliability: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::ReliabilityConfig::default()),
19277            provider_runtime_options: zeroclaw_providers::ModelProviderRuntimeOptions::default(),
19278            workspace_dir: Arc::new(std::env::temp_dir()),
19279            prompt_config: Arc::new(zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default()),
19280            message_timeout_secs: CHANNEL_MESSAGE_TIMEOUT_SECS,
19281            interrupt_on_new_message: InterruptOnNewMessageConfig {
19282                telegram: false,
19283                slack: true,
19284                discord: false,
19285                mattermost: false,
19286                matrix: false,
19287                whatsapp: false,
19288            },
19289            multimodal: zeroclaw_config::schema::MultimodalConfig::default(),
19290            media_pipeline: zeroclaw_config::schema::MediaPipelineConfig::default(),
19291            transcription_config: zeroclaw_config::schema::TranscriptionConfig::default(),
19292            agent_transcription_provider: String::new(),
19293            hooks: None,
19294            non_cli_excluded_tools: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
19295            autonomy_level: AutonomyLevel::default(),
19296            tool_call_dedup_exempt: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
19297            model_routes: Arc::new(Vec::new()),
19298            query_classification: zeroclaw_config::schema::QueryClassificationConfig::default(),
19299            ack_reactions: true,
19300            show_tool_calls: true,
19301            session_store: None,
19302            approval_manager: Arc::new(ApprovalManager::for_non_interactive(
19303                &zeroclaw_config::schema::RiskProfileConfig::default(),
19304            )),
19305            activated_tools: None,
19306            cost_tracking: None,
19307            pacing: zeroclaw_config::schema::PacingConfig::default(),
19308            max_tool_result_chars: 0,
19309            context_token_budget: 0,
19310            debouncer: Arc::new(zeroclaw_infra::debounce::MessageDebouncer::new(
19311                Duration::ZERO,
19312            )),
19313            receipt_generator: None,
19314            show_receipts_in_response: false,
19315            last_applied_config_stamp: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
19316            runtime_defaults_override: Arc::new(Mutex::new(None)),
19317        });
19318
19319        let (tx, rx) = tokio::sync::mpsc::channel::<zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage>(8);
19320        let send_task = zeroclaw_spawn::spawn!(async move {
19321            // Two messages from same sender but in different Slack threads —
19322            // they must NOT cancel each other.
19323            tx.send(zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
19324                id: "1741234567.100001".to_string(),
19325                sender: "alice".to_string(),
19326                reply_target: "C123".to_string(),
19327                content: "thread-a question".to_string(),
19328                channel: "slack".to_string(),
19329                channel_alias: None,
19330                timestamp: 1,
19331                thread_ts: Some("1741234567.100001".to_string()),
19332                interruption_scope_id: Some("1741234567.100001".to_string()),
19333                attachments: vec![],
19334                subject: None,
19335            })
19336            .await
19337            .unwrap();
19338            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(30)).await;
19339            tx.send(zeroclaw_api::channel::ChannelMessage {
19340                id: "1741234567.200002".to_string(),
19341                sender: "alice".to_string(),
19342                reply_target: "C123".to_string(),
19343                content: "thread-b question".to_string(),
19344                channel: "slack".to_string(),
19345                channel_alias: None,
19346                timestamp: 2,
19347                thread_ts: Some("1741234567.200002".to_string()),
19348                interruption_scope_id: Some("1741234567.200002".to_string()),
19349                attachments: vec![],
19350                subject: None,
19351            })
19352            .await
19353            .unwrap();
19354        });
19355
19356        run_message_dispatch_loop(rx, AgentRouter::single(runtime_ctx), 4).await;
19357        send_task.await.unwrap();
19358
19359        // Both tasks should have completed — different threads, no cancellation.
19360        let sent_messages = channel_impl.sent_messages.lock().await;
19361        assert_eq!(
19362            sent_messages.len(),
19363            2,
19364            "both Slack thread messages should complete, got: {sent_messages:?}"
19365        );
19366    }
19367
19368    #[test]
19369    fn sanitize_channel_response_redacts_detected_credentials() {
19370        let tools: Vec<Box<dyn Tool>> = Vec::new();
19371        let leaked = "Temporary key: AKIAABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP"; // gitleaks:allow
19372
19373        let result = sanitize_channel_response(leaked, &tools);
19374
19375        assert!(!result.contains("AKIAABCDEFGHIJKLMNOP")); // gitleaks:allow
19376        assert!(result.contains("[REDACTED"));
19377    }
19378

19383
19384        let result = sanitize_channel_response(clean_text, &tools);
19385
19386        assert_eq!(result, clean_text);
19387    }

19662

19672        let response = "Intro text\n{\"content\":\"{\\\"toolcalls\\\":[{\\\"name\\\":\\\"mock_price\\\",\\\"arguments\\\":{\\\"symbol\\\":\\\"BTC\\\"}}]}\",\"tool_calls\":[{\"name\":\"mock_price\",\"arguments\":{\"symbol\":\"BTC\"}}]}\nDone.";
19673
19674        let result = sanitize_channel_response(response, &tools);
19675
19676        assert!(result.contains("Intro text"));
19677        assert!(result.contains("Done."));
19678        assert!(!result.contains("toolcalls"));
19679        assert!(!result.contains("shell"));
19680    }

19682    #[test]
19683    fn sanitize_channel_response_strips_xml_tool_result_blocks() {
19684        let tools: Vec<Box<dyn Tool>> = Vec::new();
19685        let input = "<tool_result>\n{\"results\":[]}\n</tool_result>\n<tool_result>\n{\"command\":\"ls\",\"exit_code\":0}\n</tool_result>Here is what I found.";
19686
19687        let result = sanitize_channel_response(input, &tools);
19688
19689        assert!(!result.contains("tool_result"));
19690        assert!(!result.contains("exit_code"));
19691        assert!(result.contains("Here is what I found."));
19692    }
19693
19694    #[test]
19695    fn sanitize_channel_response_strips_mixed_tool_result_and_text() {
19696        let tools: Vec<Box<dyn Tool>> = Vec::new();
19697        let input = "Let me check.\n<tool_result name=\"shell\">\noutput here\n</tool_result>\nThe answer is 42.";
19698
19699        let result = sanitize_channel_response(input, &tools);
19700
19701        assert!(!result.contains("<tool_result"));
19702        assert!(!result.contains("output here"));
19703        assert!(result.contains("The answer is 42."));
19704    }

19706    // ── Tests for strip_think_tags_inline (streaming draft sanitization) ──
19707
19708    #[test]
19709    fn strip_think_tags_inline_removes_single_block() {
19710        assert_eq!(
19711            strip_think_tags_inline("<think>reasoning</think>Hello"),
19712            "Hello"
19713        );
19714    }
19715
19716    #[test]
19717    fn strip_think_tags_inline_removes_multiple_blocks() {
19718        assert_eq!(
19719            strip_think_tags_inline("<think>a</think>X<think>b</think>Y"),
19720            "XY"
19721        );
19722    }
19723
19724    #[test]
19725    fn strip_think_tags_inline_handles_unclosed_block() {
19726        assert_eq!(
19727            strip_think_tags_inline("visible<think>hidden tail"),
19728            "visible"
19729        );
19730    }
19731
19732    #[test]
19733    fn strip_think_tags_inline_preserves_text_without_tags() {
19734        assert_eq!(strip_think_tags_inline("plain text"), "plain text");
19735    }
19736
19737    #[test]
19738    fn strip_think_tags_inline_handles_empty_string() {
19739        assert_eq!(strip_think_tags_inline(""), "");
19740    }
19741
19742    #[test]
19743    fn strip_think_tags_inline_strips_surrounding_whitespace() {
19744        assert_eq!(
19745            strip_think_tags_inline("<think>hidden</think>  Answer  "),
19746            "Answer"
19747        );
19748    }
19749
19750    // ── Tests for #4827: tool context preservation ──────────────
19751
19752    #[test]
19753    fn extract_current_turn_tool_messages_returns_intermediate_messages() {
19754        let history = vec![
19755            ChatMessage::system("sys"),
19756            ChatMessage::user("older msg"),
19757            ChatMessage::assistant("older reply"),
19758            ChatMessage::user("block the iPad"),
19759            ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"),
19760            ChatMessage::tool("ok"),
19761            ChatMessage::assistant("Done, iPad is blocked."),
19762        ];
19763
19764        let tool_msgs = extract_current_turn_tool_messages(&history);
19765        assert_eq!(tool_msgs.len(), 2);
19766        assert_eq!(tool_msgs[0].role, "assistant");
19767        assert!(tool_msgs[0].content.contains("tool_call"));
19768        assert_eq!(tool_msgs[1].role, "tool");
19769    }
19770
19771    #[test]
19772    fn extract_current_turn_tool_messages_empty_when_no_tools() {
19773        let history = vec![
19774            ChatMessage::user("hello"),
19775            ChatMessage::assistant("Hi there!"),
19776        ];
19777
19778        let tool_msgs = extract_current_turn_tool_messages(&history);
19779        assert!(tool_msgs.is_empty());
19780    }
19781
19782    #[test]
19783    fn extract_current_turn_tool_messages_multiple_tool_rounds() {
19784        let history = vec![
19785            ChatMessage::user("do two things"),
19786            ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"read_skill\"}"),
19787            ChatMessage::tool("skill content"),
19788            ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"),
19789            ChatMessage::tool("shell output"),
19790            ChatMessage::assistant("All done."),
19791        ];
19792
19793        let tool_msgs = extract_current_turn_tool_messages(&history);
19794        assert_eq!(tool_msgs.len(), 4);
19795    }
19796
19797    #[test]
19798    fn is_tool_call_content_detects_tool_calls() {
19799        assert!(is_tool_call_content("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"));
19800        assert!(is_tool_call_content("<tool_call>shell</tool_call>"));
19801        assert!(is_tool_call_content(
19802            "{\"name\": \"read_file\", \"args\": {}}"
19803        ));
19804        assert!(!is_tool_call_content("The iPad has been blocked."));
19805        assert!(!is_tool_call_content(""));
19806    }
19807
19808    #[test]
19809    fn is_tool_call_content_does_not_misclassify_regular_name_json() {
19810        assert!(!is_tool_call_content(
19811            "{\"name\":\"Alice\",\"role\":\"admin\"}"
19812        ));
19813    }
19814
19815    #[test]
19816    fn strip_old_tool_context_preserves_recent_tool_context_when_history_within_keep_window() {
19817        let ctx = router_test_ctx();
19818        let sender = "tool-window-short";
19819        seed_sender_history(
19820            ctx.as_ref(),
19821            sender,
19822            vec![
19823                ChatMessage::user("block the iPad"),
19824                ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"),
19825                ChatMessage::tool(r#"{"tool_call_id":"call_1","content":"ok"}"#),
19826                ChatMessage::assistant("Done, iPad is blocked."),
19827            ],
19828        );
19829
19830        strip_old_tool_context(ctx.as_ref(), sender, 2);
19831
19832        let turns = cloned_sender_history(ctx.as_ref(), sender);
19833        assert_eq!(
19834            turns.len(),
19835            4,
19836            "tool context in protected turns must not be stripped"
19837        );
19838        assert_eq!(turns[1].role, "assistant");
19839        assert!(turns[1].content.contains("tool_call"));
19840        assert_eq!(turns[2].role, "tool");
19841    }
19842
19843    #[test]
19844    fn strip_old_tool_context_strips_tool_context_before_keep_window_boundary() {
19845        let ctx = router_test_ctx();
19846        let sender = "tool-window-boundary";
19847        seed_sender_history(
19848            ctx.as_ref(),
19849            sender,
19850            vec![
19851                ChatMessage::user("first task"),
19852                ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"),
19853                ChatMessage::tool("ok"),
19854                ChatMessage::assistant("first task done"),
19855                ChatMessage::user("second task"),
19856                ChatMessage::assistant("second task done"),
19857                ChatMessage::user("third task"),
19858                ChatMessage::assistant("third task done"),
19859            ],
19860        );
19861
19862        strip_old_tool_context(ctx.as_ref(), sender, 2);
19863
19864        let turns = cloned_sender_history(ctx.as_ref(), sender);
19865        assert_eq!(
19866            history_signature(&turns),
19867            vec![
19868                ("user".to_string(), "first task".to_string()),
19869                ("assistant".to_string(), "first task done".to_string()),
19870                ("user".to_string(), "second task".to_string()),
19871                ("assistant".to_string(), "second task done".to_string()),
19872                ("user".to_string(), "third task".to_string()),
19873                ("assistant".to_string(), "third task done".to_string()),
19874            ],
19875            "tool context older than the protected keep window should be stripped"
19876        );
19877    }
19878
19879    #[test]
19880    fn strip_old_tool_context_removes_native_tool_call_assistant_messages() {
19881        let ctx = router_test_ctx();
19882        let sender = "tool-window-native";
19883        seed_sender_history(
19884            ctx.as_ref(),
19885            sender,
19886            vec![
19887                ChatMessage::user("first task"),
19888                ChatMessage::assistant(
19889                    r#"{"content":"Need to call tool","tool_calls":[{"id":"call_1","name":"shell","arguments":"{\"command\":\"pwd\"}"}]}"#,
19890                ),
19891                ChatMessage::tool(r#"{"tool_call_id":"call_1","content":"ok"}"#),
19892                ChatMessage::assistant("first task done"),
19893                ChatMessage::user("second task"),
19894                ChatMessage::assistant("second task done"),
19895                ChatMessage::user("third task"),
19896                ChatMessage::assistant("third task done"),
19897            ],
19898        );
19899
19900        strip_old_tool_context(ctx.as_ref(), sender, 1);
19901
19902        let turns = cloned_sender_history(ctx.as_ref(), sender);
19903        assert_eq!(
19904            history_signature(&turns),
19905            vec![
19906                ("user".to_string(), "first task".to_string()),
19907                ("assistant".to_string(), "first task done".to_string()),
19908                ("user".to_string(), "second task".to_string()),
19909                ("assistant".to_string(), "second task done".to_string()),
19910                ("user".to_string(), "third task".to_string()),
19911                ("assistant".to_string(), "third task done".to_string()),
19912            ],
19913            "native assistant tool-call JSON should be stripped together with old tool results"
19914        );
19915    }
19916
19917    #[test]
19918    fn normalize_cached_channel_turns_passes_through_tool_messages() {
19919        let turns = vec![
19920            ChatMessage::user("block the iPad"),
19921            ChatMessage::assistant("{\"tool_call\": \"shell\"}"),
19922            ChatMessage::tool("ok"),
19923            ChatMessage::assistant("iPad blocked."),
19924            ChatMessage::user("next question"),
19925        ];
19926
19927        let normalized = normalize_cached_channel_turns(turns);
19928        // user, assistant(tool_call), tool, assistant(final), user
19929        assert_eq!(normalized.len(), 5);
19930        assert_eq!(normalized[2].role, "tool");
19931    }
19932
19933    #[test]
19934    fn default_keep_tool_context_turns_is_two() {
19935        let config = zeroclaw_config::schema::AliasedAgentConfig::default();
19936        assert_eq!(config.resolved.keep_tool_context_turns, 2);
19937    }
19938
19939    #[test]
19940    fn build_channel_system_prompt_includes_sender_id() {
19941        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt(
19942            "You are a helpful assistant.",
19943            "mattermost",
19944            "channel123:root456",
19945            "user_abc123",
19946            "msg-xyz789",
19947            None,
19948        );
19949        // Pin the comma-separated tuple in the format string so a refactor
19950        // that splits, reorders, or rewords the context block fails loudly
19951        // rather than silently.
19952        assert!(
19953            prompt.contains(
19954                "channel=mattermost, reply_target=channel123:root456, \
19955                 sender=user_abc123, message_id=msg-xyz789"
19956            ),
19957            "prompt missing the joint channel-context tuple: {prompt}"
19958        );
19959    }
19960
19961    #[test]
19962    fn build_channel_system_prompt_omits_context_when_reply_target_empty() {
19963        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt(
19964            "Base prompt.",
19965            "mattermost",
19966            "",
19967            "user_abc123",
19968            "msg-xyz789",
19969            None,
19970        );
19971        assert!(!prompt.contains("sender="));
19972        assert!(!prompt.contains("Channel context:"));
19973    }
19974
19975    #[test]
19976    fn build_channel_system_prompt_sender_distinguishes_users() {
19977        let prompt_a = build_channel_system_prompt(
19978            "Base.",
19979            "mattermost",
19980            "ch:thread",
19981            "user_aaa",
19982            "msg-1",
19983            None,
19984        );
19985        let prompt_b = build_channel_system_prompt(
19986            "Base.",
19987            "mattermost",
19988            "ch:thread",
19989            "user_bbb",
19990            "msg-1",
19991            None,
19992        );
19993        assert!(prompt_a.contains("sender=user_aaa"));
19994        assert!(prompt_b.contains("sender=user_bbb"));
19995        assert_ne!(prompt_a, prompt_b);
19996    }
19997
19998    #[test]
19999    fn build_channel_system_prompt_refreshes_legacy_datetime_section_to_date_only() {
20000        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt(
20001            "Base.\n\n## Current Date\n\nProject note, not generated date context.\n\n## Current Date & Time\n\n2026-01-01 01:02:03 (UTC)\n\n## Runtime\n\nHost: old\n",
20002            "mattermost",
20003            "ch:thread",
20004            "user_aaa",
20005            "msg-1",
20006            None,
20007        );
20008
20009        assert!(prompt.contains("## Current Date\n\n"));
20010        assert!(prompt.contains("Project note, not generated date context."));
20011        assert!(!prompt.contains("## Current Date & Time"));
20012        assert!(!prompt.contains("01:02:03"));
20013        let generated_section = prompt
20014            .split("## Runtime")
20015            .next()
20016            .expect("prompt should contain runtime section before generated date assertion");
20017        let date_line = generated_section
20018            .rsplit("## Current Date\n\n")
20019            .next()
20020            .and_then(|rest| rest.lines().next())
20021            .expect("current date section should have a date line");
20022        assert_eq!(
20023            &date_line[..10],
20024            &chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string()
20025        );
20026        assert!(
20027            date_line[10..].starts_with(" ("),
20028            "date line should contain only date plus UTC offset: {date_line}"
20029        );
20030    }
20031
20032    #[test]
20033    fn build_channel_system_prompt_refreshes_current_date_section() {
20034        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt(
20035            "Base.\n\n## Current Date\n\n2026-01-01 (+00:00)\n\n## Runtime\n\nHost: old\n",
20036            "mattermost",
20037            "ch:thread",
20038            "user_aaa",
20039            "msg-1",
20040            None,
20041        );
20042
20043        assert!(prompt.contains("## Current Date\n\n"));
20044        assert!(!prompt.contains("2026-01-01 (+00:00)"));
20045        let date_line = prompt
20046            .split("## Current Date\n\n")
20047            .nth(1)
20048            .and_then(|rest| rest.lines().next())
20049            .expect("current date section should have a date line");
20050        assert_eq!(
20051            &date_line[..10],
20052            &chrono::Local::now().format("%Y-%m-%d").to_string()
20053        );
20054    }
20055
20056    #[test]
20057    fn build_channel_system_prompt_for_message_propagates_channel_fields() {
20058        // The wrapper unpacks ChannelMessage into build_channel_system_prompt
20059        // args. Pin the rendered prompt against every msg.* field the LLM
20060        // is expected to see so a future refactor adding more fields can't
20061        // silently drop existing ones.
20062        let msg = channel_message("discord", None);
20063        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt_for_message("Base.", &msg, None);
20064        assert!(
20065            prompt.contains("channel=discord, reply_target=r1, sender=u1, message_id=m1"),
20066            "wrapper did not propagate channel/reply_target/sender/message_id \
20067             from ChannelMessage: {prompt}"
20068        );
20069    }
20070
20071    #[test]
20072    fn build_channel_system_prompt_webhook_cron_hint_carries_thread_id() {
20073        // On the webhook channel `reply_target` is the inbound thread/conversation
20074        // id, not a recipient. Using it as `delivery.to` would strip the thread
20075        // context from the cron-announce callback (see #6634). The hint must
20076        // place the sender in `to` and the reply_target in `thread_id`.
20077        let prompt = build_channel_system_prompt(
20078            "Base.",
20079            "webhook",
20080            "agent-chat:agent-1:thread-7",
20081            "user:abc",
20082            "msg-1",
20083            None,
20084        );
20085        assert!(
20086            prompt.contains("\"to\":\"user:abc\""),
20087            "webhook cron hint must use sender as `to`: {prompt}"
20088        );
20089        assert!(
20090            prompt.contains("\"thread_id\":\"agent-chat:agent-1:thread-7\""),
20091            "webhook cron hint must carry the reply_target as `thread_id`: {prompt}"
20092        );
20093        assert!(
20094            !prompt.contains("\"to\":\"agent-chat:agent-1:thread-7\""),
20095            "webhook cron hint must not put the thread id in `to`: {prompt}"
20096        );
20097    }
20098
20099    #[test]
20100    fn build_channel_system_prompt_non_webhook_cron_hint_keeps_to_as_reply_target() {
20101        let prompt =
20102            build_channel_system_prompt("Base.", "slack", "C12345", "U67890", "msg-1", None);
20103        assert!(
20104            prompt.contains("\"to\":\"C12345\""),
20105            "non-webhook cron hint should keep reply_target as `to`: {prompt}"
20106        );
20107        assert!(
20108            !prompt.contains("\"thread_id\""),
20109            "non-webhook cron hint should not emit a thread_id field: {prompt}"
20110        );
20111    }
20112
20113    #[tokio::test]
20114    #[cfg(feature = "channel-lark")]
20115    async fn deliver_announcement_routes_lark_to_lark_arm() {
20116        // Both names must enter the merged lark|feishu arm. Falling through
20117        // to `unsupported delivery channel` would mean the schema enum and
20118        // the match arm have drifted apart.
20119        let config = zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default();
20120
20121        for channel in ["lark.default", "feishu.default"] {
20122            let err = deliver_announcement(&config, channel, "oc_test_chat", None, "hi")
20123                .await
20124                .err()
20125                .unwrap_or_else(|| {
20126                    panic!("expected {channel} to bail because channel is not configured")
20127                });
20128            let msg = format!("{err:#}");
20129            assert!(
20130                !msg.contains("unsupported delivery channel"),
20131                "{channel} must route to lark|feishu arm, not fall through; got: {msg}"
20132            );
20133            assert!(
20134                msg.contains("[channels.lark.default] not configured"),
20135                "{channel} must report the real config table [channels.lark.default]; got: {msg}"
20136            );
20137        }
20138    }
20139
20140    #[tokio::test]
20141    #[cfg(feature = "channel-lark")]
20142    async fn deliver_announcement_rejects_feishu_value_when_use_feishu_false() {
20143        // Reject (not warn): otherwise the message silently lands on the
20144        // Lark endpoint despite the user explicitly naming Feishu.
20145        let mut config = zeroclaw_config::schema::Config::default();
20146        config.channels.lark.insert(
20147            "work".to_string(),
20148            zeroclaw_config::schema::LarkConfig {
20149                enabled: true,
20150                use_feishu: false,
20151                app_id: "cli_test".to_string(),
20152                app_secret: "secret".to_string(),
20153                approval_timeout_secs: 300,
20154                per_user_session: false,
20155                ..Default::default()
20156            },
20157        );
20158
20159        let err = deliver_announcement(&config, "feishu.work", "oc_test_chat", None, "hi")
20160            .await
20161            .expect_err("expected bail when channel=feishu but use_feishu=false");
20162        let msg = format!("{err:#}");
20163        assert!(
20164            msg.contains("use_feishu=false"),
20165            "bail must explain the use_feishu mismatch; got: {msg}"
20166        );
20167        assert!(
20168            msg.contains("[channels.lark.work]"),
20169            "bail must point at the real config table; got: {msg}"
20170        );
20171    }
20172
20173    fn email_msg(id: &str, subject: Option<&str>) -> ChannelMessage {
20174        ChannelMessage {
20175            subject: subject.map(Into::into),
20176            ..ChannelMessage::new(
20177                id,
20178                "user@example.com",
20179                "user@example.com",
20180                "Hello",
20181                "email",
20182                0,
20183            )
20184        }
20185    }
20186
20187    #[test]
20188    fn reply_to_sets_in_reply_to_and_re_subject() {
20189        let msg = email_msg("<abc123@mail.example>", Some("Weekly report"));
20190        let sm = SendMessage::reply_to(&msg, "Here is the answer");
20191        assert_eq!(sm.in_reply_to.as_deref(), Some("<abc123@mail.example>"));
20192        assert_eq!(sm.subject.as_deref(), Some("Re: Weekly report"));
20193    }
20194
20195    #[test]
20196    fn reply_to_does_not_double_re_prefix() {
20197        let msg = email_msg("<abc123@mail.example>", Some("Re: Weekly report"));
20198        let sm = SendMessage::reply_to(&msg, "Here is the answer");
20199        assert_eq!(sm.subject.as_deref(), Some("Re: Weekly report"));
20200    }
20201
20202    #[test]
20203    fn reply_to_no_subject_still_sets_in_reply_to() {
20204        let msg = email_msg("<abc123@mail.example>", None);
20205        let sm = SendMessage::reply_to(&msg, "Here is the answer");
20206        assert_eq!(sm.in_reply_to.as_deref(), Some("<abc123@mail.example>"));
20207        assert!(sm.subject.is_none());
20208    }
20209}
20210
20211#[cfg(test)]
20212mod omitted_feature_tests {
20213    /// When `channel-telegram` is not compiled, a configured Telegram entry must
20214    /// produce no channel in `collect_configured_channels`. This pins the behaviour
20215    /// that selective builds never silently include a channel whose feature was
20216    /// omitted, and ensures the `#[cfg(not(feature = "channel-telegram"))]` warn
20217    /// path compiles correctly.
20218    #[cfg(not(feature = "channel-telegram"))]
20219    #[test]
20220    fn collect_configured_channels_omits_telegram_when_compiled_out() {
20221        use super::*;
20222        let mut config = Config::default();
20223        config.channels.telegram.insert(
20224            "default".to_string(),
20225            zeroclaw_config::schema::TelegramConfig {
20226                enabled: true,
20227                ..Default::default()
20228            },
20229        );
20230        let config_arc = Arc::new(RwLock::new(config));
20231        let channels = collect_configured_channels(&config_arc, "test", &[]);
20232        assert!(
20233            channels.iter().all(|c| c.display_name != "Telegram"),
20234            "Telegram must be absent from collect_configured_channels when \
20235             channel-telegram feature is not compiled in"
20236        );
20237    }
20238}
